packs.name: Apache HTTPD operations
packs.description: Apache version, modules, mod_status snapshot, config syntax check, vhost dump, error/access log tails, plus narrow mutators (graceful reload, graceful stop). Full restart not included — use systemd for that.

actions.title: Top client IPs from access log
actions.summary: Show a tally of source IPs from the last N access log lines.
actions.description: Show a tally of source IPs from the last N access log lines.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many access-log lines to read.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} "${HTTPD_ACCESS_LOG:-/var/log/apache2/access.log}" | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -50

actions.title: Top URLs from access log
actions.summary: Show a tally of request URLs from the last N access log lines.
actions.description: Show a tally of request URLs from the last N access log lines.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many access-log lines to read.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} "${HTTPD_ACCESS_LOG:-/var/log/apache2/access.log}" | awk '{print $7}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -50

actions.title: tail error_log
actions.summary: Tail the last N lines from the Apache error log.
actions.description: Tail the last N lines from the Apache error log.

actions.title: apachectl graceful
actions.summary: Re-reads config and gracefully reloads child workers — no dropped requests.
actions.description: Re-reads config and gracefully reloads child workers — no dropped requests.
side_effects: Workers finish in-flight requests, then respawn with the new config.
If new config is invalid, master logs and keeps running old config.

actions.title: apachectl graceful-stop
actions.summary: Drains in-flight requests then shuts down. Apache will NOT come back automatically; use systemd to restart.
actions.description: Drains in-flight requests then shuts down. Apache will NOT come back automatically; use systemd to restart.
side_effects: In-flight requests are allowed to complete.
Workers exit; supervisor must restart to bring back online.

actions.title: apachectl -M
actions.summary: List all loaded modules (static + shared).
actions.description: List all loaded modules (static + shared).

actions.title: mod_status snapshot
actions.summary: Show a snapshot of mod_status (requires mod_status enabled). Set HTTPD_STATUS_URL env var.
actions.description: Show a snapshot of mod_status (requires mod_status enabled). Set HTTPD_STATUS_URL env var.

actions.title: apachectl -t
actions.summary: Check config syntax. Run before graceful_reload.
actions.description: Check config syntax. Run before graceful_reload.

actions.title: apachectl -V
actions.summary: Show Apache version + compiled-in directives + MPM.
actions.description: Show Apache version + compiled-in directives + MPM.

actions.title: apachectl -S
actions.summary: List all configured vhosts with their effective listen + ServerName.
actions.description: List all configured vhosts with their effective listen + ServerName.

packs.name: AWS CloudWatch operations
packs.description: Metric + alarm + log group + log stream + log query introspection. Read-only. Auth via AWS_PROFILE.

actions.title: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --state-value ALARM
actions.summary: List only currently-firing alarms.
actions.description: List only currently-firing alarms.

actions.title: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms
actions.summary: List all CloudWatch alarms with state + threshold.
actions.description: List all CloudWatch alarms with state + threshold.

actions.title: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics (last 1h)
actions.summary: Get 1-hour Average + Max for one metric. Use for quick health snapshots.
actions.description: Get 1-hour Average + Max for one metric. Use for quick health snapshots.

argv: END=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ); START=$(date -u -d '1 hour ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-1H +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ); aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace '{{ args.namespace }}' --metric-name '{{ args.metric }}' --start-time $START --end-time $END --period 300 --statistics Average Maximum --output json

actions.title: aws cloudwatch list-metrics --namespace
actions.summary: List all metrics in one namespace.
actions.description: List all metrics in one namespace.

actions.title: aws logs describe-log-groups
actions.summary: List all CloudWatch log groups.
actions.description: List all CloudWatch log groups.

actions.title: aws logs describe-log-streams
actions.summary: List streams for one log group, sorted by last event time.
actions.description: List streams for one log group, sorted by last event time.

actions.title: aws logs tail
actions.summary: Tails recent log events for one log group (no follow).
actions.description: Tails recent log events for one log group (no follow).

packs.name: AWS Cost Explorer operations
packs.description: Read-only AWS spend introspection — month-to-date by service, by account, forecast. Auth via AWS_PROFILE with ce:* permissions. Cost Explorer must be enabled.

actions.title: Forecast current-month spend
actions.summary: Show AWS-projected total spend for the current month based on usage so far.
actions.description: Show AWS-projected total spend for the current month based on usage so far.

argv: START=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d); END=$(date -u -d 'first day of next month' +%Y-%m-%d 2>/dev/null || date -u -v+1m -v1d +%Y-%m-%d); aws ce get-cost-forecast --time-period Start=$START,End=$END --metric UNBLENDED_COST --granularity MONTHLY --output json

actions.title: Last-7-day daily totals
actions.summary: Show daily unblended cost for the last 7 days. Use to spot spend spikes.
actions.description: Show daily unblended cost for the last 7 days. Use to spot spend spikes.

argv: END=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d); START=$(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%d 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-7d +%Y-%m-%d); aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=$START,End=$END --granularity DAILY --metrics UnblendedCost --output json

actions.title: Month-to-date cost by linked account
actions.summary: Show MTD cost grouped by linked account ID (org master account view).
actions.description: Show MTD cost grouped by linked account ID (org master account view).

argv: START=$(date -u +%Y-%m-01); END=$(date -u -d tomorrow +%Y-%m-%d); aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=$START,End=$END --granularity MONTHLY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=DIMENSION,Key=LINKED_ACCOUNT --output json

actions.title: Month-to-date cost by service
actions.summary: Show unblended cost from the 1st of this month until today, grouped by service.
actions.description: Show unblended cost from the 1st of this month until today, grouped by service.

argv: START=$(date -u +%Y-%m-01); END=$(date -u -d tomorrow +%Y-%m-%d); aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=$START,End=$END --granularity MONTHLY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=DIMENSION,Key=SERVICE --output json

actions.title: Savings Plans coverage (last month)
actions.summary: Show what fraction of eligible compute spend was covered by Savings Plans / RI last month.
actions.description: Show what fraction of eligible compute spend was covered by Savings Plans / RI last month.

argv: END=$(date -u +%Y-%m-01); START=$(date -u -d 'last month 1st' +%Y-%m-01 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-1m -v1d +%Y-%m-01); aws ce get-savings-plans-coverage --time-period Start=$START,End=$END --granularity MONTHLY --output json

packs.name: AWS EC2 operations
packs.description: EC2 instance inventory + state introspection plus narrow mutators (stop, start, reboot, terminate). Auth via AWS_PROFILE + AWS_REGION on the runner host.

actions.title: aws ec2 get-console-output
actions.summary: Get serial console output for one instance. Useful for boot diagnostics.
actions.description: Get serial console output for one instance. Useful for boot diagnostics.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-instances --instance-ids
actions.summary: Get details for one EC2 instance.
actions.description: Get details for one EC2 instance.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-instances
actions.summary: List all EC2 instances in the configured region with state + tags + IPs.
actions.description: List all EC2 instances in the configured region with state + tags + IPs.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-security-groups
actions.summary: List all SGs with ingress/egress rules.
actions.description: List all SGs with ingress/egress rules.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-subnets
actions.summary: List all subnets in this region.
actions.description: List all subnets in this region.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-volumes
actions.summary: List all EBS volumes with size + state + attachment.
actions.description: List all EBS volumes with size + state + attachment.

actions.title: aws ec2 describe-instance-status
actions.summary: Show per-instance system + instance status checks.
actions.description: Show per-instance system + instance status checks.

actions.title: aws ec2 reboot-instances
actions.summary: Reboots one instance. Instance keeps its IPs + ephemeral storage.
actions.description: Reboots one instance. Instance keeps its IPs + ephemeral storage.
side_effects: Instance reboots; ephemeral storage and IPs persist.
Brief downtime — services on host go down with it.

actions.title: aws ec2 start-instances
actions.summary: Starts one stopped instance.
actions.description: Starts one stopped instance.
side_effects: New public IPv4 unless Elastic IP attached.

actions.title: aws ec2 stop-instances
actions.summary: Stops one instance. Public IP is released (use Elastic IP to retain).
actions.description: Stops one instance. Public IP is released (use Elastic IP to retain).
side_effects: Instance powers off; not billed for compute hours while stopped.
Public IPv4 address is lost unless Elastic IP attached.

actions.title: aws ec2 terminate-instances
actions.summary: Permanently destroys one instance + its ephemeral storage. Cannot be undone.
actions.description: Permanently destroys one instance + its ephemeral storage. Cannot be undone.
side_effects: All non-EBS storage (instance store) is lost.
EBS volumes with DeleteOnTermination=true are deleted too.

packs.name: AWS IAM operations
packs.description: IAM introspection — users, roles, policies, attached policies, access keys — plus incident-response mutators: deactivate access key, delete access key, detach user policy. Auth via AWS_PROFILE. Routine IAM changes belong in IaC; these actions are for emergency lockout.

actions.title: aws iam update-access-key --status Inactive
actions.summary: Disable an IAM access key. ALL API calls using this key fail immediately. Use during an incident when a key is suspected compromised — it stops blast radius before rotation completes. Reversible by setting status back to Active.
actions.description: Disable an IAM access key. ALL API calls using this key fail immediately. Use during an incident when a key is suspected compromised — it stops blast radius before rotation completes. Reversible by setting status back to Active.
side_effects: All API calls with this key fail immediately.
Services using the key see auth errors until rotated.

actions.title: aws iam delete-access-key
actions.summary: Permanently delete an IAM access key. Irreversible. Use after rotation when the old key is no longer needed. Prefer deactivate_access_key for incident response (it's reversible).
actions.description: Permanently delete an IAM access key. Irreversible. Use after rotation when the old key is no longer needed. Prefer deactivate_access_key for incident response (it's reversible).
side_effects: Any service still using it gets a hard auth error.

actions.title: aws iam detach-user-policy
actions.summary: Detach one managed policy from one user. Use to remove overly broad permissions during an incident. The policy itself is not modified; just the attachment.
actions.description: Detach one managed policy from one user. Use to remove overly broad permissions during an incident. The policy itself is not modified; just the attachment.
side_effects: User loses the permissions that policy granted.
In-flight requests using those permissions begin failing.

actions.title: aws iam get-role
actions.summary: Get one IAM role's details + trust policy.
actions.description: Get one IAM role's details + trust policy.

actions.title: aws iam get-user
actions.summary: Get one IAM user's details.
actions.description: Get one IAM user's details.

actions.title: aws iam get-access-key-last-used
actions.summary: Show when one access key was last used (use to spot dormant keys).
actions.description: Show when one access key was last used (use to spot dormant keys).

actions.title: aws iam list-access-keys
actions.summary: List access key IDs for one user (NOT the secret).
actions.description: List access key IDs for one user (NOT the secret).

actions.title: aws iam list-attached-user-policies
actions.summary: List policies attached to one IAM user.
actions.description: List policies attached to one IAM user.

actions.title: aws iam list-policies --scope Local
actions.summary: List all customer-managed IAM policies.
actions.description: List all customer-managed IAM policies.

actions.title: aws iam list-roles
actions.summary: List all IAM roles in the account.
actions.description: List all IAM roles in the account.

actions.title: aws iam list-users
actions.summary: List all IAM users in the account.
actions.description: List all IAM users in the account.

packs.name: AWS RDS operations
packs.description: RDS instance + cluster inventory, parameter groups, snapshots, plus narrow operator actions (reboot, create-snapshot). Auth via AWS_PROFILE. Does NOT include delete-instance/delete-cluster.

actions.title: aws rds create-db-snapshot
actions.summary: Triggers a manual snapshot of one instance. Stored in your account; outlives instance deletion.
actions.description: Triggers a manual snapshot of one instance. Stored in your account; outlives instance deletion.
side_effects: Brief IO impact during snapshot start.
Storage charges apply until you delete the snapshot.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-clusters --db-cluster-identifier
actions.summary: Show one Aurora cluster's full details + members.
actions.description: Show one Aurora cluster's full details + members.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-instances --db-instance-identifier
actions.summary: Show full details for one RDS instance.
actions.description: Show full details for one RDS instance.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-clusters
actions.summary: List all Aurora clusters.
actions.description: List all Aurora clusters.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-instances
actions.summary: List all RDS instances with class, engine, status.
actions.description: List all RDS instances with class, engine, status.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-snapshots
actions.summary: List all DB snapshots.
actions.description: List all DB snapshots.

actions.title: aws rds describe-db-parameter-groups
actions.summary: List all DB parameter groups.
actions.description: List all DB parameter groups.

actions.title: aws rds reboot-db-instance
actions.summary: Reboots one RDS instance. Brief downtime.
actions.description: Reboots one RDS instance. Brief downtime.
side_effects: Multi-AZ reboot can failover if --force-failover used.

packs.name: AWS S3 operations
packs.description: Read-only S3 introspection — bucket inventory, per-bucket versioning/policy/encryption/lifecycle, object listing + metadata. Excludes every mutator (no put-bucket-*, no delete-*). Object-level changes belong in IaC. Auth via AWS_PROFILE.

actions.title: aws s3api get-bucket-encryption
actions.summary: Show default-encryption settings for one bucket.
actions.description: Show default-encryption settings for one bucket.

actions.title: aws s3api get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration
actions.summary: Show lifecycle rules (auto-expiry, glacier transition).
actions.description: Show lifecycle rules (auto-expiry, glacier transition).

actions.title: aws s3api get-bucket-location
actions.summary: Show the region for one bucket.
actions.description: Show the region for one bucket.

actions.title: aws s3api get-bucket-policy
actions.summary: Show bucket policy JSON.
actions.description: Show bucket policy JSON.

actions.title: aws s3api get-bucket-versioning
actions.summary: Show versioning state of one bucket.
actions.description: Show versioning state of one bucket.

actions.title: aws s3 ls
actions.summary: List all buckets the AWS_PROFILE has access to.
actions.description: List all buckets the AWS_PROFILE has access to.

actions.title: aws s3 ls s3://<bucket>/<prefix>
actions.summary: List objects under a prefix (capped at 1000 per page).
actions.description: List objects under a prefix (capped at 1000 per page).

actions.title: aws s3api head-object
actions.summary: Show metadata for one object (size, content-type, storage class, etag).
actions.description: Show metadata for one object (size, content-type, storage class, etag).

packs.name: BIND DNS server
packs.description: Authoritative/recursive BIND ops: rndc status + stats, zone validation, cache inspection, local resolution probes, plus narrow operator actions (rndc reload, zone freeze/thaw). Requires rndc key on the runner host.

actions.title: named-checkconf
actions.summary: Validates named.conf syntax. Run before any rndc reload.
actions.description: Validates named.conf syntax. Run before any rndc reload.

actions.title: named-checkzone <zone> <file>
actions.summary: Validates one zone file.
actions.description: Validates one zone file.

actions.title: dig @localhost <name> <type>
actions.summary: Resolve one name against the local named.
actions.description: Resolve one name against the local named.

actions.title: rndc flush
actions.summary: Flush the resolver cache. Use after a downstream domain's records change and BIND is still serving stale answers. All clients hit upstream until cache repopulates.
actions.description: Flush the resolver cache. Use after a downstream domain's records change and BIND is still serving stale answers. All clients hit upstream until cache repopulates.
side_effects: Brief upstream-query spike as cache repopulates.

actions.title: rndc freeze <zone>
actions.summary: Freeze a zone (suspend dynamic updates) — used before editing zone file by hand.
actions.description: Freeze a zone (suspend dynamic updates) — used before editing zone file by hand.
side_effects: Dynamic-update writes paused for the zone.

actions.title: rndc reload [zone]
actions.summary: Reloads named config (or one zone). Validates first if zone specified.
actions.description: Reloads named config (or one zone). Validates first if zone specified.
side_effects: Brief lock on the zone(s) being reloaded.
Invalid config aborts the reload; old data stays in place.

args.name: zone
args.description: Specific zone (empty = full config + all zones).

argv: if [ -z "{{ args.zone }}" ]; then rndc reload; else rndc reload "{{ args.zone }}"; fi

actions.title: rndc stats + read dump
actions.summary: Forces a stats dump and reads it. Includes query counts, cache stats, NSID.
actions.description: Forces a stats dump and reads it. Includes query counts, cache stats, NSID.
side_effects: Read-only-ish — file rewrite, no service impact.

actions.title: rndc status
actions.summary: Show named version, uptime, zone count, configured vs current settings.
actions.description: Show named version, uptime, zone count, configured vs current settings.

actions.title: rndc thaw <zone>
actions.summary: Unfreeze a zone — resumes dynamic updates + reloads it.
actions.description: Unfreeze a zone — resumes dynamic updates + reloads it.

actions.title: dig @localhost <zone> AXFR
actions.summary: Dump zone transfer from localhost (requires allow-transfer to include 127.0.0.1).
actions.description: Dump zone transfer from localhost (requires allow-transfer to include 127.0.0.1).

actions.title: SOA serial of <zone>
actions.summary: Show SOA serial from localhost — use to confirm a zone reloaded.
actions.description: Show SOA serial from localhost — use to confirm a zone reloaded.

packs.name: Linux network bonding
packs.description: Inspect Linux network bonding / LACP: list bond interfaces, read a bond's full status from /proc/net/bonding (mode, LACP actor/partner state, per-slave link status), and show link-layer details. Read-only.

actions.title: ip -d link show <bond>
actions.summary: Show link-layer + bond detail for one interface (state, MTU, mode, xmit hash, LACP rate).
actions.description: Show link-layer + bond detail for one interface (state, MTU, mode, xmit hash, LACP rate).

actions.title: ip -d link show type bond
actions.summary: List bond interfaces with their link-layer and bond-mode details.
actions.description: List bond interfaces with their link-layer and bond-mode details.

actions.title: cat /proc/net/bonding/<bond>
actions.summary: Show full bond status from /proc/net/bonding/<bond> — bonding mode, MII status, and for 802.3ad (LACP) the actor/partner state plus each slave's link state and aggregator id.
actions.description: Show full bond status from /proc/net/bonding/<bond> — bonding mode, MII status, and for 802.3ad (LACP) the actor/partner state plus each slave's link state and aggregator id.

packs.name: Caddy web server
packs.description: Caddy v2 ops via its admin API (default http://127.0.0.1:2019) and CLI. Config dump, upstream health, PKI inventory, config validation, plus reload (live config swap). Set CADDY_ADMIN env var if not on default.

actions.title: tail caddy access log
actions.summary: Tail the access log (path configurable via CADDY_ACCESS_LOG env).
actions.description: Tail the access log (path configurable via CADDY_ACCESS_LOG env).

actions.title: caddy adapt --config <file>
actions.summary: Converts a Caddyfile to JSON config without loading it.
actions.description: Converts a Caddyfile to JSON config without loading it.

actions.title: GET /config (current config)
actions.summary: Dump the currently-loaded Caddy configuration as JSON. This surfaces the full config, which can carry secrets (TLS private-key material, basic-auth hashes, upstream credentials, API tokens). The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
actions.description: Dump the currently-loaded Caddy configuration as JSON. This surfaces the full config, which can carry secrets (TLS private-key material, basic-auth hashes, upstream credentials, API tokens). The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
side_effects: Read-only, but exposes the full Caddy config (may include secrets).

actions.title: GET /pki/ca/<id>/certificates
actions.summary: List certificates managed by Caddy's internal PKI.
actions.description: List certificates managed by Caddy's internal PKI.

actions.title: caddy reload --config <file>
actions.summary: Live-swap the running config. No connection drops on success; rejected config keeps the old one running.
actions.description: Live-swap the running config. No connection drops on success; rejected config keeps the old one running.
side_effects: Existing connections keep running on their handlers.

actions.title: GET /reverse_proxy/upstreams
actions.summary: List all reverse-proxy upstreams with current health.
actions.description: List all reverse-proxy upstreams with current health.

actions.title: GET /metrics (Prometheus exposition)
actions.summary: Show Caddy's Prometheus-format metrics endpoint.
actions.description: Show Caddy's Prometheus-format metrics endpoint.

actions.title: caddy stop
actions.summary: Stop the running Caddy process via its admin API. All listeners close; in-flight requests drain. Recovery requires starting Caddy again via systemd or `caddy start`. Use as a last resort during incidents.
actions.description: Stop the running Caddy process via its admin API. All listeners close; in-flight requests drain. Recovery requires starting Caddy again via systemd or `caddy start`. Use as a last resort during incidents.

actions.title: caddy validate --config <file>
actions.summary: Validates a Caddyfile or JSON config without loading it.
actions.description: Validates a Caddyfile or JSON config without loading it.

actions.title: caddy version
actions.summary: Show Caddy binary version + build info.
actions.description: Show Caddy binary version + build info.

packs.name: Cassandra operations
packs.description: Deep Cassandra ops via nodetool + cqlsh: topology (status, ring, gossip, describering, failure detector), health (tpstats, netstats, proxy + table histograms, compaction stats + history, throughput), schema introspection, maintenance mutators (snapshot, flush, cleanup, verify, compact, stop, throughput, cache invalidation), repair workflows, node lifecycle (drain, decommission, assassinate, removenode, rebuild), and logging level control. JMX on 127.0.0.1:7199.

actions.title: Analyze Cassandra disk pressure
actions.summary: Runs a packaged shell script that inspects filesystem usage of the Cassandra data and commitlog directories. Read-only. Use as a first step when disk pressure is suspected. Output is human-readable; do not parse it.
actions.description: Runs a packaged shell script that inspects filesystem usage of the Cassandra data and commitlog directories. Read-only. Use as a first step when disk pressure is suspected. Output is human-readable; do not parse it.
side_effects: Does not modify Cassandra data or configuration.
May create temporary files inside the runner's work directory.

args.name: keyspace_filter
args.description: Optional keyspace name to focus the analysis on.

actions.title: cqlsh -e "DESCRIBE KEYSPACE <ks>"
actions.summary: Show the full DDL for one keyspace (tables, types, indexes, materialized views).
actions.description: Show the full DDL for one keyspace (tables, types, indexes, materialized views).

actions.title: cqlsh -e "DESCRIBE SCHEMA"
actions.summary: Dump the full schema as CQL. Note: large clusters produce big output; rely on the byte cap.
actions.description: Dump the full schema as CQL. Note: large clusters produce big output; rely on the byte cap.

actions.title: cqlsh -e "DESCRIBE KEYSPACES"
actions.summary: List all keyspaces.
actions.description: List all keyspaces.

actions.title: cqlsh -e "LIST ROLES"
actions.summary: List all roles + their grants (requires CassandraAuthorizer/Authenticator).
actions.description: List all roles + their grants (requires CassandraAuthorizer/Authenticator).

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.peers_v2
actions.summary: List the peer nodes as this coordinator sees them: dc, rack, schema version, tokens.
actions.description: List the peer nodes as this coordinator sees them: dc, rack, schema version, tokens.

argv: cqlsh -e 'SELECT peer, data_center, rack, schema_version, host_id, tokens FROM system.peers_v2;' "${CQLSH_HOST:-127.0.0.1}" "${CQLSH_PORT:-9042}" || cqlsh -e 'SELECT peer, data_center, rack, schema_version, host_id, tokens FROM system.peers;' "${CQLSH_HOST:-127.0.0.1}" "${CQLSH_PORT:-9042}"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.size_estimates
actions.summary: Show per-table partition + size estimates from the gossiped size_estimates table.
actions.description: Show per-table partition + size estimates from the gossiped size_estimates table.

argv: cqlsh -e 'SELECT keyspace_name, table_name, range_start, range_end, mean_partition_size, partitions_count FROM system.size_estimates LIMIT 200;' "${CQLSH_HOST:-127.0.0.1}" "${CQLSH_PORT:-9042}"

actions.title: nodetool assassinate <address>
actions.summary: Forcibly removes a dead node from gossip without streaming data. ONLY use when the node is permanently gone AND removenode failed. Risks: orphaned data, hint bleed, token misownership.
actions.description: Forcibly removes a dead node from gossip without streaming data. ONLY use when the node is permanently gone AND removenode failed. Risks: orphaned data, hint bleed, token misownership.
side_effects: No data streaming — data that was on the node is gone.
Other replicas eventually catch up via repair.

args.name: address
args.description: IP address of the dead node.

actions.title: nodetool cleanup [ks]
actions.summary: Removes data no longer owned by this node (after a topology change). IO-heavy.
actions.description: Removes data no longer owned by this node (after a topology change). IO-heavy.
side_effects: SSTables rewritten without data that moved off this node.
Heavy IO + CPU; may take hours on large tables.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.keyspace }}' ]; then nodetool cleanup {{ args.keyspace }}; else nodetool cleanup; fi

actions.title: nodetool clearsnapshot -t <name>
actions.summary: Deletes one snapshot tag from all keyspaces. Frees disk that was pinned by the snapshot.
actions.description: Deletes one snapshot tag from all keyspaces. Frees disk that was pinned by the snapshot.
side_effects: Disk space reclaims as the underlying SSTables become orphaned.

actions.title: nodetool compact <ks> [table]
actions.summary: Forces major compaction. For STCS this merges everything into one big SSTable — almost always a mistake. Prefer per-token-range compaction or letting the strategy run.
actions.description: Forces major compaction. For STCS this merges everything into one big SSTable — almost always a mistake. Prefer per-token-range compaction or letting the strategy run.
side_effects: Heavy disk + CPU for the duration.
For STCS, creates one giant SSTable that is hard to compact later.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.table }}' ]; then nodetool compact {{ args.keyspace }} {{ args.table }}; else nodetool compact {{ args.keyspace }}; fi

actions.title: nodetool compactionhistory
actions.summary: List the last few compactions with bytes-in/out, duration, and dropped tombstones.
actions.description: List the last few compactions with bytes-in/out, duration, and dropped tombstones.

actions.title: Cassandra compaction statistics
actions.summary: Runs `nodetool compactionstats`. Pending compactions in the dozens-to-hundreds indicate the node is behind. Triggering repair on a node already behind on compactions usually makes things worse — wait for the queue to drain before recommending repair.
actions.description: Runs `nodetool compactionstats`. Pending compactions in the dozens-to-hundreds indicate the node is behind. Triggering repair on a node already behind on compactions usually makes things worse — wait for the queue to drain before recommending repair.

actions.title: nodetool decommission
actions.summary: Streams this node's data to other replicas, then leaves the ring. NOT reversible without re-bootstrapping.
actions.description: Streams this node's data to other replicas, then leaves the ring. NOT reversible without re-bootstrapping.
side_effects: All data streams to remaining replicas.
Heavy network + disk on this and peer nodes.
Node leaves the ring; tokens are reassigned.
Can take many hours on big datasets.

actions.title: nodetool describecluster
actions.summary: Show the cluster name, partitioner, snitch, and schema versions per host. Schema disagreement here is a sign of partial DDL propagation.
actions.description: Show the cluster name, partitioner, snitch, and schema versions per host. Schema disagreement here is a sign of partial DDL propagation.

actions.title: nodetool describering <keyspace>
actions.summary: Show token range → replica endpoint mapping for one keyspace.
actions.description: Show token range → replica endpoint mapping for one keyspace.

actions.title: nodetool drain
actions.summary: Stops accepting writes, flushes memtables, persists commit log positions. Node is unusable until restart.
actions.description: Stops accepting writes, flushes memtables, persists commit log positions. Node is unusable until restart.

actions.title: nodetool failuredetector
actions.summary: Show phi accrual failure detector scores per peer. Phi > 8 ≈ marked down.
actions.description: Show phi accrual failure detector scores per peer. Phi > 8 ≈ marked down.

actions.title: nodetool flush [ks] [table]
actions.summary: Forces memtable → SSTable flush. Without args: all keyspaces. Brief IO spike + writeahead replay simplification.
actions.description: Forces memtable → SSTable flush. Without args: all keyspaces. Brief IO spike + writeahead replay simplification.
side_effects: Memtables for the targeted scope are flushed to disk.
Commit log may be marked clean for the affected segments.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.table }}' ]; then nodetool flush {{ args.keyspace }} {{ args.table }}; elif [ -n '{{ args.keyspace }}' ]; then nodetool flush {{ args.keyspace }}; else nodetool flush; fi

actions.title: nodetool getcompactionthroughput
actions.summary: Show the current compaction throughput cap (MB/s; 0 = unlimited).
actions.description: Show the current compaction throughput cap (MB/s; 0 = unlimited).

actions.title: nodetool getconcurrentcompactors
actions.summary: Show the current concurrent_compactors setting.
actions.description: Show the current concurrent_compactors setting.

actions.title: nodetool getendpoints <ks> <table> <key>
actions.summary: Show which replicas own a specific partition key. Use to confirm read/write routing.
actions.description: Show which replicas own a specific partition key. Use to confirm read/write routing.

actions.title: nodetool getlogginglevels
actions.summary: Show the current per-logger levels.
actions.description: Show the current per-logger levels.

actions.title: nodetool gossipinfo
actions.summary: Show per-peer gossip state — schema version, status, load, dc, rack, generation.
actions.description: Show per-peer gossip state — schema version, status, load, dc, rack, generation.

actions.title: nodetool info
actions.summary: Show this node: uptime, heap, load, exceptions, key+row+counter cache hit rates.
actions.description: Show this node: uptime, heap, load, exceptions, key+row+counter cache hit rates.

actions.title: nodetool invalidatecountercache
actions.summary: Drops the counter cache.
actions.description: Drops the counter cache.

actions.title: nodetool invalidatekeycache
actions.summary: Drops the key cache. Reads pay cold-cache cost until it warms.
actions.description: Drops the key cache. Reads pay cold-cache cost until it warms.
side_effects: Next reads must do bloom-filter + summary + index lookups.

actions.title: nodetool invalidaterowcache
actions.summary: Drops the row cache.
actions.description: Drops the row cache.

actions.title: nodetool listsnapshots
actions.summary: List all snapshots on this node with size + creation timestamp.
actions.description: List all snapshots on this node with size + creation timestamp.

actions.title: nodetool netstats
actions.summary: Show streaming + read repair stats: completed/pending bytes, files transferred, pool stats.
actions.description: Show streaming + read repair stats: completed/pending bytes, files transferred, pool stats.

actions.title: nodetool proxyhistograms
actions.summary: Show coordinator-side read/write latency histograms — what clients actually see.
actions.description: Show coordinator-side read/write latency histograms — what clients actually see.

actions.title: nodetool rebuild [source_dc]
actions.summary: Re-bootstraps a node by streaming from another DC (or any DC if unspecified). Use after expanding into a new DC.
actions.description: Re-bootstraps a node by streaming from another DC (or any DC if unspecified). Use after expanding into a new DC.
side_effects: Existing data on this node is NOT removed first.
Best run on a node that has empty data dirs.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.source_dc }}' ]; then nodetool rebuild {{ args.source_dc }}; else nodetool rebuild; fi

actions.title: nodetool removenode <host-id>
actions.summary: Removes a down node from the cluster and streams its data from other replicas. Preferred over assassinate when there's quorum.
actions.description: Removes a down node from the cluster and streams its data from other replicas. Preferred over assassinate when there's quorum.
side_effects: Other replicas stream the dead node's data to their successors.

actions.title: Cassandra repair
actions.summary: Wraps `nodetool repair`.
actions.description: Wraps `nodetool repair`. The most dangerous "normal" operation — repair reconciles data between replicas, can take hours, produces significant cluster-wide load, may interact poorly with TTL/tombstone-heavy tables, and can worsen latency on every replica it touches. Always inspect ring status, compactions, disk, and logs first. Prefer mode=preview — a dry run that estimates the repair without performing it (requires Cassandra 4.0+) — before a real repair. Refuse to proceed if the ring has DN/UJ/UL/UM nodes.
side_effects: Repair coordinates with replicas across the cluster.
Generates significant network, CPU, and disk I/O.
Can run for minutes to hours depending on dataset size.
May worsen latency on the local node and on replica nodes for the duration.

args.name: table
args.description: Optional single table to repair (empty = whole keyspace).

args.name: primary_range
args.description: Restrict to the primary token range (recommended).

examples.title: Dry-run repair preview on one keyspace

actions.title: nodetool ring [keyspace]
actions.summary: Show the token ring with owner host per token.
actions.description: Show the token ring with owner host per token.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.keyspace }}' ]; then nodetool ring {{ args.keyspace }}; else nodetool ring; fi

actions.title: nodetool setcompactionthroughput <MB/s>
actions.summary: Sets max compaction throughput. 0 = unlimited (use carefully).
actions.description: Sets max compaction throughput. 0 = unlimited (use carefully).
side_effects: In-flight + future compactions throttled to the new cap.
Lower values reduce IO pressure but grow SSTable count.

actions.title: nodetool setlogginglevel <logger> <level>
actions.summary: Sets one logger's level. Use empty logger to reset all to the configured defaults.
actions.description: Sets one logger's level. Use empty logger to reset all to the configured defaults.
side_effects: DEBUG/TRACE levels can dramatically increase log volume.

actions.title: nodetool snapshot -t <name> [ks]
actions.summary: Atomic hard-link snapshot of SSTables. Cheap to take, expensive if left around.
actions.description: Atomic hard-link snapshot of SSTables. Cheap to take, expensive if left around.
side_effects: Hard links created in each table's snapshots/<name>/ dir.
Disk usage grows as SSTables roll over (snapshot pins originals).

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.keyspace }}' ]; then nodetool snapshot -t {{ args.tag }} {{ args.keyspace }}; else nodetool snapshot -t {{ args.tag }}; fi

actions.title: Cassandra node ring status
actions.summary: Runs `nodetool status`. Read-only — does not change Cassandra state. Use this before suggesting repair, cleanup, decommission, replacement, or topology changes. If any node is DN/UJ/UL/UM, do not recommend repair until the failure mode is understood.
actions.description: Runs `nodetool status`. Read-only — does not change Cassandra state. Use this before suggesting repair, cleanup, decommission, replacement, or topology changes. If any node is DN/UJ/UL/UM, do not recommend repair until the failure mode is understood.
side_effects: Starts a short-lived JMX connection to the local Cassandra node.
May fail if JMX auth is misconfigured.
Does not modify Cassandra data or cluster state.

actions.title: nodetool stop <operation>
actions.summary: Stops in-flight operations of one type (COMPACTION, CLEANUP, VERIFY, etc).
actions.description: Stops in-flight operations of one type (COMPACTION, CLEANUP, VERIFY, etc).
side_effects: In-flight ops of the named type are aborted.
SSTables in progress are abandoned (no partial result).

actions.title: nodetool tablehistograms <ks> <table>
actions.summary: Show local-node read/write/sstable/partition-size histograms for one table.
actions.description: Show local-node read/write/sstable/partition-size histograms for one table.

actions.title: Cassandra table stats
actions.summary: Runs `nodetool tablestats`, optionally scoped to a single keyspace. Read-only. Output can be large for clusters with many tables. Use to identify tables with large sstable counts or large on-disk size — repairs on very large or tombstone-heavy tables are risky and worth surfacing before a repair recommendation.
actions.description: Runs `nodetool tablestats`, optionally scoped to a single keyspace. Read-only. Output can be large for clusters with many tables. Use to identify tables with large sstable counts or large on-disk size — repairs on very large or tombstone-heavy tables are risky and worth surfacing before a repair recommendation.
side_effects: Output can be large for clusters with many tables.

args.name: keyspace
args.description: Optional keyspace to scope to (omit for all keyspaces).

actions.title: Cassandra thread pool stats
actions.summary: Runs `nodetool tpstats` for active/pending/blocked counts per pool. High pending or blocked counts on MutationStage, CompactionExecutor, or ReadStage usually indicate ongoing pressure — investigate the cause before recommending operations that add load (repair, large reads, compaction tuning).
actions.description: Runs `nodetool tpstats` for active/pending/blocked counts per pool. High pending or blocked counts on MutationStage, CompactionExecutor, or ReadStage usually indicate ongoing pressure — investigate the cause before recommending operations that add load (repair, large reads, compaction tuning).

actions.title: nodetool verify [ks] [table]
actions.summary: Verifies SSTable checksums for one (or all) tables. Detects on-disk corruption.
actions.description: Verifies SSTable checksums for one (or all) tables. Detects on-disk corruption.
side_effects: Reads every SSTable for the targeted scope.

argv: if [ -n '{{ args.table }}' ]; then nodetool verify {{ args.keyspace }} {{ args.table }}; elif [ -n '{{ args.keyspace }}' ]; then nodetool verify {{ args.keyspace }}; else nodetool verify; fi

actions.title: nodetool version
actions.summary: Show the Cassandra version string for the node.
actions.description: Show the Cassandra version string for the node.

packs.name: ClickHouse analytics DB
packs.description: ClickHouse server + table introspection — metrics, errors, slow & failed queries, parts/partitions, merges, mutations, replication queue, Keeper/ZK health, detached parts, distributed-send backlog, backups — plus narrow mutators (OPTIMIZE, KILL QUERY, SYSTEM RELOAD CONFIG, replica ops). Auth via CH_HOST + CH_USER + CH_PASSWORD env vars on the runner host. Uses clickhouse-client with --query to keep arg surface minimal.

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.asynchronous_metrics
actions.summary: Show periodically-computed metrics — replica lag (ReplicasMaxAbsoluteDelay), max parts per partition, filesystem and memory. The async counterpart to system.metrics.
actions.description: Show periodically-computed metrics — replica lag (ReplicasMaxAbsoluteDelay), max parts per partition, filesystem and memory. The async counterpart to system.metrics.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT metric, value, description FROM system.asynchronous_metrics ORDER BY metric FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.backups
actions.summary: List BACKUP/RESTORE operations since the last server start with status and error. Non-persistent across restarts.
actions.description: List BACKUP/RESTORE operations since the last server start with status and error. Non-persistent across restarts.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT id, name, status, substring(error, 1, 300) AS error, start_time, end_time, num_files, total_size FROM system.backups ORDER BY start_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.detached_parts
actions.summary: List parts ClickHouse detached and will not auto-attach (corruption, manual detach, broken), each with a reason. Empty is healthy.
actions.description: List parts ClickHouse detached and will not auto-attach (corruption, manual detach, broken), each with a reason. Empty is healthy.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, partition_id, name, reason, disk, bytes_on_disk FROM system.detached_parts ORDER BY bytes_on_disk DESC LIMIT 500 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.dictionaries
actions.summary: List loaded dictionaries with status, element count, source.
actions.description: List loaded dictionaries with status, element count, source.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, status, element_count, source FROM system.dictionaries FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.disks
actions.summary: List configured disks with free/used bytes + paths.
actions.description: List configured disks with free/used bytes + paths.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, path, free_space, total_space, type FROM system.disks FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.distribution_queue
actions.summary: Show distributed-table async send backlog — pending files, errors, blocked sends. Spot a Distributed table not flushing to shards.
actions.description: Show distributed-table async send backlog — pending files, errors, blocked sends. Spot a Distributed table not flushing to shards.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, is_blocked, error_count, data_files, data_compressed_bytes, broken_data_files, last_exception_time, substring(last_exception, 1, 300) AS last_exception FROM system.distribution_queue ORDER BY data_files DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.errors
actions.summary: List error codes seen since startup with counts and the last message. Spot error storms.
actions.description: List error codes seen since startup with counts and the last message. Spot error storms.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, code, value, last_error_time, substring(last_error_message, 1, 300) AS last_message, remote FROM system.errors WHERE value > 0 ORDER BY last_error_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: List a Keeper/ZooKeeper path (system.zookeeper)
actions.summary: List child nodes and metadata under a Keeper/ZooKeeper path. system.zookeeper requires a path filter, so the path arg is mandatory.
actions.description: List child nodes and metadata under a Keeper/ZooKeeper path. system.zookeeper requires a path filter, so the path arg is mandatory.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, value, numChildren, ctime, mtime, version, czxid, mzxid FROM system.zookeeper WHERE path = '$ZKPATH' ORDER BY name LIMIT 500 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: Cancel a stuck mutation (KILL MUTATION)
actions.summary: Cancel one ClickHouse mutation by its (database, table, mutation_id) — KILL MUTATION WHERE …. Use to stop a mutation wedged on a failure (see ch.stuck_mutations) so the table's merges and inserts can proceed. Destructive — the mutation is abandoned mid-flight — so risk:critical → default-denied. An operator allows it by explicit policy, and WHICH mutations are permitted is a portal policy decision.
actions.description: Cancel one ClickHouse mutation by its (database, table, mutation_id) — KILL MUTATION WHERE …. Use to stop a mutation wedged on a failure (see ch.stuck_mutations) so the table's merges and inserts can proceed. Destructive — the mutation is abandoned mid-flight — so risk:critical → default-denied. An operator allows it by explicit policy, and WHICH mutations are permitted is a portal policy decision.
side_effects: The matching mutation is cancelled; its partial work is abandoned.
Targets exactly one mutation — all three identity args are required.
Reversible only by re-issuing the ALTER that created the mutation.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "KILL MUTATION WHERE database = '{{ args.database }}' AND table = '{{ args.table }}' AND mutation_id = '{{ args.mutation_id }}'"

actions.title: KILL QUERY WHERE query_id = '<id>'
actions.summary: Cancels one running query by ID.
actions.description: Cancels one running query by ID.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "KILL QUERY WHERE query_id = '$Q' SYNC"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.merges
actions.summary: List in-progress + queued merges.
actions.description: List in-progress + queued merges.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, elapsed, progress, num_parts, source_part_names, result_part_name, total_size_bytes_compressed, memory_usage FROM system.merges FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.mutations
actions.summary: List in-progress + pending mutations (ALTER TABLE).
actions.description: List in-progress + pending mutations (ALTER TABLE).

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, mutation_id, command, create_time, is_done, parts_to_do, latest_failed_part, latest_fail_reason FROM system.mutations WHERE NOT is_done FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL
actions.summary: Forces merge of all parts into one. Heavy disk + CPU.
actions.description: Forces merge of all parts into one. Heavy disk + CPU.
side_effects: Background merge runs synchronously; can take minutes-to-hours on large tables.
Temporary doubling of disk space during the merge.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "OPTIMIZE TABLE {{ args.database }}.{{ args.table }} FINAL"

actions.title: Active part count per partition (system.parts)
actions.summary: List active parts grouped by partition, top offenders first. Where you actually diagnose too-many-parts / merge backlog (parts_summary is table-level only).
actions.description: List active parts grouped by partition, top offenders first. Where you actually diagnose too-many-parts / merge backlog (parts_summary is table-level only).

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, partition, count() AS parts, sum(rows) AS rows, sum(bytes_on_disk) AS bytes FROM system.parts WHERE active GROUP BY database, table, partition ORDER BY parts DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT system.parts (by table)
actions.summary: List active part counts + sizes per table. Use to spot too-many-parts.
actions.description: List active part counts + sizes per table. Use to spot too-many-parts.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, sum(rows) as rows, sum(bytes_on_disk) as bytes, count() as parts FROM system.parts WHERE active GROUP BY database, table ORDER BY bytes DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.processes
actions.summary: List currently-running queries.
actions.description: List currently-running queries.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT query_id, user, elapsed, memory_usage, read_rows, query FROM system.processes ORDER BY elapsed DESC FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: Failed queries from system.query_log (last hour)
actions.summary: List queries that failed in the last hour (exception types 3 and 4) with code, message, and query. The error counterpart to slow_queries.
actions.description: List queries that failed in the last hour (exception types 3 and 4) with code, message, and query. The error counterpart to slow_queries.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event_time, type, user, query_id, exception_code, substring(exception, 1, 300) AS exception, substring(query, 1, 200) AS q FROM system.query_log WHERE event_time > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AND type IN (3, 4) ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SYSTEM RELOAD CONFIG
actions.summary: Re-reads config.xml + users.xml without restarting.
actions.description: Re-reads config.xml + users.xml without restarting.

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.replicas
actions.summary: Show per-replica state — leader, queue size, log delays, errors.
actions.description: Show per-replica state — leader, queue size, log delays, errors.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, is_leader, is_readonly, future_parts, parts_to_check, queue_size, absolute_delay, log_max_index, log_pointer, total_replicas, active_replicas FROM system.replicas FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.replication_queue
actions.summary: Show per-task replication queue — retries, postpones, last exception. Spot stuck fetches/merges (distinct from per-replica state).
actions.description: Show per-task replication queue — retries, postpones, last exception. Spot stuck fetches/merges (distinct from per-replica state).

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, type, create_time, new_part_name, num_tries, is_currently_executing, num_postponed, postpone_reason, last_attempt_time, substring(last_exception, 1, 300) AS last_exception FROM system.replication_queue ORDER BY num_tries DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: system.query_log slowest queries
actions.summary: List top 50 slowest queries from the last hour.
actions.description: List top 50 slowest queries from the last hour.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event_time, query_duration_ms, user, read_rows, memory_usage, query_id, substring(query, 1, 300) AS q FROM system.query_log WHERE event_time > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AND type = 2 ORDER BY query_duration_ms DESC LIMIT 50 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: Failing mutations (system.mutations)
actions.summary: List mutations stuck on a failure (latest_fail_reason set), with the failed part and reason. Narrower than the full mutation queue — surfaces only what is actively failing.
actions.description: List mutations stuck on a failure (latest_fail_reason set), with the failed part and reason. Narrower than the full mutation queue — surfaces only what is actively failing.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, mutation_id, command, create_time, parts_to_do, latest_failed_part, latest_fail_time, substring(latest_fail_reason, 1, 300) AS fail_reason FROM system.mutations WHERE NOT is_done AND latest_fail_reason != '' ORDER BY create_time ASC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SYSTEM DROP REPLICA <name>
actions.summary: Remove a replica's metadata from ZooKeeper for one table. Use only for an already-dead replica that is never coming back. The replica cannot rejoin without recreating its tables. Wrong replica name destroys a healthy cluster member's metadata.
actions.description: Remove a replica's metadata from ZooKeeper for one table. Use only for an already-dead replica that is never coming back. The replica cannot rejoin without recreating its tables. Wrong replica name destroys a healthy cluster member's metadata.
side_effects: Replica cannot rejoin without recreating tables.

args.name: replica
args.description: Replica name as listed in system.replicas.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SYSTEM DROP REPLICA '$RPL' FROM TABLE $TBL"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.events
actions.summary: List process-lifetime event counters.
actions.description: List process-lifetime event counters.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event, value FROM system.events ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SYSTEM FLUSH LOGS
actions.summary: Force write buffered system.*log tables to disk. Use before querying very recent activity from query_log, part_log, etc.
actions.description: Force write buffered system.*log tables to disk. Use before querying very recent activity from query_log, part_log, etc.

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.metrics
actions.summary: Show per-metric current values (gauges + counters).
actions.description: Show per-metric current values (gauges + counters).

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT metric, value FROM system.metrics WHERE value != 0 ORDER BY metric FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SYSTEM RESTART REPLICA <table>
actions.summary: Reinitialize the local replication state for one table. Useful when the replica is stuck or its ZooKeeper state diverged. Heavy — may re-fetch many parts.
actions.description: Reinitialize the local replication state for one table. Useful when the replica is stuck or its ZooKeeper state diverged. Heavy — may re-fetch many parts.
side_effects: May trigger large data re-fetch from peers.

actions.title: SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA <table>
actions.summary: Wait for the local replica to catch up with all peers. Returns when in sync. Use to confirm replication caught up before doing reads from this replica.
actions.description: Wait for the local replica to catch up with all peers. Returns when in sync. Use to confirm replication caught up before doing reads from this replica.

actions.title: system.tables uncompressed sizes
actions.summary: List top 50 tables by uncompressed size.
actions.description: List top 50 tables by uncompressed size.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, total_rows, total_bytes, total_bytes_uncompressed FROM system.tables WHERE NOT is_temporary AND engine NOT LIKE 'View%' ORDER BY total_bytes_uncompressed DESC LIMIT 50 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: Engine inventory (system.tables)
actions.summary: List all non-system tables with engine, row/byte totals, and part counts. Engine inventory + fleet shape.
actions.description: List all non-system tables with engine, row/byte totals, and part counts. Engine inventory + fleet shape.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, engine, total_rows, total_bytes, parts, active_parts FROM system.tables WHERE database NOT IN ('system','INFORMATION_SCHEMA','information_schema') ORDER BY total_bytes DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

actions.title: SELECT version, uptime
actions.summary: Show server version + uptime.
actions.description: Show server version + uptime.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT version(), uptime() FORMAT TabSeparatedWithNames"

actions.title: SELECT * FROM system.zookeeper_connection
actions.summary: List active Keeper/ZooKeeper connections — host, session age, expiry, API version. Empty on a non-replicated single node.
actions.description: List active Keeper/ZooKeeper connections — host, session age, expiry, API version. Empty on a non-replicated single node.

argv: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, host, port, index, connected_time, session_uptime_elapsed_seconds, is_expired, keeper_api_version, client_id FROM system.zookeeper_connection FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT metric, value, description FROM system.asynchronous_metrics ORDER BY metric FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT id, name, status, substring(error, 1, 300) AS error, start_time, end_time, num_files, total_size FROM system.backups ORDER BY start_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, partition_id, name, reason, disk, bytes_on_disk FROM system.detached_parts ORDER BY bytes_on_disk DESC LIMIT 500 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, status, element_count, source FROM system.dictionaries FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, path, free_space, total_space, type FROM system.disks FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, is_blocked, error_count, data_files, data_compressed_bytes, broken_data_files, last_exception_time, substring(last_exception, 1, 300) AS last_exception FROM system.distribution_queue ORDER BY data_files DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, code, value, last_error_time, substring(last_error_message, 1, 300) AS last_message, remote FROM system.errors WHERE value > 0 ORDER BY last_error_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, value, numChildren, ctime, mtime, version, czxid, mzxid FROM system.zookeeper WHERE path = '$ZKPATH' ORDER BY name LIMIT 500 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "KILL MUTATION WHERE database = '{{ args.database }}' AND table = '{{ args.table }}' AND mutation_id = '{{ args.mutation_id }}'"
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "KILL QUERY WHERE query_id = '$Q' SYNC"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, elapsed, progress, num_parts, source_part_names, result_part_name, total_size_bytes_compressed, memory_usage FROM system.merges FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, mutation_id, command, create_time, is_done, parts_to_do, latest_failed_part, latest_fail_reason FROM system.mutations WHERE NOT is_done FORMAT JSONEachRow"
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "OPTIMIZE TABLE {{ args.database }}.{{ args.table }} FINAL"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, partition, count() AS parts, sum(rows) AS rows, sum(bytes_on_disk) AS bytes FROM system.parts WHERE active GROUP BY database, table, partition ORDER BY parts DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, sum(rows) as rows, sum(bytes_on_disk) as bytes, count() as parts FROM system.parts WHERE active GROUP BY database, table ORDER BY bytes DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT query_id, user, elapsed, memory_usage, read_rows, query FROM system.processes ORDER BY elapsed DESC FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event_time, type, user, query_id, exception_code, substring(exception, 1, 300) AS exception, substring(query, 1, 200) AS q FROM system.query_log WHERE event_time > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AND type IN (3, 4) ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, is_leader, is_readonly, future_parts, parts_to_check, queue_size, absolute_delay, log_max_index, log_pointer, total_replicas, active_replicas FROM system.replicas FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, type, create_time, new_part_name, num_tries, is_currently_executing, num_postponed, postpone_reason, last_attempt_time, substring(last_exception, 1, 300) AS last_exception FROM system.replication_queue ORDER BY num_tries DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event_time, query_duration_ms, user, read_rows, memory_usage, query_id, substring(query, 1, 300) AS q FROM system.query_log WHERE event_time > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AND type = 2 ORDER BY query_duration_ms DESC LIMIT 50 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, table, mutation_id, command, create_time, parts_to_do, latest_failed_part, latest_fail_time, substring(latest_fail_reason, 1, 300) AS fail_reason FROM system.mutations WHERE NOT is_done AND latest_fail_reason != '' ORDER BY create_time ASC LIMIT 100 FORMAT JSONEachRow"
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SYSTEM DROP REPLICA '$RPL' FROM TABLE $TBL"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT event, value FROM system.events ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT metric, value FROM system.metrics WHERE value != 0 ORDER BY metric FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, total_rows, total_bytes, total_bytes_uncompressed FROM system.tables WHERE NOT is_temporary AND engine NOT LIKE 'View%' ORDER BY total_bytes_uncompressed DESC LIMIT 50 FORMAT JSONEachRow"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT database, name, engine, total_rows, total_bytes, parts, active_parts FROM system.tables WHERE database NOT IN ('system','INFORMATION_SCHEMA','information_schema') ORDER BY total_bytes DESC LIMIT 200 FORMAT JSONEachRow"
CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT version(), uptime() FORMAT TabSeparatedWithNames"

CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="$CH_PASSWORD" clickhouse-client --host "${CH_HOST:-localhost}" --user "${CH_USER:-default}" --query "SELECT name, host, port, index, connected_time, session_uptime_elapsed_seconds, is_expired, keeper_api_version, client_id FROM system.zookeeper_connection FORMAT JSONEachRow"

packs.name: cloud-init operations
packs.description: cloud-init introspection + boot diagnostics + stage re-runs. Use when an EC2/GCE/Azure VM finishes booting but the workload didn't come up the way it should: check overall status, blame slow modules, dump user-data / cloud-config / vendor-data as the instance actually saw them, tail the cloud-init logs, and (with operator approval) re-run individual modules or the full init pipeline.

actions.title: cloud-init analyze blame
actions.summary: List modules ranked by duration. The single fastest answer to "why did cloud-init take 90 seconds?" — the slow module is at the top.
actions.description: List modules ranked by duration. The single fastest answer to "why did cloud-init take 90 seconds?" — the slow module is at the top.

actions.title: cloud-init analyze dump
actions.summary: Dump raw event records from cloud-init.log as JSON. Useful for downstream tooling that wants structured timing data.
actions.description: Dump raw event records from cloud-init.log as JSON. Useful for downstream tooling that wants structured timing data.

actions.title: cloud-init analyze show
actions.summary: Show per-stage timing for the most recent boot — when each stage started, duration, and which records contributed. Read-only.
actions.description: Show per-stage timing for the most recent boot — when each stage started, duration, and which records contributed. Read-only.

actions.title: cloud-init clean
actions.summary: Wipe ALL cloud-init state — /var/lib/cloud/, logs, instance-id, module-run records. NEXT BOOT cloud-init treats the host as a brand-new instance and re-runs every module from scratch. Combine with a reboot to genuinely re-bootstrap the host. Catastrophic on a long-lived host where users have been added outside cloud-init — those won't be re-added.
actions.description: Wipe ALL cloud-init state — /var/lib/cloud/, logs, instance-id, module-run records. NEXT BOOT cloud-init treats the host as a brand-new instance and re-runs every module from scratch. Combine with a reboot to genuinely re-bootstrap the host. Catastrophic on a long-lived host where users have been added outside cloud-init — those won't be re-added.
side_effects: Next boot re-runs init-local, init, config, final.
Any post-cloud-init changes that cloud-init didn't make will be left alone, BUT cloud-init modules will treat their state as fresh and may overwrite.

args.name: seed
args.description: Also wipe the seed dir (NoCloud datasource).

argv: if [ '{{ args.seed }}' = 'true' ]; then cloud-init clean --seed; else cloud-init clean; fi

actions.title: cloud-init clean --logs
actions.summary: Truncate cloud-init logs without resetting instance state. Use to clear noisy stale errors before re-running a stage to confirm a fix worked.
actions.description: Truncate cloud-init logs without resetting instance state. Use to clear noisy stale errors before re-running a stage to confirm a fix worked.
side_effects: instance-id + module-run state preserved (use `clean` to also reset those).

actions.title: cat /var/lib/cloud/instance/cloud-config.txt
actions.summary: Dump the final merged cloud-config (user-data + vendor-data + system defaults) as cloud-init evaluated it. The source of truth for "what did cloud-init actually try to do?".
actions.description: Dump the final merged cloud-config (user-data + vendor-data + system defaults) as cloud-init evaluated it. The source of truth for "what did cloud-init actually try to do?".
side_effects: May expose secrets baked into user-data / vendor-data — the merged cloud-config is a superset; the runner redacts via its configured redaction rules.

actions.title: cloud-init collect-logs
actions.summary: Bundle /var/log/cloud-init* + journals + config into a tarball under /tmp/cloud-init.tar.gz. Use when you need to share state with cloud-init upstream or attach to a support ticket.
actions.description: Bundle /var/log/cloud-init* + journals + config into a tarball under /tmp/cloud-init.tar.gz. Use when you need to share state with cloud-init upstream or attach to a support ticket.
side_effects: May briefly stress disk + tar throughput.

actions.title: cloud-init init
actions.summary: Run the (non-local) init stage — fetch user-data + vendor-data, merge cloud-config, populate /var/lib/cloud/instance/. Without `cloud-init clean` first, an unchanged instance-id is a no-op.
actions.description: Run the (non-local) init stage — fetch user-data + vendor-data, merge cloud-config, populate /var/lib/cloud/instance/. Without `cloud-init clean` first, an unchanged instance-id is a no-op.
side_effects: Subsequent stages (config, final) NOT re-run unless invoked.

actions.title: cloud-init init --local
actions.summary: Run the init-local stage — datasource detection, fallback networking, early disk setup. Normally only runs at boot. Re-running mid-life rarely useful except for testing a datasource fix.
actions.description: Run the init-local stage — datasource detection, fallback networking, early disk setup. Normally only runs at boot. Re-running mid-life rarely useful except for testing a datasource fix.

actions.title: cat /var/lib/cloud/data/instance-id
actions.summary: Show the instance ID cloud-init currently tracks. If this differs from the cloud's idea, cloud-init thinks the machine is "new" and may re-run all modules at next boot.
actions.description: Show the instance ID cloud-init currently tracks. If this differs from the cloud's idea, cloud-init thinks the machine is "new" and may re-run all modules at next boot.

actions.title: journalctl -u cloud-init -n <lines>
actions.summary: Tail systemd journal entries for the four cloud-init units (cloud-init, cloud-init-local, cloud-config, cloud-final). Use when boot failed before /var/log/cloud-init.log was even written.
actions.description: Tail systemd journal entries for the four cloud-init units (cloud-init, cloud-init-local, cloud-config, cloud-final). Use when boot failed before /var/log/cloud-init.log was even written.

actions.title: tail /var/log/cloud-init.log
actions.summary: Tail the main cloud-init log (last N lines). Contains module execution, errors, and per-stage timing.
actions.description: Tail the main cloud-init log (last N lines). Contains module execution, errors, and per-stage timing.

actions.title: cloud-init modules --mode=config
actions.summary: Re-run every module in the `config` stage (locale, users-groups, ssh, set-passwords, apt-pipelining, package-update-upgrade-install, etc.). Mutates the host the same way boot does. Use after fixing a buggy cloud-config — no need to reboot.
actions.description: Re-run every module in the `config` stage (locale, users-groups, ssh, set-passwords, apt-pipelining, package-update-upgrade-install, etc.). Mutates the host the same way boot does. Use after fixing a buggy cloud-config — no need to reboot.
side_effects: Every module in the config stage re-runs.
User accounts, ssh keys, apt sources may change.
Idempotent in design — but only as idempotent as the modules in your config.

actions.title: cloud-init modules --mode=final
actions.summary: Re-run every module in the `final` stage (runcmd, scripts-user, phone-home, power-state-change, etc.). Anything that was scripted to run "after first boot" runs again. Power-state modules can reboot/shutdown — make sure your cloud-config has no power-state directive before invoking.
actions.description: Re-run every module in the `final` stage (runcmd, scripts-user, phone-home, power-state-change, etc.). Anything that was scripted to run "after first boot" runs again. Power-state modules can reboot/shutdown — make sure your cloud-config has no power-state directive before invoking.
side_effects: Every module in the final stage re-runs.
User scripts under /var/lib/cloud/scripts/per-once and per-boot may execute.

actions.title: tail /var/log/cloud-init-output.log
actions.summary: Tail captured stdout/stderr from every command cloud-init ran during boot (`runcmd`, `bootcmd`, package install, etc.). Where to look when a shell command in cloud-config silently failed.
actions.description: Tail captured stdout/stderr from every command cloud-init ran during boot (`runcmd`, `bootcmd`, package install, etc.). Where to look when a shell command in cloud-config silently failed.

actions.title: cloud-init query <key>
actions.summary: Pull one key out of cloud-init's runtime metadata (instance-id, hostname, region, availability_zone, …). Pass `--all` to dump everything. Sensitive fields are redacted by cloud-init itself.
actions.description: Pull one key out of cloud-init's runtime metadata (instance-id, hostname, region, availability_zone, …). Pass `--all` to dump everything. Sensitive fields are redacted by cloud-init itself.

actions.title: cloud-init schema --system
actions.summary: Validate the rendered cloud-config against the cloud-init schema. Use to catch typos / deprecated keys before they cause a silent no-op on next boot.
actions.description: Validate the rendered cloud-config against the cloud-init schema. Use to catch typos / deprecated keys before they cause a silent no-op on next boot.

actions.title: cloud-init single --name=<module>
actions.summary: Run one named cloud-init module out-of-band (e.g. `users-groups`, `ssh`, `runcmd`, `set_hostname`). The module is invoked with its current configuration; effects depend entirely on which module.
actions.description: Run one named cloud-init module out-of-band (e.g. `users-groups`, `ssh`, `runcmd`, `set_hostname`). The module is invoked with its current configuration; effects depend entirely on which module.
side_effects: Behavior depends on the module — could mutate users, ssh keys, hostname, packages, runcmd, etc.

args.name: frequency
args.description: When to consider the module previously run.

actions.title: cloud-init status
actions.summary: Show the current overall cloud-init state — `running`, `done`, `error`, `disabled`, or `not run`. Use as the first answer to "did boot finish?".
actions.description: Show the current overall cloud-init state — `running`, `done`, `error`, `disabled`, or `not run`. Use as the first answer to "did boot finish?".

actions.title: cloud-init status --long [--wait]
actions.summary: Show detailed status — per-stage state, last update, recoverable + last errors, datasource detected. With `block: true`, blocks until cloud-init finishes (useful right after boot).
actions.description: Show detailed status — per-stage state, last update, recoverable + last errors, datasource detected. With `block: true`, blocks until cloud-init finishes (useful right after boot).
side_effects: One CLI call (may block up to timeout if block=true).

args.name: block
args.description: Block until cloud-init exits the running state (passes --wait).
