argv: if [ '{{ args.block }}' = 'true' ]; then cloud-init status --long --wait; else cloud-init status --long; fi

actions.title: cat /var/lib/cloud/instance/user-data.txt
actions.summary: Dump raw user-data as cloud-init received it (after MIME decoding, before cloud-config merging). Compare against what you set in the launch config to confirm delivery.
actions.description: Dump raw user-data as cloud-init received it (after MIME decoding, before cloud-config merging). Compare against what you set in the launch config to confirm delivery.
side_effects: May expose secrets baked into user-data — the runner redacts via its configured redaction rules.

actions.title: cat /var/lib/cloud/instance/vendor-data.txt
actions.summary: Dump vendor-data the cloud provider injected (AWS / GCE / Azure provider defaults that ran before user-data). Useful when "why is X installed that I didn't ask for?" leads back to a vendor module.
actions.description: Dump vendor-data the cloud provider injected (AWS / GCE / Azure provider defaults that ran before user-data). Useful when "why is X installed that I didn't ask for?" leads back to a vendor module.
side_effects: May expose secrets baked into vendor-data — the runner redacts via its configured redaction rules.

actions.title: cloud-init --version
actions.summary: Show the build + version string for the cloud-init on this host.
actions.description: Show the build + version string for the cloud-init on this host.

packs.name: Cloudflare zone operations
packs.description: Read-only zone, DNS record, cache, firewall, and analytics introspection plus narrow mutators (purge cache, enable/disable dev mode). Auth via CF_API_TOKEN env var.

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>/settings
actions.summary: List all zone-level settings (cache TTL, security level, SSL mode, etc).
actions.description: List all zone-level settings (cache TTL, security level, SSL mode, etc).

actions.title: PATCH /zones/<id>/settings/development_mode (off)
actions.summary: Ends development mode early. Cache resumes normal behavior immediately.
actions.description: Ends development mode early. Cache resumes normal behavior immediately.
side_effects: Cache resumes serving from edge — origin load drops back to normal.

actions.title: PATCH /zones/<id>/settings/development_mode
actions.summary: Enables development mode for 3 hours — bypasses caching for this zone.
actions.description: Enables development mode for 3 hours — bypasses caching for this zone.

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>/dns_records
actions.summary: List all DNS records in one zone.
actions.description: List all DNS records in one zone.

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>/rulesets/phases/http_request_firewall_custom/entrypoint
actions.summary: List zone WAF custom rules — the entrypoint ruleset for the http_request_firewall_custom phase. Replaces the legacy /firewall/rules API, which Cloudflare sunset on 2025-06-15.
actions.description: List zone WAF custom rules — the entrypoint ruleset for the http_request_firewall_custom phase. Replaces the legacy /firewall/rules API, which Cloudflare sunset on 2025-06-15.

actions.title: GET /zones
actions.summary: List all zones accessible by the API token.
actions.description: List all zones accessible by the API token.

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>/pagerules
actions.summary: List all page rules for one zone.
actions.description: List all page rules for one zone.

actions.title: POST /zones/<id>/purge_cache (everything)
actions.summary: Purges ALL cached content for one zone. Cold-cache origin spike — every URL becomes a miss until it warms again. Avoid on busy zones.
actions.description: Purges ALL cached content for one zone. Cold-cache origin spike — every URL becomes a miss until it warms again. Avoid on busy zones.
side_effects: Origin sees a 100% miss rate until cache re-warms.
Rate-limited by Cloudflare to once per minute per zone.

actions.title: POST /zones/<id>/purge_cache (single URL)
actions.summary: Purges cache for one URL. Safe — only affects that URL's cached variants.
actions.description: Purges cache for one URL. Safe — only affects that URL's cached variants.
side_effects: Next request for that URL is a cache miss; hits origin.

argv: { [ -n "$CF_API_TOKEN" ] && printf 'Authorization: Bearer %s\n' "$CF_API_TOKEN"; } | curl -sS -XPOST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/{{ args.zone_id }}/purge_cache" -H @- -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"files":["{{ args.url }}"]}'

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>/analytics/dashboard
actions.summary: Show last-24h analytics for one zone (requests, bandwidth, threats).
actions.description: Show last-24h analytics for one zone (requests, bandwidth, threats).

actions.title: GET /zones/<id>
actions.summary: Show one zone's full settings + state + nameservers.
actions.description: Show one zone's full settings + state + nameservers.

packs.name: CockroachDB
packs.description: Investigate a CockroachDB cluster over the `cockroach` CLI — node liveness / store capacity, under-replicated & unavailable ranges, running queries / sessions / transactions, contention & locks, jobs, statement statistics, table sizes, and cluster settings — plus a few gated operators (cancel a query / session / job, pause / resume a job, decommission / recommission a node). Reads are SQL against crdb_internal / SHOW statements; mutators use SQL or `cockroach node`.

actions.title: Cancel a job (CANCEL JOB)
actions.summary: Cancel one job by id (from jobs). The job stops and rolls back any partial work where applicable. Use to abort a wrong or stuck schema change / backup / import.
actions.description: Cancel one job by id (from jobs). The job stops and rolls back any partial work where applicable. Use to abort a wrong or stuck schema change / backup / import.
side_effects: The target job is cancelled and begins reverting.

actions.title: Cancel a running query (CANCEL QUERY)
actions.summary: Stop one in-flight query by its query_id (from cluster_queries). The query ends with an error; the session/connection stays open. Use to kill a runaway query without dropping the client.
actions.description: Stop one in-flight query by its query_id (from cluster_queries). The query ends with an error; the session/connection stays open. Use to kill a runaway query without dropping the client.
side_effects: The target query is cancelled with an error.

actions.title: Cancel a session (CANCEL SESSION)
actions.summary: End one client session by its session_id (from cluster_sessions) — its current query is cancelled and the connection closed; an open transaction is rolled back. Use to evict a stuck or abusive connection.
actions.description: End one client session by its session_id (from cluster_sessions) — its current query is cancelled and the connection closed; an open transaction is rolled back. Use to evict a stuck or abusive connection.
side_effects: The target session is terminated and its connection closed.
Any open transaction on it is rolled back.

actions.title: Show running queries cluster-wide
actions.summary: Show in-flight queries across all nodes (the SHOW CLUSTER QUERIES surface) — query id, node, user, start time, client address, application, and the query text (truncated). Use to find a long-running or runaway query.
actions.description: Show in-flight queries across all nodes (the SHOW CLUSTER QUERIES surface) — query id, node, user, start time, client address, application, and the query text (truncated). Use to find a long-running or runaway query.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT query_id, node_id, user_name, start, client_address, application_name, left(query,200) AS query FROM crdb_internal.cluster_queries ORDER BY start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show open sessions cluster-wide
actions.summary: Show open SQL sessions across all nodes (the SHOW CLUSTER SESSIONS surface) — session id, node, user, client address, application, active queries, and session start. Use to see who is connected and from where.
actions.description: Show open SQL sessions across all nodes (the SHOW CLUSTER SESSIONS surface) — session id, node, user, client address, application, active queries, and session start. Use to see who is connected and from where.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT session_id, node_id, user_name, client_address, application_name, active_queries, session_start FROM crdb_internal.cluster_sessions ORDER BY session_start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show cluster settings
actions.summary: Show all cluster settings with their current value, type, and description (SHOW CLUSTER SETTINGS). Use to confirm a setting's value during an investigation. Read-only — this pack does not change settings.
actions.description: Show all cluster settings with their current value, type, and description (SHOW CLUSTER SETTINGS). Use to confirm a setting's value during an investigation. Read-only — this pack does not change settings.
side_effects: One read-only SHOW CLUSTER SETTINGS query.

actions.title: Show open transactions cluster-wide
actions.summary: Show open transactions across all nodes — id, node, session, start time, application, statement count, and retry count, from crdb_internal.cluster_transactions. Use to spot long-open or heavily-retried transactions.
actions.description: Show open transactions across all nodes — id, node, session, start time, application, statement count, and retry count, from crdb_internal.cluster_transactions. Use to spot long-open or heavily-retried transactions.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT id, node_id, session_id, start, application_name, num_stmts, num_retries FROM crdb_internal.cluster_transactions ORDER BY start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show who is blocking whom (contention events)
actions.summary: Show recent transaction-contention events — blocking vs waiting txn, how long the wait was, and the object (database / table / index) and key contended, from crdb_internal.transaction_contention_events. The core "what's blocking my writes" view.
actions.description: Show recent transaction-contention events — blocking vs waiting txn, how long the wait was, and the object (database / table / index) and key contended, from crdb_internal.transaction_contention_events. The core "what's blocking my writes" view.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT collection_ts, blocking_txn_id, waiting_txn_id, contention_duration, database_name, table_name, index_name, contending_pretty_key FROM crdb_internal.transaction_contention_events ORDER BY collection_ts DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show most-contended objects
actions.summary: Show the most-contended tables/indexes by cumulative contention time, from crdb_internal.cluster_contention_events. Use to find the hotspot behind widespread contention.
actions.description: Show the most-contended tables/indexes by cumulative contention time, from crdb_internal.cluster_contention_events. Use to find the hotspot behind widespread contention.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT table_id, index_id, num_contention_events, cumulative_contention_time FROM crdb_internal.cluster_contention_events ORDER BY cumulative_contention_time DESC LIMIT 50"

actions.title: List databases (SHOW DATABASES)
actions.summary: List the cluster's databases. Cheap connectivity + auth check, and the pack's verify action.
actions.description: List the cluster's databases. Cheap connectivity + auth check, and the pack's verify action.

actions.title: Show recent / running jobs
actions.summary: Show cluster jobs (schema changes, backups, restores, imports, row-TTL) from crdb_internal.jobs — id, type, status, running status, timing, fraction complete, description, and any error. Optionally filter by status. Use to find a stuck or failed job.
actions.description: Show cluster jobs (schema changes, backups, restores, imports, row-TTL) from crdb_internal.jobs — id, type, status, running status, timing, fraction complete, description, and any error. Optionally filter by status. Use to find a stuck or failed job.

args.name: status
args.description: Optional status filter (e.g. running, paused, failed); empty = all.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT job_id, job_type, status, running_status, created, finished, fraction_completed, left(coalesce(description,''),120) AS description, left(coalesce(error,''),200) AS error FROM crdb_internal.jobs WHERE ('{{ args.status }}' = '' OR status = '{{ args.status }}') ORDER BY created DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show contended locks (lock table)
actions.summary: Show contended entries in the lock table — range, object, the pretty key, holding txn, lock strength, and how long it's been held, from crdb_internal.cluster_locks. Use to find the lock behind a stall.
actions.description: Show contended entries in the lock table — range, object, the pretty key, holding txn, lock strength, and how long it's been held, from crdb_internal.cluster_locks. Use to find the lock behind a stall.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT range_id, database_name, table_name, index_name, lock_key_pretty, txn_id, lock_strength, granted, contended, duration FROM crdb_internal.cluster_locks WHERE contended ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 100"

actions.title: Decommission a node (cockroach node decommission)
actions.summary: Mark one node decommissioning and start moving its replicas off. Effectively irreversible once it completes — a fully decommissioned node cannot rejoin under the same id. Uses --wait=none, so the call returns after initiating; track progress with node_status.
actions.description: Mark one node decommissioning and start moving its replicas off. Effectively irreversible once it completes — a fully decommissioned node cannot rejoin under the same id. Uses --wait=none, so the call returns after initiating; track progress with node_status.
side_effects: The target node stops accepting new replicas and sheds its existing ones.
A fully decommissioned node cannot rejoin the cluster under the same id.

actions.title: Show node liveness / draining / decommissioning
actions.summary: Show per-node liveness from crdb_internal.gossip_liveness — epoch, draining, decommissioning, membership, and last update. Use to see which nodes are live and which are leaving the cluster.
actions.description: Show per-node liveness from crdb_internal.gossip_liveness — epoch, draining, decommissioning, membership, and last update. Use to see which nodes are live and which are leaving the cluster.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT node_id, epoch, draining, decommissioning, membership, updated_at FROM crdb_internal.gossip_liveness ORDER BY node_id"

actions.title: Recommission a node (cockroach node recommission)
actions.summary: Reverse an in-progress decommission so the node resumes accepting replicas. Only valid before the decommission completes. Use to abort a decommission started by mistake.
actions.description: Reverse an in-progress decommission so the node resumes accepting replicas. Only valid before the decommission completes. Use to abort a decommission started by mistake.

actions.title: Show node status (liveness, ranges, decommission, disk)
actions.summary: Show every node's status — address, build, liveness, replica/leaseholder counts, range counts (incl. unavailable / under-replicated), liveness bytes, and decommission / draining / membership state. The first stop for cluster health.
actions.description: Show every node's status — address, build, liveness, replica/leaseholder counts, range counts (incl. unavailable / under-replicated), liveness bytes, and decommission / draining / membership state. The first stop for cluster health.

actions.title: Show status for one node
actions.summary: Show full status for a single node by id — same columns as node_status, scoped to one node. Use to inspect a node flagged elsewhere.
actions.description: Show full status for a single node by id — same columns as node_status, scoped to one node. Use to inspect a node flagged elsewhere.

actions.title: Pause a job (PAUSE JOB)
actions.summary: Pause one running job by id (from jobs). It can be resumed later with resume_job. Use to relieve load from a heavy backup / schema change without losing its progress.
actions.description: Pause one running job by id (from jobs). It can be resumed later with resume_job. Use to relieve load from a heavy backup / schema change without losing its progress.

actions.title: Show replication constraint violations
actions.summary: Show zones whose ranges violate their replication constraints (e.g. a region/locality requirement that can't be met), from system.replication_constraint_stats. Empty means all constraints satisfied.
actions.description: Show zones whose ranges violate their replication constraints (e.g. a region/locality requirement that can't be met), from system.replication_constraint_stats. Empty means all constraints satisfied.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT zone_id, subzone_id, type, config, violating_ranges, violation_start FROM system.replication_constraint_stats WHERE violating_ranges > 0 ORDER BY violating_ranges DESC"

actions.title: Show under-replicated / unavailable range counts
actions.summary: Show cluster-wide range health — total, under-replicated, unavailable, and over-replicated range counts, summed from system.replication_stats. Any non-zero unavailable count means data is offline; under-replicated means recovery is in progress.
actions.description: Show cluster-wide range health — total, under-replicated, unavailable, and over-replicated range counts, summed from system.replication_stats. Any non-zero unavailable count means data is offline; under-replicated means recovery is in progress.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT coalesce(sum(total_ranges),0) AS total, coalesce(sum(under_replicated_ranges),0) AS under_replicated, coalesce(sum(unavailable_ranges),0) AS unavailable, coalesce(sum(over_replicated_ranges),0) AS over_replicated FROM system.replication_stats"

actions.title: Resume a paused job (RESUME JOB)
actions.summary: Resume one paused job by id (from jobs). Use to continue a job paused with pause_job.
actions.description: Resume one paused job by id (from jobs). Use to continue a job paused with pause_job.

actions.title: Set a cluster setting (SET CLUSTER SETTING)
actions.summary: Change one CockroachDB cluster setting cluster-wide — SET CLUSTER SETTING <name> = <value>. An unrestricted config write (rate limits, GC TTLs, feature flags) that can materially change cluster behavior, so risk:critical → default-denied. An operator allows it by explicit policy, and WHICH settings/values are permitted is a portal policy decision, not a hardcoded list. Read cockroach.cluster_settings first to see the current value.
actions.description: Change one CockroachDB cluster setting cluster-wide — SET CLUSTER SETTING <name> = <value>. An unrestricted config write (rate limits, GC TTLs, feature flags) that can materially change cluster behavior, so risk:critical → default-denied. An operator allows it by explicit policy, and WHICH settings/values are permitted is a portal policy decision, not a hardcoded list. Read cockroach.cluster_settings first to see the current value.
side_effects: The named cluster setting is changed for the WHOLE cluster, immediately.
Rate-limit / GC / admission settings can throttle or destabilize the cluster.

args.name: name
args.description: Cluster setting name, e.g. kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate (see cockroach.cluster_settings).

args.name: value
args.description: The value in CockroachDB literal form — a bare number/boolean (100, true, DEFAULT) or a quoted string ('64 MiB', '24h'). Passed verbatim into the statement; bounded so it can't break out of its slot.

examples.title: Reset a setting to its default

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING {{ args.name }} = {{ args.value }}"

actions.title: Show top statement fingerprints by exec count
actions.summary: Show the busiest statement fingerprints — application, execution count, mean service latency, and the query text — from crdb_internal.statement_statistics (the data behind the DB Console's Statements page). Use to find the highest-volume or slowest statements.
actions.description: Show the busiest statement fingerprints — application, execution count, mean service latency, and the query text — from crdb_internal.statement_statistics (the data behind the DB Console's Statements page). Use to find the highest-volume or slowest statements.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT app_name, (statistics->'statistics'->>'cnt')::INT AS count, (statistics->'statistics'->'svcLat'->>'mean')::FLOAT AS mean_svc_lat_s, left(metadata->>'query',200) AS query FROM crdb_internal.statement_statistics ORDER BY (statistics->'statistics'->>'cnt')::INT DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show per-store disk capacity / range counts
actions.summary: Show each store's capacity, available and used bytes, range and lease counts, and writes/sec from crdb_internal.kv_store_status. Use to spot a node running low on disk or carrying too many ranges.
actions.description: Show each store's capacity, available and used bytes, range and lease counts, and writes/sec from crdb_internal.kv_store_status. Use to spot a node running low on disk or carrying too many ranges.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT node_id, store_id, capacity, available, used, logical_bytes, range_count, lease_count, writes_per_second FROM crdb_internal.kv_store_status ORDER BY node_id, store_id"

actions.title: Show range count + size for one table
actions.summary: Show the ranges backing one table — range id, leaseholder, size in MB, and replica nodes — via SHOW RANGES ... WITH DETAILS. Use to find a table's data size and how its ranges are spread across nodes.
actions.description: Show the ranges backing one table — range id, leaseholder, size in MB, and replica nodes — via SHOW RANGES ... WITH DETAILS. Use to find a table's data size and how its ranges are spread across nodes.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT range_id, lease_holder, range_size_mb, replicas FROM [SHOW RANGES FROM TABLE {{ args.database }}.{{ args.table }} WITH DETAILS]"

actions.title: Show estimated row counts per table
actions.summary: Show estimated row counts for every table from crdb_internal.table_row_statistics, largest first. Cheap (uses table statistics, not a COUNT). Use to find the biggest tables.
actions.description: Show estimated row counts for every table from crdb_internal.table_row_statistics, largest first. Cheap (uses table statistics, not a COUNT). Use to find the biggest tables.

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT table_id, table_name, estimated_row_count FROM crdb_internal.table_row_statistics ORDER BY estimated_row_count DESC LIMIT 200"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT query_id, node_id, user_name, start, client_address, application_name, left(query,200) AS query FROM crdb_internal.cluster_queries ORDER BY start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT session_id, node_id, user_name, client_address, application_name, active_queries, session_start FROM crdb_internal.cluster_sessions ORDER BY session_start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT id, node_id, session_id, start, application_name, num_stmts, num_retries FROM crdb_internal.cluster_transactions ORDER BY start ASC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show who is blocking whom (contention events)

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT collection_ts, blocking_txn_id, waiting_txn_id, contention_duration, database_name, table_name, index_name, contending_pretty_key FROM crdb_internal.transaction_contention_events ORDER BY collection_ts DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT table_id, index_id, num_contention_events, cumulative_contention_time FROM crdb_internal.cluster_contention_events ORDER BY cumulative_contention_time DESC LIMIT 50"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT job_id, job_type, status, running_status, created, finished, fraction_completed, left(coalesce(description,''),120) AS description, left(coalesce(error,''),200) AS error FROM crdb_internal.jobs WHERE ('{{ args.status }}' = '' OR status = '{{ args.status }}') ORDER BY created DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT range_id, database_name, table_name, index_name, lock_key_pretty, txn_id, lock_strength, granted, contended, duration FROM crdb_internal.cluster_locks WHERE contended ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 100"
cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT node_id, epoch, draining, decommissioning, membership, updated_at FROM crdb_internal.gossip_liveness ORDER BY node_id"

actions.title: Show node status (liveness, ranges, decommission, disk)

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT zone_id, subzone_id, type, config, violating_ranges, violation_start FROM system.replication_constraint_stats WHERE violating_ranges > 0 ORDER BY violating_ranges DESC"

cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT coalesce(sum(total_ranges),0) AS total, coalesce(sum(under_replicated_ranges),0) AS under_replicated, coalesce(sum(unavailable_ranges),0) AS unavailable, coalesce(sum(over_replicated_ranges),0) AS over_replicated FROM system.replication_stats"

actions.title: Set a cluster setting (SET CLUSTER SETTING)

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING {{ args.name }} = {{ args.value }}"

actions.title: Show top statement fingerprints by exec count

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT app_name, (statistics->'statistics'->>'cnt')::INT AS count, (statistics->'statistics'->'svcLat'->>'mean')::FLOAT AS mean_svc_lat_s, left(metadata->>'query',200) AS query FROM crdb_internal.statement_statistics ORDER BY (statistics->'statistics'->>'cnt')::INT DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }}"

actions.title: Show per-store disk capacity / range counts

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT node_id, store_id, capacity, available, used, logical_bytes, range_count, lease_count, writes_per_second FROM crdb_internal.kv_store_status ORDER BY node_id, store_id"

actions.title: Show range count + size for one table

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT range_id, lease_holder, range_size_mb, replicas FROM [SHOW RANGES FROM TABLE {{ args.database }}.{{ args.table }} WITH DETAILS]"

actions.title: Show estimated row counts per table

argv: cockroach sql --url "$COCKROACH_URL" --format=tsv -e "SELECT table_id, table_name, estimated_row_count FROM crdb_internal.table_row_statistics ORDER BY estimated_row_count DESC LIMIT 200"

packs.name: HashiCorp Consul operations
packs.description: Deep Consul ops — agent self/metrics/host introspection, operator raft + autopilot + reload, catalog (services + nodes + datacenters), health (passing/warning/critical) with force-pass/fail/warn check mutators, KV (get/list/recursive), ACL tokens/policies/roles, Connect mesh (CA roots, intentions), sessions, prepared queries, snapshots (save/inspect/restore), and narrow operator actions (deregister, maintenance, raft remove-peer). Auth via CONSUL_HTTP_ADDR + CONSUL_HTTP_TOKEN env vars.

actions.title: GET /v1/acl/token/self
actions.summary: Show metadata on the runner's own token — accessor, policies, roles, expiration. The SecretID the API returns is redacted from the output.
actions.description: Show metadata on the runner's own token — accessor, policies, roles, expiration. The SecretID the API returns is redacted from the output.
side_effects: Read-only; the SecretID field returned by the API is redacted before output.

actions.title: GET /v1/agent/checks
actions.summary: List checks registered with the local agent + their current status.
actions.description: List checks registered with the local agent + their current status.

actions.title: GET /v1/agent/host
actions.summary: Show host info: OS, CPU, memory, filesystem, network from the agent's view.
actions.description: Show host info: OS, CPU, memory, filesystem, network from the agent's view.

actions.title: GET /v1/agent/metrics
actions.summary: Show current runtime metrics gauges + counters.
actions.description: Show current runtime metrics gauges + counters.

actions.title: GET /v1/agent/self
actions.summary: Show this agent's effective config, runtime, member, and ACL state.
actions.description: Show this agent's effective config, runtime, member, and ACL state.

actions.title: GET /v1/agent/services
actions.summary: List the services registered with the local agent.
actions.description: List the services registered with the local agent.

actions.title: GET /v1/operator/autopilot/state
actions.summary: Show the Autopilot view of cluster health: server stabilization, leader, failure tolerance.
actions.description: Show the Autopilot view of cluster health: server stabilization, leader, failure tolerance.

actions.title: GET /v1/catalog/datacenters
actions.summary: List all WAN-federated datacenters known to this server.
actions.description: List all WAN-federated datacenters known to this server.

actions.title: GET /v1/catalog/service/<name>
actions.summary: List all instances of one service across the cluster.
actions.description: List all instances of one service across the cluster.

actions.title: GET /v1/connect/ca/configuration
actions.summary: Show CA provider configuration (which CA, intermediate cert TTL, etc). Provider secrets the config map may carry — the Vault provider's Token and the built-in provider's PrivateKey — are redacted from the output.
actions.description: Show CA provider configuration (which CA, intermediate cert TTL, etc). Provider secrets the config map may carry — the Vault provider's Token and the built-in provider's PrivateKey — are redacted from the output.
side_effects: Read-only; provider secret fields (Token, PrivateKey) are redacted before output.

actions.title: GET /v1/connect/ca/roots
actions.summary: List currently-trusted root CAs for Connect mesh TLS.
actions.description: List currently-trusted root CAs for Connect mesh TLS.

actions.title: consul services deregister
actions.summary: Removes one service registration from this agent.
actions.description: Removes one service registration from this agent.
side_effects: Service immediately disappears from discovery on this node.

actions.title: PUT /v1/session/destroy/<id>
actions.summary: Destroys one session. Any locks held are released; KV entries with release behavior are unlocked.
actions.description: Destroys one session. Any locks held are released; KV entries with release behavior are unlocked.
side_effects: Held KV locks released (or keys deleted, if behavior=delete).
Distributed lock holders may need to handle the loss.

actions.title: PUT /v1/agent/check/fail/<check_id>
actions.summary: Forces one check into CRITICAL state. Service discovery stops returning it.
actions.description: Forces one check into CRITICAL state. Service discovery stops returning it.
side_effects: Discovery routes traffic away from the associated service.

actions.title: PUT /v1/agent/check/pass/<check_id>
actions.summary: Forces one TTL check into the PASSING state. The check stays passing until the next TTL expires.
actions.description: Forces one TTL check into the PASSING state. The check stays passing until the next TTL expires.
side_effects: Services watching this check may resume routing traffic.
For non-TTL checks, the override is overwritten by the next actual run.

actions.title: PUT /v1/agent/check/warn/<check_id>
actions.summary: Forces one check into WARNING state.
actions.description: Forces one check into WARNING state.
side_effects: Strict service discovery (passing-only) routes away from it.

actions.title: GET /v1/connect/intentions
actions.summary: List all Connect mesh intentions (allow/deny rules between services).
actions.description: List all Connect mesh intentions (allow/deny rules between services).

actions.title: GET /v1/connect/intentions/match (by destination)
actions.summary: List all intentions whose destination is the named service. Use to answer "what can talk to X?".
actions.description: List all intentions whose destination is the named service. Use to answer "what can talk to X?".

actions.title: consul kv get <key>
actions.summary: Get the value at one KV key.
actions.description: Get the value at one KV key.
side_effects: Read-only — value will be returned, may be redacted by audit rules.

actions.title: consul kv get -recurse <prefix>
actions.summary: Get all keys + values under a prefix.
actions.description: Get all keys + values under a prefix.
side_effects: Read-only — values may include secrets; rely on audit redaction.

actions.title: consul kv get -keys <prefix>
actions.summary: List all KV keys under one prefix.
actions.description: List all KV keys under one prefix.

actions.title: GET /v1/status/leader
actions.summary: Show the current Raft leader address for this datacenter. Reads the status endpoint, which is not ACL-gated, so it answers "is there a leader?" even when no CONSUL_HTTP_TOKEN is set.
actions.description: Show the current Raft leader address for this datacenter. Reads the status endpoint, which is not ACL-gated, so it answers "is there a leader?" even when no CONSUL_HTTP_TOKEN is set.

actions.title: consul acl policy list
actions.summary: List all ACL policies.
actions.description: List all ACL policies.

actions.title: consul acl role list
actions.summary: List all ACL roles (groups of policies).
actions.description: List all ACL roles (groups of policies).

actions.title: consul acl token list
actions.summary: List ACL tokens (descriptions + accessor IDs only, not secrets).
actions.description: List ACL tokens (descriptions + accessor IDs only, not secrets).

actions.title: GET /v1/health/state/critical
actions.summary: List every check currently in CRITICAL state across the catalog.
actions.description: List every check currently in CRITICAL state across the catalog.

actions.title: GET /v1/health/state/warning
actions.summary: List every check currently in WARNING state.
actions.description: List every check currently in WARNING state.

actions.title: consul catalog nodes
actions.summary: List all registered nodes.
actions.description: List all registered nodes.

actions.title: consul catalog services
actions.summary: List all registered service names.
actions.description: List all registered service names.

actions.title: GET /v1/session/list
actions.summary: List active sessions cluster-wide: ID, node, TTL, behavior.
actions.description: List active sessions cluster-wide: ID, node, TTL, behavior.

actions.title: consul members
actions.summary: List all agents in the gossip pool with status, role, version.
actions.description: List all agents in the gossip pool with status, role, version.

actions.title: GET /v1/health/node/<node>
actions.summary: List all checks for one node + their status.
actions.description: List all checks for one node + their status.

actions.title: consul maint -enable
actions.summary: Enables maintenance mode on this agent's node. Health checks fail until disabled.
actions.description: Enables maintenance mode on this agent's node. Health checks fail until disabled.
side_effects: All node service health checks report critical.
Service discovery routes traffic away from this node.

args.name: note
args.description: Maintenance reason recorded in the health check.

actions.title: GET /v1/catalog/node-services/<node>
actions.summary: List all services registered against one node.
actions.description: List all services registered against one node.

actions.title: GET /v1/query
actions.summary: List all defined prepared queries (named service-discovery templates with failover).
actions.description: List all defined prepared queries (named service-discovery templates with failover).

actions.title: consul operator raft list-peers
actions.summary: List the server peers with voter status, suffix, address.
actions.description: List the server peers with voter status, suffix, address.

actions.title: consul operator raft remove-peer
actions.summary: Forcibly removes a server from Raft. Use ONLY when a server is permanently gone and autopilot hasn't cleaned it up.
actions.description: Forcibly removes a server from Raft. Use ONLY when a server is permanently gone and autopilot hasn't cleaned it up.
side_effects: Wrong target = lost quorum / split brain.

actions.title: consul reload
actions.summary: Reloads the local agent's config (re-reads HCL files). Some settings can't be reloaded — see consul docs.
actions.description: Reloads the local agent's config (re-reads HCL files). Some settings can't be reloaded — see consul docs.
side_effects: Bind address, encryption keys, etc., remain at boot values.

actions.title: Health of a service's instances
actions.summary: Show per-node health for one service.
actions.description: Show per-node health for one service.

actions.title: consul maint -enable -service <svc>
actions.summary: Puts one local-agent service into maintenance mode. Its checks report critical until disabled.
actions.description: Puts one local-agent service into maintenance mode. Its checks report critical until disabled.

actions.title: GET /v1/health/service/<name>?passing
actions.summary: List only healthy (all-passing) instances of one service. What service discovery would return.
actions.description: List only healthy (all-passing) instances of one service. What service discovery would return.

actions.title: consul snapshot inspect <path>
actions.summary: Show a summary of one snapshot file — size, index, KV count, ACL count.
actions.description: Show a summary of one snapshot file — size, index, KV count, ACL count.

actions.title: consul snapshot restore <path>
actions.summary: Restores cluster state from a snapshot. ALL existing state (KV, services, sessions, intentions, ACL) is REPLACED. Cluster briefly unavailable during restore.
actions.description: Restores cluster state from a snapshot. ALL existing state (KV, services, sessions, intentions, ACL) is REPLACED. Cluster briefly unavailable during restore.
side_effects: All current Raft state is replaced.
KV, services, sessions, ACLs, intentions all swap to snapshot contents.
Cannot be undone without another snapshot.

actions.title: consul snapshot save <path>
actions.summary: Writes a Raft snapshot to a local file. Use before risky operations + as a backup.
actions.description: Writes a Raft snapshot to a local file. Use before risky operations + as a backup.
side_effects: One read of the entire state store.
File written at the configured path on the runner host.
Brief leader IO; no service impact.

packs.name: Debian/Ubuntu package operations
packs.description: Operator pack for Debian/Ubuntu hosts. Read-only inventory and patching diagnostics, plus narrow apt install/remove actions for a single named package. Designed for production hosts where a human approver must see the package name before a write happens.

actions.title: apt autoremove
actions.summary: Drops packages that were installed as dependencies and are no longer required. Run after a series of removes. Considered high-risk because old kernels and headers are exactly the things autoremove cleans up — verify the running kernel is not on the removal list before approving.
actions.description: Drops packages that were installed as dependencies and are no longer required. Run after a series of removes. Considered high-risk because old kernels and headers are exactly the things autoremove cleans up — verify the running kernel is not on the removal list before approving.
side_effects: Removes possibly many packages, including old kernels.

actions.title: apt install (one package)
actions.summary: Installs ONE named package non-interactively. Args are restricted to one package — multi-package installs and arbitrary apt flags are intentionally not supported. Verify with `dpkg_status` afterward. Holds the apt lock for the duration; concurrent dpkg/apt operations will queue.
actions.description: Installs ONE named package non-interactively. Args are restricted to one package — multi-package installs and arbitrary apt flags are intentionally not supported. Verify with `dpkg_status` afterward. Holds the apt lock for the duration; concurrent dpkg/apt operations will queue.

actions.title: apt remove (one package)
actions.summary: Removes ONE named package non-interactively. Config files are kept (apt remove, not purge). For data-bearing packages this still drops the binaries — confirm via `dpkg_status` first that you are not removing a service still in use. Holds the apt lock.
actions.description: Removes ONE named package non-interactively. Config files are kept (apt remove, not purge). For data-bearing packages this still drops the binaries — confirm via `dpkg_status` first that you are not removing a service still in use. Holds the apt lock.

actions.title: Pending security upgrades
actions.summary: Lists upgradable packages whose origin matches the security archive (`-security`). Read-only. Use as a one-shot patch-posture check. Requires `unattended-upgrades` or at minimum the security source enabled in /etc/apt/sources.list.
actions.description: Lists upgradable packages whose origin matches the security archive (`-security`). Read-only. Use as a one-shot patch-posture check. Requires `unattended-upgrades` or at minimum the security source enabled in /etc/apt/sources.list.
side_effects: Briefly takes a shared apt lock.

argv: apt list --upgradable 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\-security|security\.(debian|ubuntu)' || echo 'no pending security upgrades'

actions.title: apt-get update
actions.summary: Refreshes the apt package index from configured sources. Does not upgrade anything. Network-bound, may fail on a host behind a restrictive egress filter. Counts as medium-risk because it touches /var/lib/apt and a stale index can confuse later patching.
actions.description: Refreshes the apt package index from configured sources. Does not upgrade anything. Network-bound, may fail on a host behind a restrictive egress filter. Counts as medium-risk because it touches /var/lib/apt and a stale index can confuse later patching.

actions.title: List upgradable packages
actions.summary: Lists packages that have an upgrade available based on the current apt index. Read-only — does NOT refresh the index. Pair with `apt_update` if you suspect the cache is stale.
actions.description: Lists packages that have an upgrade available based on the current apt index. Read-only — does NOT refresh the index. Pair with `apt_update` if you suspect the cache is stale.
side_effects: Briefly takes a shared apt lock.

actions.title: Recent dpkg installs/removes
actions.summary: Show recent dpkg install / remove / upgrade activity across /var/log/dpkg.log and its rotated logs (.1 and .*.gz), returned in time order. Use to answer "what was changed on this host recently?" before deeper forensics. Read-only.
actions.description: Show recent dpkg install / remove / upgrade activity across /var/log/dpkg.log and its rotated logs (.1 and .*.gz), returned in time order. Use to answer "what was changed on this host recently?" before deeper forensics. Read-only.

actions.title: dpkg package status
actions.summary: Returns the dpkg status (installed version, architecture, depends, maintainer) for one named package. Read-only. The package name is pattern-restricted to safe Debian package syntax.
actions.description: Returns the dpkg status (installed version, architecture, depends, maintainer) for one named package. Read-only. The package name is pattern-restricted to safe Debian package syntax.

examples.title: Is openssl installed and what version?

actions.title: Kernel + uptime + reboot-required
actions.summary: Show kernel version (`uname -a`), uptime, and whether the host has /var/run/reboot-required (set by some packages — most often after a libc or kernel upgrade). Read-only. Use to plan a reboot window.
actions.description: Show kernel version (`uname -a`), uptime, and whether the host has /var/run/reboot-required (set by some packages — most often after a libc or kernel upgrade). Read-only. Use to plan a reboot window.
side_effects: One uname call, one uptime call, stat on /var/run/reboot-required.

argv: uname -a; uptime; if [ -f /var/run/reboot-required ]; then echo 'reboot-required: yes'; cat /var/run/reboot-required.pkgs 2>/dev/null || true; else echo 'reboot-required: no'; fi

packs.name: Linux debugging toolkit
packs.description: General-purpose Linux diagnostics + low-level remediation: process and memory tops, vmstat/iostat snapshots, socket inventories, per-PID inspection, kernel-state checks, network reachability, plus fix-it actions (drop_caches, kill_pid by signal, sysctl_set). Use as the first-touch pack when something is wrong.

actions.title: df + mounts
actions.summary: Returns `df -hT` output. Filesystem type, size, used, avail, and mountpoint for every mounted filesystem. Use as the first check when a write fails with ENOSPC or when /var/log has gone dark.
actions.description: Returns `df -hT` output. Filesystem type, size, used, avail, and mountpoint for every mounted filesystem. Use as the first check when a write fails with ENOSPC or when /var/log has gone dark.

actions.title: OOM-kill events from dmesg
actions.summary: Filters dmesg for OOM-killer events. Returns the kernel log lines showing process id, name, RSS, and OOM score for every killed process. The "why did mysqld vanish?" answer. Falls back to `journalctl -k` when dmesg is not permitted (needs CAP_SYSLOG / root, or journal read access via systemd-journal / adm). Read-only.
actions.description: Filters dmesg for OOM-killer events. Returns the kernel log lines showing process id, name, RSS, and OOM score for every killed process. The "why did mysqld vanish?" answer. Falls back to `journalctl -k` when dmesg is not permitted (needs CAP_SYSLOG / root, or journal read access via systemd-journal / adm). Read-only.

argv: { dmesg -T 2>/dev/null || journalctl -k --no-pager; } | grep -i -E 'oom|killed process|out of memory' | tail -30

actions.title: Recent kernel messages
actions.summary: Returns the last N kernel log lines. Surfaces OOM kills, hardware errors, network link flaps, and dropped packets. Reads the kernel ring buffer via dmesg; when that is not permitted (modern kernels gate it behind CAP_SYSLOG) it falls back to `journalctl -k`, which works when the runner can read the journal (root, or a member of systemd-journal / adm). Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the last N kernel log lines. Surfaces OOM kills, hardware errors, network link flaps, and dropped packets. Reads the kernel ring buffer via dmesg; when that is not permitted (modern kernels gate it behind CAP_SYSLOG) it falls back to `journalctl -k`, which works when the runner can read the journal (root, or a member of systemd-journal / adm). Read-only.

actions.title: echo <n> > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
actions.summary: Force the kernel to drop pagecache / dentries / inodes. Use only when a benchmark or memory-fragmentation test requires a cold cache — never on prod for "I want more free RAM". Production RSS appears to drop briefly, then the cache repopulates and the next workload is slower until it warms back up.
actions.description: Force the kernel to drop pagecache / dentries / inodes. Use only when a benchmark or memory-fragmentation test requires a cold cache — never on prod for "I want more free RAM". Production RSS appears to drop briefly, then the cache repopulates and the next workload is slower until it warms back up.
side_effects: Page cache emptied (1), or slab caches emptied (2), or both (3).
Brief I/O spike as caches repopulate.
Production read latency increases until warm.

actions.title: iostat per-device sample
actions.summary: Runs `iostat -xz 1 N` to capture N one-second samples of extended per-device statistics. Surfaces await, %util, queue depth. Use to prove or disprove "the disk is slow" before chasing application code.
actions.description: Runs `iostat -xz 1 N` to capture N one-second samples of extended per-device statistics. Surfaces await, %util, queue depth. Use to prove or disprove "the disk is slow" before chasing application code.
side_effects: One iostat invocation lasting N seconds.

args.name: samples
args.description: How many one-second samples to collect.

actions.title: Kernel taint state
actions.summary: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/tainted. A non-zero value means a binary module, a proprietary driver, or a kernel crash has compromised the integrity of the running kernel. The number is a bitmask; this action returns both the raw value and the decoded flags.
actions.description: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/tainted. A non-zero value means a binary module, a proprietary driver, or a kernel crash has compromised the integrity of the running kernel. The number is a bitmask; this action returns both the raw value and the decoded flags.

argv: echo "tainted: $(cat /proc/sys/kernel/tainted)"; cat /usr/share/doc/linux-doc/admin-guide/tainted-kernels.rst 2>/dev/null | head -50 || echo 'flags doc not installed'

actions.title: kill -<signal> <pid>
actions.summary: Send a signal to one process by PID. SIGTERM is the polite default — the process gets a chance to flush state. SIGKILL is unrecoverable — use only when SIGTERM is ignored. Watch for PID reuse: confirm the target via `pid_status` first.
actions.description: Send a signal to one process by PID. SIGTERM is the polite default — the process gets a chance to flush state. SIGKILL is unrecoverable — use only when SIGTERM is ignored. Watch for PID reuse: confirm the target via `pid_status` first.
side_effects: SIGKILL terminates immediately; open files closed by kernel; pid may be reused.

actions.title: Load + memory + uptime snapshot
actions.summary: Reads /proc/loadavg, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/uptime to produce a one-shot system snapshot. Cheap. Use as the very first check when triaging a host alert.
actions.description: Reads /proc/loadavg, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/uptime to produce a one-shot system snapshot. Cheap. Use as the very first check when triaging a host alert.

actions.title: Who owns a TCP port?
actions.summary: Returns the PID/process that has a given TCP port open (listening or connected), via `ss -tnp` (not lsof, despite the name). Use to answer "EADDRINUSE: who's on 8080?" or to confirm a stuck connection to an upstream. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the PID/process that has a given TCP port open (listening or connected), via `ss -tnp` (not lsof, despite the name). Use to answer "EADDRINUSE: who's on 8080?" or to confirm a stuck connection to an upstream. Read-only.

actions.title: Top processes by RSS
actions.summary: Returns the top N processes sorted by resident-set size (RSS). Use when /proc/meminfo or `free` shows pressure and you need the offender. RSS does not double-count shared pages, so a "leak" candidate showing high RSS is worth investigating.
actions.description: Returns the top N processes sorted by resident-set size (RSS). Use when /proc/meminfo or `free` shows pressure and you need the offender. RSS does not double-count shared pages, so a "leak" candidate showing high RSS is worth investigating.

actions.title: Established connection summary
actions.summary: Returns counts of TCP connections grouped by remote peer + state. Useful for spotting connection storms (single host) or TIME_WAIT pressure. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns counts of TCP connections grouped by remote peer + state. Useful for spotting connection storms (single host) or TIME_WAIT pressure. Read-only.

args.name: limit
args.description: How many peer/state groups to return.

argv: ss -ant | awk 'NR>1 {print $1, $5}' | sed 's/:[0-9]*$//' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -n {{ args.limit }}

actions.title: Listening sockets
actions.summary: Returns TCP and UDP listening sockets with the owning process (`ss -tulnp`). Use to confirm whether an expected daemon is actually bound and on which interfaces. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns TCP and UDP listening sockets with the owning process (`ss -tulnp`). Use to confirm whether an expected daemon is actually bound and on which interfaces. Read-only.

actions.title: Process cwd + exe
actions.summary: Returns the working directory and executable path of a PID. Use before drawing conclusions from a process name — `nginx` could be any of several binaries depending on PATH order.
actions.description: Returns the working directory and executable path of a PID. Use before drawing conclusions from a process name — `nginx` could be any of several binaries depending on PATH order.

argv: echo cwd=$(readlink /proc/{{ args.pid }}/cwd); echo exe=$(readlink /proc/{{ args.pid }}/exe)

actions.title: Process environment
actions.summary: Show the full set of environment variables a PID was started with — reads /proc/<pid>/environ and turns NULs into newlines. This deliberately surfaces the process's entire environment, which commonly carries injected secrets (DB URLs, API keys, cloud credentials); scope it by policy and prefer pid_status / pid_limits when you don't need the values. The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
actions.description: Show the full set of environment variables a PID was started with — reads /proc/<pid>/environ and turns NULs into newlines. This deliberately surfaces the process's entire environment, which commonly carries injected secrets (DB URLs, API keys, cloud credentials); scope it by policy and prefer pid_status / pid_limits when you don't need the values. The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
side_effects: Reads /proc/<pid>/environ — requires runner uid to match or root.
Read-only, but exposes the process's full environment (may include secrets).

actions.title: Process open file descriptors
actions.summary: Lists what each FD in /proc/<pid>/fd points at. Sockets show up as `socket:[N]`, files as their path. Use to spot FD-leak candidates or confirm a daemon has the log file you expect.
actions.description: Lists what each FD in /proc/<pid>/fd points at. Sockets show up as `socket:[N]`, files as their path. Use to spot FD-leak candidates or confirm a daemon has the log file you expect.

actions.title: Process I/O accounting
actions.summary: Reads /proc/<pid>/io — bytes read/written (logical + physical), syscall counts, cancelled writes. Use to find which process is driving disk I/O. Pair with `debugging.iostat` for the disk side.
actions.description: Reads /proc/<pid>/io — bytes read/written (logical + physical), syscall counts, cancelled writes. Use to find which process is driving disk I/O. Pair with `debugging.iostat` for the disk side.

actions.title: Resource limits applied to a PID
actions.summary: Reads /proc/<pid>/limits — every ulimit applied (open files, stack size, NPROC, memlock, msgqueue, niceness). Use to confirm whether a process is actually getting the higher limits its service file requested.
actions.description: Reads /proc/<pid>/limits — every ulimit applied (open files, stack size, NPROC, memlock, msgqueue, niceness). Use to confirm whether a process is actually getting the higher limits its service file requested.

actions.title: Process kernel stack
actions.summary: Reads /proc/<pid>/stack — the current kernel-side stack trace. Use to find what syscall a stuck process is hung in (futex, read, write, lock_kernel). Needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN or root to read.
actions.description: Reads /proc/<pid>/stack — the current kernel-side stack trace. Use to find what syscall a stuck process is hung in (futex, read, write, lock_kernel). Needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN or root to read.

actions.title: Process /proc status block
actions.summary: Dumps /proc/<pid>/status — capabilities, uid/gid, signal masks, RSS, peak RSS, voluntary/involuntary context switches, OOM score. More detail than `ps` for one PID. Read-only.
actions.description: Dumps /proc/<pid>/status — capabilities, uid/gid, signal masks, RSS, peak RSS, voluntary/involuntary context switches, OOM score. More detail than `ps` for one PID. Read-only.

actions.title: Per-thread stats for one PID
actions.summary: Lists every thread of one PID with CPU%, policy, priority, comm name. Use when one PID's CPU is high but it's unclear which thread inside it is hot. Read-only.
actions.description: Lists every thread of one PID with CPU%, policy, priority, comm name. Use when one PID's CPU is high but it's unclear which thread inside it is hot. Read-only.

actions.title: Ping a host
actions.summary: Sends N ICMP echo requests to a target. Use to confirm L3 reachability when a higher-layer probe (TCP/HTTP) fails. Target is pattern-restricted to safe hostname/IPv4 characters to prevent argument injection.
actions.description: Sends N ICMP echo requests to a target. Use to confirm L3 reachability when a higher-layer probe (TCP/HTTP) fails. Target is pattern-restricted to safe hostname/IPv4 characters to prevent argument injection.
side_effects: One ping process running for ~N seconds.

actions.title: Top processes by CPU
actions.summary: Returns the top N processes sorted by CPU%. Standard `ps` output — pid, user, %cpu, %mem, rss, command. Use as a first-touch check before going deeper with per-PID inspection.
actions.description: Returns the top N processes sorted by CPU%. Standard `ps` output — pid, user, %cpu, %mem, rss, command. Use as a first-touch check before going deeper with per-PID inspection.

actions.title: sar 3-sample CPU snapshot
actions.summary: `sar -u 5 3` — three 5-second CPU utilization samples. Surfaces user/system/iowait/steal/idle split with statistical smoothing. Use when `vmstat`'s noise hides the signal. Requires the sysstat package.
actions.description: `sar -u 5 3` — three 5-second CPU utilization samples. Surfaces user/system/iowait/steal/idle split with statistical smoothing. Use when `vmstat`'s noise hides the signal. Requires the sysstat package.

actions.title: Kernel slab cache top consumers
actions.summary: `slabtop -o -s c | head -40` — top 40 kernel slab caches by cache size. Use when /proc/meminfo shows high `Slab` but no userland process accounts for the memory. dentry / inode pressure is the usual answer.
actions.description: `slabtop -o -s c | head -40` — top 40 kernel slab caches by cache size. Use when /proc/meminfo shows high `Slab` but no userland process accounts for the memory. dentry / inode pressure is the usual answer.

actions.title: Socket counts by family + state
actions.summary: `ss -s` — aggregate counts: TCP/UDP/raw/frag, timewait, by state. Cheaper than the per-connection enumeration. Use as a one-shot "are we close to a port-tuple exhaustion?" check.
actions.description: `ss -s` — aggregate counts: TCP/UDP/raw/frag, timewait, by state. Cheaper than the per-connection enumeration. Use as a one-shot "are we close to a port-tuple exhaustion?" check.

actions.title: Swap usage summary
actions.summary: `swapon --show` plus the per-process swap usage (top 20). Read-only. Use to spot a host actively swapping — swap-in I/O is the slowest userland-visible memory tier.
actions.description: `swapon --show` plus the per-process swap usage (top 20). Read-only. Use to spot a host actively swapping — swap-in I/O is the slowest userland-visible memory tier.

argv: swapon --show; echo; cat /proc/swaps; echo; for f in /proc/[0-9]*/status; do awk '/VmSwap|Name/{printf "%s %s ",$2,$3}END{print ""}' "$f" 2>/dev/null; done | sort -k 2 -n -r | head -20

actions.title: sysctl -w <key>=<value>
actions.summary: Change a runtime kernel parameter. Change is not persistent — reverts at next boot unless mirrored in /etc/sysctl.d/. Wrong values can crash the kernel (net.* tunables especially). Read the current value first.
actions.description: Change a runtime kernel parameter. Change is not persistent — reverts at next boot unless mirrored in /etc/sysctl.d/. Wrong values can crash the kernel (net.* tunables especially). Read the current value first.
side_effects: Kernel tunable updated for current boot.
Effect varies — net buffers, vm overcommit, kernel.panic, etc.

actions.title: TCP connections with retransmits
actions.summary: `ss -i state established` filtered to flows showing retrans counters. Use when network latency is high — surfaces which peers are seeing TCP loss without a tcpdump. Read-only.
actions.description: `ss -i state established` filtered to flows showing retrans counters. Use when network latency is high — surfaces which peers are seeing TCP loss without a tcpdump. Read-only.

argv: ss -tnpi state established | awk '/retrans/{print}' | sort -k 5 | head -50

actions.title: TCP state counts
actions.summary: Returns the count of TCP sockets in each state (ESTAB, TIME-WAIT, CLOSE-WAIT, FIN-WAIT-*, SYN-*). High CLOSE-WAIT usually means the application isn't close()ing; high TIME-WAIT means short-lived client connections. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the count of TCP sockets in each state (ESTAB, TIME-WAIT, CLOSE-WAIT, FIN-WAIT-*, SYN-*). High CLOSE-WAIT usually means the application isn't close()ing; high TIME-WAIT means short-lived client connections. Read-only.

actions.title: Top processes by open-file count
actions.summary: Aggregates `lsof` by PID and returns the top 20 PIDs holding the most file descriptors. Use to find FD-leak candidates before EMFILE breaks something. Read-only.
actions.description: Aggregates `lsof` by PID and returns the top 20 PIDs holding the most file descriptors. Use to find FD-leak candidates before EMFILE breaks something. Read-only.
side_effects: Read-only; may be slow on large systems.

argv: lsof -F p 2>/dev/null | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

actions.title: vmstat sample
actions.summary: Runs `vmstat 1 N` to capture N one-second samples. Shows run/block queues, free memory, swap pressure, context switches, and per-CPU user/system/iowait. Use to spot a CPU-bound vs IO-bound vs context-switch-storm problem.
actions.description: Runs `vmstat 1 N` to capture N one-second samples. Shows run/block queues, free memory, swap pressure, context switches, and per-CPU user/system/iowait. Use to spot a CPU-bound vs IO-bound vs context-switch-storm problem.
side_effects: One vmstat invocation lasting N seconds.

args.name: samples
args.description: How many one-second samples to collect.

packs.name: Dell iDRAC (Redfish)
packs.description: Monitor and operate a Dell iDRAC over its Redfish REST API with curl — system identity and health rollup, the Dell subsystem rollup, storage controllers and drives, power and thermal telemetry, firmware inventory, the System Event Log and Lifecycle Controller log, and the Lifecycle Controller job queue. Plus gated mutators: power control (ComputerSystem.Reset), clear / delete a job, and clear the SEL. Targets an iDRAC by hostname/IP; credentials come from the runner environment. Reads are read-only GETs.

actions.title: Clear the Lifecycle Controller job queue
actions.summary: Clear the iDRAC job queue — POST /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Oem/Dell/DellJobService/Actions/DellJobService.DeleteJobQueue. JID_CLEARALL deletes all scheduled jobs and the pending attribute values attached to them; JID_CLEARALL_FORCE additionally restarts the Lifecycle Controller services — a last resort when the iDRAC/LC is wedged. Use to unstick a queue that is blocking new config or firmware jobs. Inspect the queue with jobs first; this can cancel work that is mid-flight.
actions.description: Clear the iDRAC job queue — POST /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Oem/Dell/DellJobService/Actions/DellJobService.DeleteJobQueue. JID_CLEARALL deletes all scheduled jobs and the pending attribute values attached to them; JID_CLEARALL_FORCE additionally restarts the Lifecycle Controller services — a last resort when the iDRAC/LC is wedged. Use to unstick a queue that is blocking new config or firmware jobs. Inspect the queue with jobs first; this can cancel work that is mid-flight.
side_effects: JID_CLEARALL: deletes every scheduled job and discards the pending configuration values staged with them.
JID_CLEARALL_FORCE: the above, plus restarts the Lifecycle Controller services (brief LC unavailability).
Can cancel an in-progress configuration or firmware-update job; pending changes are lost, not applied.
Does not change the server's power state or running OS.

args.name: scope
args.description: JID_CLEARALL clears all scheduled jobs; JID_CLEARALL_FORCE also restarts the LC services (last resort).

examples.title: Force-clear and restart LC services (wedged iDRAC)

actions.title: Clear the System Event Log
actions.summary: Erase the iDRAC System Event Log — POST /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Sel/Actions/LogService.ClearLog. IRREVERSIBLE: every SEL entry is wiped and cannot be recovered. Pull the log with sel first if you need a record. Routine after addressing the faults a server logged, but it destroys forensic history, so it is gated. (The Lifecycle Controller log is append-only and is intentionally not clearable here.)
actions.description: Erase the iDRAC System Event Log — POST /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Sel/Actions/LogService.ClearLog. IRREVERSIBLE: every SEL entry is wiped and cannot be recovered. Pull the log with sel first if you need a record. Routine after addressing the faults a server logged, but it destroys forensic history, so it is gated. (The Lifecycle Controller log is append-only and is intentionally not clearable here.)
side_effects: Irreversibly erases ALL System Event Log entries on the iDRAC.
Cannot be undone; prior hardware-fault history is lost.
Does not affect the Lifecycle Controller log, the running OS, or power state.

examples.title: Clear the SEL after addressing faults

actions.title: Delete one Lifecycle Controller job
actions.summary: Delete a single job from the queue by id — DELETE /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs/JID_xxxxxxxxxxxx. The surgical alternative to clear_job_queue: remove one stuck or unwanted job (find its JID with jobs) without touching the rest of the queue. Deleting a scheduled job discards its pending change; it does not undo a job that already completed.
actions.description: Delete a single job from the queue by id — DELETE /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs/JID_xxxxxxxxxxxx. The surgical alternative to clear_job_queue: remove one stuck or unwanted job (find its JID with jobs) without touching the rest of the queue. Deleting a scheduled job discards its pending change; it does not undo a job that already completed.
side_effects: Deletes the one named job from the iDRAC job queue.
If the job was scheduled (not yet applied), its pending change is discarded.
Leaves all other jobs and the server's power state untouched.

args.name: job_id
args.description: The job id to delete (JID_ followed by digits), as shown by jobs.

examples.title: Delete a stuck job by id

actions.title: List installed firmware versions
actions.summary: List the firmware inventory — GET /redfish/v1/UpdateService/FirmwareInventory?$expand=*($levels=1). Returns one member per component (BIOS, iDRAC, NIC, PERC, PSU, drives, …) with its Version and whether it is Updateable. Members are prefixed Installed- (running), Available- (staged/pending), or Previous- (rollback image). The answer to "what firmware is on this box and is anything pending?".
actions.description: List the firmware inventory — GET /redfish/v1/UpdateService/FirmwareInventory?$expand=*($levels=1). Returns one member per component (BIOS, iDRAC, NIC, PERC, PSU, drives, …) with its Version and whether it is Updateable. Members are prefixed Installed- (running), Available- (staged/pending), or Previous- (rollback image). The answer to "what firmware is on this box and is anything pending?".
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the firmware inventory, expanded one level.

actions.title: GET an arbitrary Redfish resource
actions.summary: Get any Redfish resource by path — a read-only GET under /redfish/v1 that the curated reads do not cover. Give a path like /Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Sensors or /Systems/System.Embedded.1/Memory?$expand=*($levels=1), including a query string ($expand, $skip, $filter). Use it to reach newer subsystems (/PowerSubsystem, /ThermalSubsystem), page a long log (?$skip=50), or read a single job (/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs/JID_xx…). Read-only — it only ever issues a GET.
actions.description: Get any Redfish resource by path — a read-only GET under /redfish/v1 that the curated reads do not cover. Give a path like /Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Sensors or /Systems/System.Embedded.1/Memory?$expand=*($levels=1), including a query string ($expand, $skip, $filter). Use it to reach newer subsystems (/PowerSubsystem, /ThermalSubsystem), page a long log (?$skip=50), or read a single job (/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs/JID_xx…). Read-only — it only ever issues a GET.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the supplied path.

args.name: path
args.description: Resource path under /redfish/v1, starting with /. May include a query string; URL-encode any spaces (e.g. %20 inside a $filter).

examples.title: Read the flat Sensors collection (newer firmware)
Page the SEL past the first 50 entries

actions.title: Show per-subsystem health rollup
actions.summary: Show the Dell subsystem health rollup in one call — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Oem/Dell/DellRollupStatus. Returns a member per subsystem (Processor, Memory, Storage, Fan, PowerSupply, Voltage, Intrusion, …), each with a SubSystem name and its RollupStatus (Ok / Warning / Critical), so one read tells you exactly which subsystem is degraded. The fastest "what is unhealthy on this box?" answer.
actions.description: Show the Dell subsystem health rollup in one call — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Oem/Dell/DellRollupStatus. Returns a member per subsystem (Processor, Memory, Storage, Fan, PowerSupply, Voltage, Intrusion, …), each with a SubSystem name and its RollupStatus (Ok / Warning / Critical), so one read tells you exactly which subsystem is degraded. The fastest "what is unhealthy on this box?" answer.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the Dell rollup-status collection.

actions.title: List Lifecycle Controller jobs
actions.summary: List the Lifecycle Controller job queue — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs?$expand=*($levels=1). Each job is expanded inline with its Id (JID_...), JobState (Scheduled / Running / Completed / Failed), PercentComplete, and Message. The answer to "what config/firmware jobs are queued or running, and how far along?". A queued job shows JobState Scheduled — read PercentComplete for true progress. Read-only.
actions.description: List the Lifecycle Controller job queue — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/Jobs?$expand=*($levels=1). Each job is expanded inline with its Id (JID_...), JobState (Scheduled / Running / Completed / Failed), PercentComplete, and Message. The answer to "what config/firmware jobs are queued or running, and how far along?". A queued job shows JobState Scheduled — read PercentComplete for true progress. Read-only.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the iDRAC job collection, expanded one level.

examples.title: All jobs with state and progress

actions.title: List Lifecycle Controller log entries
actions.summary: List the Lifecycle Controller log — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Lclog/Entries. The LC log is the iDRAC's own activity history: configuration changes, firmware updates, job results, and component events, each with Created, Severity, MessageId, and Message. Broader than the SEL (which is hardware faults only). Read-only.
actions.description: List the Lifecycle Controller log — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Lclog/Entries. The LC log is the iDRAC's own activity history: configuration changes, firmware updates, job results, and component events, each with Created, Severity, MessageId, and Message. Broader than the SEL (which is hardware faults only). Read-only.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the Lifecycle Controller log entries.

actions.title: Show iDRAC manager state and firmware
actions.summary: Show the iDRAC controller itself — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1. Returns the iDRAC firmware version, model, date/time, and its own Status.Health. Use to confirm the management controller's firmware level and that the iDRAC is healthy, as distinct from the server it manages.
actions.description: Show the iDRAC controller itself — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1. Returns the iDRAC firmware version, model, date/time, and its own Status.Health. Use to confirm the management controller's firmware level and that the iDRAC is healthy, as distinct from the server it manages.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the Manager resource.

actions.title: Show power supplies and consumption
actions.summary: Show power telemetry — GET /redfish/v1/Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Power. Returns each power supply (model, capacity, line input, Status.Health) and their redundancy, the system voltages, and the current/average power consumed in watts. Use to check PSU health/redundancy and draw. (Newer iDRAC firmware also serves /PowerSubsystem; this universal /Power resource works on iDRAC8 and iDRAC9.)
actions.description: Show power telemetry — GET /redfish/v1/Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Power. Returns each power supply (model, capacity, line input, Status.Health) and their redundancy, the system voltages, and the current/average power consumed in watts. Use to check PSU health/redundancy and draw. (Newer iDRAC firmware also serves /PowerSubsystem; this universal /Power resource works on iDRAC8 and iDRAC9.)
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the Power resource.

actions.title: Reset / power-control the server
actions.summary: Change the server's power state via Redfish — POST /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Actions/ComputerSystem.Reset with the chosen ResetType.
actions.description: Change the server's power state via Redfish — POST /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Actions/ComputerSystem.Reset with the chosen ResetType. GracefulShutdown and GracefulRestart ask the OS to stop cleanly; ForceOff, ForceRestart, PowerCycle, and Nmi are abrupt and can lose in-flight data. On (power up) and PushPowerButton (toggle) are also available. Check the current state with system (PowerState) first. The exact ResetType set varies by iDRAC firmware — the iDRAC rejects an unsupported value with an error.
side_effects: GracefulShutdown / GracefulRestart: asks the OS to shut down or reboot in an orderly way (the OS may delay or refuse).
ForceOff / ForceRestart / PowerCycle: abrupt power-off / reset with no OS coordination — unsaved data can be lost.
Nmi: sends a non-maskable interrupt (triggers a crash/diagnostic dump), not a clean power action.
On / PushPowerButton: powers up / toggles the virtual power button.
Any restart or off drops every running service and open connection on the host.

args.name: reset_type
args.description: Redfish ResetType. Graceful: GracefulShutdown, GracefulRestart. Forceful: ForceOff, ForceRestart, PowerCycle, Nmi. Power up: On, PushPowerButton.

actions.title: List System Event Log entries
actions.summary: List the System Event Log — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Sel/Entries. Each entry has Created, Severity (OK / Warning / Critical), Message, MessageId, and the sensor that raised it — the hardware-fault history (ECC, PSU, thermal, intrusion). Read-only. For a long log, page with the generic get action and ?$skip=N, or filter with ?$filter=Severity eq 'Critical'.
actions.description: List the System Event Log — GET /redfish/v1/Managers/iDRAC.Embedded.1/LogServices/Sel/Entries. Each entry has Created, Severity (OK / Warning / Critical), Message, MessageId, and the sensor that raised it — the hardware-fault history (ECC, PSU, thermal, intrusion). Read-only. For a long log, page with the generic get action and ?$skip=N, or filter with ?$filter=Severity eq 'Critical'.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the SEL entries collection.

actions.title: Show storage controllers and drives
actions.summary: List the storage subsystem with controllers and drives expanded in one call — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Storage?$expand=*($levels=1). Returns each controller (e.g. PERC) and its physical drives with capacity, media type, and Status.Health, so you can spot a predicted or failed drive. Read-only.
actions.description: List the storage subsystem with controllers and drives expanded in one call — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1/Storage?$expand=*($levels=1). Returns each controller (e.g. PERC) and its physical drives with capacity, media type, and Status.Health, so you can spot a predicted or failed drive. Read-only.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the Storage collection, expanded one level.

actions.title: Show system identity, power state, health
actions.summary: Show the server's top-line state — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1. Returns model, service tag (SKU), BIOS version, PowerState (On/Off), the overall Status.HealthRollup (OK / Warning / Critical, aggregating subordinate subsystems), and the inline ProcessorSummary and MemorySummary health. The starting point for "what is this server and is it healthy?", and the action to verify the pack can reach and authenticate to an iDRAC.
actions.description: Show the server's top-line state — GET /redfish/v1/Systems/System.Embedded.1. Returns model, service tag (SKU), BIOS version, PowerState (On/Off), the overall Status.HealthRollup (OK / Warning / Critical, aggregating subordinate subsystems), and the inline ProcessorSummary and MemorySummary health. The starting point for "what is this server and is it healthy?", and the action to verify the pack can reach and authenticate to an iDRAC.
side_effects: One read-only Redfish GET of the ComputerSystem resource.

actions.title: Show fans and temperatures
actions.summary: Show thermal telemetry — GET /redfish/v1/Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Thermal. Returns each temperature sensor (inlet, exhaust, CPU) in Celsius with its thresholds and each fan's RPM and Status.Health, plus fan redundancy. Use to check for a hot inlet, a failed fan, or lost cooling redundancy. (Newer iDRAC firmware also serves /ThermalSubsystem; this universal /Thermal resource works on iDRAC8 and iDRAC9.)
actions.description: Show thermal telemetry — GET /redfish/v1/Chassis/System.Embedded.1/Thermal. Returns each temperature sensor (inlet, exhaust, CPU) in Celsius with its thresholds and each fan's RPM and Status.Health, plus fan redundancy. Use to check for a hot inlet, a failed fan, or lost cooling redundancy. (Newer iDRAC firmware also serves /ThermalSubsystem; this universal /Thermal resource works on iDRAC8 and iDRAC9.)
