packs.name: Linux core operations pack
packs.description: Read-only Linux diagnostics plus narrow service control. Disk, mem, uptime, journalctl, log grep + tail, users/auth introspection, cron audit, network state, kernel info, and systemctl control. The front-line pack every Linux host gets.

actions.title: ARP / IPv6 neighbor table
actions.summary: `ip neigh show` — every known L2 neighbor with state (REACHABLE, STALE, FAILED). Useful for diagnosing intermittent L2 reachability problems. Read-only.
actions.description: `ip neigh show` — every known L2 neighbor with state (REACHABLE, STALE, FAILED). Useful for diagnosing intermittent L2 reachability problems. Read-only.

actions.title: CPU topology and features
actions.summary: `lscpu` output — sockets, cores per socket, threads per core, architecture, microarchitecture, MHz, cache sizes, vulnerabilities (Spectre/Meltdown mitigation state). Use to confirm a host's CPU matches what the workload assumes. Read-only.
actions.description: `lscpu` output — sockets, cores per socket, threads per core, architecture, microarchitecture, MHz, cache sizes, vulnerabilities (Spectre/Meltdown mitigation state). Use to confirm a host's CPU matches what the workload assumes. Read-only.

actions.title: Recent cron job execution log
actions.summary: Show last N journalctl entries matching CRON. Lets operators see "did the backup job fire last night?" without grepping syslog. Read-only.
actions.description: Show last N journalctl entries matching CRON. Lets operators see "did the backup job fire last night?" without grepping syslog. Read-only.

args.name: count
args.description: How many recent CRON entries to return.

actions.title: All user crontabs + system cron dirs
actions.summary: Dumps every per-user crontab AND the system cron dirs (/etc/crontab, /etc/cron.d/, /etc/cron.{hourly,daily,weekly,monthly}/). Use to answer "what's scheduled on this host?" Read-only. Cron command lines routinely carry inline credentials (a `curl` bearer token, a `mysql -p<pw>`), so this can surface secrets. The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
actions.description: Dumps every per-user crontab AND the system cron dirs (/etc/crontab, /etc/cron.d/, /etc/cron.{hourly,daily,weekly,monthly}/). Use to answer "what's scheduled on this host?" Read-only. Cron command lines routinely carry inline credentials (a `curl` bearer token, a `mysql -p<pw>`), so this can surface secrets. The runner's redaction is a fail-closed backstop, not a guarantee — it is pattern-bound and can miss a bespoke secret whose name and value match no rule.
side_effects: Requires root; exposes cron command lines (may include inline secrets).

examples.title: Every scheduled job on this host

argv: for u in $(getent passwd | awk -F: '$7 !~ /nologin|false/ {print $1}'); do echo "--- crontab -u $u ---"; crontab -l -u "$u" 2>/dev/null; done; echo '--- /etc/crontab ---'; cat /etc/crontab 2>/dev/null; echo '--- /etc/cron.d/ ---'; ls -la /etc/cron.d/ 2>/dev/null; for d in hourly daily weekly monthly; do echo "--- /etc/cron.$d/ ---"; ls -la "/etc/cron.$d/" 2>/dev/null; done

actions.title: SMART health for one disk
actions.summary: Runs `smartctl -H -A` against a block device. Returns the overall PASS/FAIL plus the attribute table (reallocated sectors, pending, uncorrectable, temperature). Read-only on the disk; needs the `smartmontools` package and CAP_SYS_RAWIO (root) to access device registers. For a disk behind a RAID controller (Dell PERC, HP SmartArray), pass `device_type` (e.g. `megaraid,0`) — the bare block device isn't reachable through the controller without it.
actions.description: Runs `smartctl -H -A` against a block device. Returns the overall PASS/FAIL plus the attribute table (reallocated sectors, pending, uncorrectable, temperature). Read-only on the disk; needs the `smartmontools` package and CAP_SYS_RAWIO (root) to access device registers. For a disk behind a RAID controller (Dell PERC, HP SmartArray), pass `device_type` (e.g. `megaraid,0`) — the bare block device isn't reachable through the controller without it.
side_effects: Reads SMART registers; no writes to the disk.

args.name: device_type
args.description: smartctl device type (-d). "auto" (default) auto-detects direct ATA/SATA/NVMe disks; use "megaraid,N" or "cciss,N" for a disk behind a RAID controller, or "sat"/"scsi"/"nvme" to force a transport.

examples.title: SMART for a disk behind a PERC / megaraid controller

actions.title: Filesystem disk usage
actions.summary: Reports filesystem usage for the supplied paths using df. Read-only. Use this to assess disk pressure before recommending cleanup, repair, compaction tuning, or adding disk. If a filesystem is >85% full, surface it but do not silently delete or truncate data — that's a separate, approval-gated action.
actions.description: Reports filesystem usage for the supplied paths using df. Read-only. Use this to assess disk pressure before recommending cleanup, repair, compaction tuning, or adding disk. If a filesystem is >85% full, surface it but do not silently delete or truncate data — that's a separate, approval-gated action.
side_effects: Does not mount, unmount, or modify anything.

args.name: paths
args.description: One or more paths to inspect. Each path is passed to df -P -h.

actions.title: Recent failed login attempts
actions.summary: Recent failed authentication attempts from the systemd journal — sshd's "Failed password" / "Invalid user" plus pam_unix "authentication failure" from su/sudo/login. High-signal for "is this host being brute-forced?" Read-only, but flagged medium-risk: the source IPs and usernames are sensitive PII. A healthy host with no failed auth returns nothing (the journal query exits 1 on no match, which is treated as success).
actions.description: Recent failed authentication attempts from the systemd journal — sshd's "Failed password" / "Invalid user" plus pam_unix "authentication failure" from su/sudo/login. High-signal for "is this host being brute-forced?" Read-only, but flagged medium-risk: the source IPs and usernames are sensitive PII. A healthy host with no failed auth returns nothing (the journal query exits 1 on no match, which is treated as success).
side_effects: One journalctl read of the auth/authpriv journal (root or the systemd-journal group).
Output includes source IPs and usernames (PII).

args.name: count
args.description: How many of the most recent failed attempts to return.

actions.title: Grep a log file
actions.summary: Greps an extended regex (POSIX -E) against a log file under /var/log.
actions.description: Greps an extended regex (POSIX -E) against a log file under /var/log. Read-only. Returns matching lines with line numbers (-n) up to max_lines. Use to find recent occurrences of a specific identifier (request ID, user ID, IP) or to spot error patterns without dumping the whole file. Output is run through the runner's redactor before it leaves the host. On journald-only hosts (no rsyslog) the system log isn't a file under /var/log — use linux.journalctl for syslog/messages; this reads on-disk files (app, nginx, …).
side_effects: Output may contain operational secrets — redactor scrubs known patterns.

args.name: file
args.description: Absolute path to the log file. Must be under /var/log/.

args.name: pattern
args.description: Extended regex (POSIX ERE) pattern. Passed to grep -E.

actions.title: Hardware + BIOS summary via dmidecode
actions.summary: `dmidecode` system + bios + chassis sections. Vendor, model, serial, BIOS version, manufacturing date. Required root (read-only on SMBIOS). Useful when correlating hardware-class to OS-class incidents. dmidecode reads SMBIOS — absent on containers and many cloud VMs, where this fails command-not-found; expect it on bare metal or VMs with SMBIOS passthrough.
actions.description: `dmidecode` system + bios + chassis sections. Vendor, model, serial, BIOS version, manufacturing date. Required root (read-only on SMBIOS). Useful when correlating hardware-class to OS-class incidents. dmidecode reads SMBIOS — absent on containers and many cloud VMs, where this fails command-not-found; expect it on bare metal or VMs with SMBIOS passthrough.

actions.title: Inode usage per filesystem
actions.summary: Shows inode usage per filesystem (`df -i`). A "disk full" report that doesn't match `df -h` is almost always inode exhaustion — this surfaces it directly. Read-only.
actions.description: Shows inode usage per filesystem (`df -i`). A "disk full" report that doesn't match `df -h` is almost always inode exhaustion — this surfaces it directly. Read-only.

actions.title: Recent systemd journal entries
actions.summary: Reads recent systemd journal entries for a named unit, filtered by priority and a time window. Use to triage service errors. Logs may reveal sensitive identifiers, IPs, hostnames, or PII; treat output as confidential. Do not echo verbatim to end users without consideration.
actions.description: Reads recent systemd journal entries for a named unit, filtered by priority and a time window. Use to triage service errors. Logs may reveal sensitive identifiers, IPs, hostnames, or PII; treat output as confidential. Do not echo verbatim to end users without consideration.
side_effects: Reads service logs which may contain operational secrets.
Output is run through the runner's redactor before leaving the host.

args.name: unit
args.description: Systemd unit name to inspect (e.g. nginx, nomad, consul.service, foo@bar.service).

args.name: since
args.description: Look back at most this far.

actions.title: Grep recent systemd journal entries
actions.summary: Like linux.journalctl, but filters entries to those matching a regex (via journalctl --grep). Read-only. Use when you already know the rough identifier or substring you're looking for (request ID, hostname, IP, error string) and don't want to download a large unfiltered journal slice. Output is run through the runner's redactor before leaving the host.
actions.description: Like linux.journalctl, but filters entries to those matching a regex (via journalctl --grep). Read-only. Use when you already know the rough identifier or substring you're looking for (request ID, hostname, IP, error string) and don't want to download a large unfiltered journal slice. Output is run through the runner's redactor before leaving the host.
side_effects: Reads service logs which may contain operational secrets.
Output is run through the runner's redactor before egress.

args.name: unit
args.description: Systemd unit name to inspect (e.g. nginx, nomad, consul.service, foo@bar.service).

args.name: since
args.description: Look back at most this far.

actions.title: Loaded kernel modules sorted by size
actions.summary: `lsmod` sorted by size, top 30. Useful to spot unexpected modules loaded on a production host (rootkits, debug tooling, vendor drivers). Read-only.
actions.description: `lsmod` sorted by size, top 30. Useful to spot unexpected modules loaded on a production host (rootkits, debug tooling, vendor drivers). Read-only.

actions.title: Recent successful logins
actions.summary: Show last N successful logins from /var/log/wtmp via `last`. Shows user, terminal, source IP, duration. Read-only.
actions.description: Show last N successful logins from /var/log/wtmp via `last`. Shows user, terminal, source IP, duration. Read-only.

args.name: count
args.description: How many recent logins to return.

actions.title: LVM volume / group / PV summary
actions.summary: Runs `lvs && vgs && pvs` for a one-shot LVM topology snapshot. Logical volumes, volume groups, physical volumes — sizes, free space, attributes. Read-only. Needs LVM tools installed.
actions.description: Runs `lvs && vgs && pvs` for a one-shot LVM topology snapshot. Logical volumes, volume groups, physical volumes — sizes, free space, attributes. Read-only. Needs LVM tools installed.

actions.title: mdadm RAID array status
actions.summary: Reads /proc/mdstat plus `mdadm --detail` for every active array. Surfaces array state, devices, sync progress, faults. Read-only. Returns "no arrays" cleanly when there's no mdadm RAID configured.
actions.description: Reads /proc/mdstat plus `mdadm --detail` for every active array. Surfaces array state, devices, sync progress, faults. Read-only. Returns "no arrays" cleanly when there's no mdadm RAID configured.
side_effects: One cat + one mdadm --detail per array.

argv: cat /proc/mdstat; for md in /dev/md[0-9]*; do [ -e "$md" ] || continue; echo; mdadm --detail "$md"; done

actions.title: System memory snapshot
actions.summary: Reports memory and swap usage via free -m. Read-only, single sample. Memory state fluctuates between consecutive calls; take two samples a few seconds apart before drawing conclusions about pressure.
actions.description: Reports memory and swap usage via free -m. Read-only, single sample. Memory state fluctuates between consecutive calls; take two samples a few seconds apart before drawing conclusions about pressure.

actions.title: Full /proc/meminfo
actions.summary: Dump full /proc/meminfo. More detail than the `linux.memory` action — surfaces hugepages, slab, dirty/writeback, page tables, KSM, cgroup memcg. Read-only.
actions.description: Dump full /proc/meminfo. More detail than the `linux.memory` action — surfaces hugepages, slab, dirty/writeback, page tables, KSM, cgroup memcg. Read-only.

actions.title: Mounted filesystems
actions.summary: Lists every mounted filesystem with type, source device, mountpoint, and mount options. Read-only. Use to confirm a service has the right disk attached or to find a stuck/duplicate mount.
actions.description: Lists every mounted filesystem with type, source device, mountpoint, and mount options. Read-only. Use to confirm a service has the right disk attached or to find a stuck/duplicate mount.

actions.title: Network interfaces
actions.summary: `ip -j addr show` JSON. Returns every interface with its addresses, state, MAC, MTU. Use to confirm an interface is up with the right IP. Read-only.
actions.description: `ip -j addr show` JSON. Returns every interface with its addresses, state, MAC, MTU. Use to confirm an interface is up with the right IP. Read-only.

actions.title: Routing table + policy rules
actions.summary: `ip route show && ip rule show && ip -6 route show`. Use to debug "why does traffic go via X instead of Y?" — surfaces every route and policy-based routing rule. Read-only.
actions.description: `ip route show && ip rule show && ip -6 route show`. Use to debug "why does traffic go via X instead of Y?" — surfaces every route and policy-based routing rule. Read-only.

argv: ip route show; echo; ip rule show; echo; ip -6 route show

actions.title: Distro + kernel identity
actions.summary: Returns /etc/os-release plus `uname -a`. Identifies the distribution, version, codename, and kernel. Use as a first sanity check before recommending distro-specific commands. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns /etc/os-release plus `uname -a`. Identifies the distribution, version, codename, and kernel. Use as a first sanity check before recommending distro-specific commands. Read-only.

actions.title: Schedule a host reboot
actions.summary: Schedules a reboot via `shutdown -r +1` — one-minute warning to give in-flight connections a chance to drain. Sends a wall message with the operator-supplied reason. Cannot be undone except by `shutdown -c` BEFORE the minute elapses; once the kernel begins shutdown the host is going down regardless.
actions.description: Schedules a reboot via `shutdown -r +1` — one-minute warning to give in-flight connections a chance to drain. Sends a wall message with the operator-supplied reason. Cannot be undone except by `shutdown -c` BEFORE the minute elapses; once the kernel begins shutdown the host is going down regardless.
side_effects: Wall message broadcast to all logged-in users.
One-minute timer set; kernel shutdown begins after.
Every running service stops; all open connections drop.

args.name: note
args.description: Message broadcast to logged-in users in the reboot warning.

actions.title: sudoers configuration audit
actions.summary: Dumps /etc/sudoers and the index of /etc/sudoers.d/. Use for an audit pass — "who can sudo to what?". Sensitive content; output goes through redaction. Read-only.
actions.description: Dumps /etc/sudoers and the index of /etc/sudoers.d/. Use for an audit pass — "who can sudo to what?". Sensitive content; output goes through redaction. Read-only.

actions.title: Disable a systemd unit at boot
actions.summary: `systemctl disable <unit>`. Removes the boot symlinks. Does NOT stop the unit now — pair with `linux.systemctl_stop` for that. Persistent change across reboots.
actions.description: `systemctl disable <unit>`. Removes the boot symlinks. Does NOT stop the unit now — pair with `linux.systemctl_stop` for that. Persistent change across reboots.

actions.title: Enable a systemd unit at boot
actions.summary: `systemctl enable <unit>`. Creates the symlinks so the unit starts at boot. Does NOT start it now — pair with `linux.systemctl_start` for that. Persistent change across reboots.
actions.description: `systemctl enable <unit>`. Creates the symlinks so the unit starts at boot. Does NOT start it now — pair with `linux.systemctl_start` for that. Persistent change across reboots.
side_effects: Idempotent — re-running on an already-enabled unit is a no-op.

actions.title: Reload a systemd unit's config
actions.summary: `systemctl reload <unit>`. The unit re-reads its config without restarting (works only for units declaring an ExecReload). Use after editing nginx.conf / postgresql.conf / etc. The reload is graceful by definition — no requests dropped.
actions.description: `systemctl reload <unit>`. The unit re-reads its config without restarting (works only for units declaring an ExecReload). Use after editing nginx.conf / postgresql.conf / etc. The reload is graceful by definition — no requests dropped.
side_effects: Sends SIGHUP (or the configured reload signal) to the unit.
Unit re-reads its config; existing workers continue.

actions.title: Restart a systemd unit
actions.summary: Restarts a named systemd unit.
actions.description: Restarts a named systemd unit. Clients will see an outage of seconds-to-minutes depending on the unit. Treat as a last-resort action: prefer diagnosis (logs, status, disk, memory) first. Never restart a database under load without confirming there is no in-flight repair, compaction, or similar background operation. WHICH units this runner may restart is an operator policy decision (high-risk → require_approval by default), not a fixed list; the unit is bounded to a valid systemd unit name so it can't carry shell metacharacters.
side_effects: Stops the named unit, then starts it.
Disconnects existing clients of the unit.
May trigger downstream alerts if the unit takes time to recover.
Does not modify configuration or on-disk state.

args.name: unit
args.description: Systemd unit to restart (e.g. nginx, nomad, consul.service, foo@bar.service).

examples.title: Restart nginx after a config reload check

actions.title: Start a systemd unit
actions.summary: `systemctl start <unit>`. Brings a unit up. The unit name is pattern-restricted to safe systemd unit naming. Combine with `linux.systemctl_status` afterward to confirm the unit reached active state.
actions.description: `systemctl start <unit>`. Brings a unit up. The unit name is pattern-restricted to safe systemd unit naming. Combine with `linux.systemctl_status` afterward to confirm the unit reached active state.
side_effects: Starts the unit (and any required dependencies).
Triggers ExecStartPre/Post hooks declared in the unit.

actions.title: Systemd unit status
actions.summary: Reports the current systemd unit status for a named service. Read-only. Use to confirm whether a service is active before recommending diagnostic or remediation actions.
actions.description: Reports the current systemd unit status for a named service. Read-only. Use to confirm whether a service is active before recommending diagnostic or remediation actions.

actions.title: Stop a systemd unit
actions.summary: `systemctl stop <unit>`. Brings a unit down. Sends SIGTERM, waits for TimeoutStopSec, then SIGKILL. In-flight requests on the service are dropped unless the unit handles graceful drain.
actions.description: `systemctl stop <unit>`. Brings a unit down. Sends SIGTERM, waits for TimeoutStopSec, then SIGKILL. In-flight requests on the service are dropped unless the unit handles graceful drain.

actions.title: Tail a log file
actions.summary: Reads the last N lines of a log file under /var/log. Read-only. Use to glance at the most recent activity for a service when triaging an alert, before deciding whether to grep deeper or pull more context. Output is run through the runner's redactor before it leaves the host. On journald-only hosts (no rsyslog) the system log isn't a file under /var/log — use linux.journalctl for syslog/messages; this reads on-disk files (app, nginx, …).
actions.description: Reads the last N lines of a log file under /var/log. Read-only. Use to glance at the most recent activity for a service when triaging an alert, before deciding whether to grep deeper or pull more context. Output is run through the runner's redactor before it leaves the host. On journald-only hosts (no rsyslog) the system log isn't a file under /var/log — use linux.journalctl for syslog/messages; this reads on-disk files (app, nginx, …).
side_effects: Output may contain operational secrets — redactor scrubs known patterns.

args.name: file
args.description: Absolute path to the log file. Must be under /var/log/.

args.name: lines
args.description: Number of trailing lines to return (tail -n).

examples.title: Last 100 lines of nginx error log
Last 500 lines of an app log

actions.title: System uptime and load average
actions.summary: Reports system uptime and 1/5/15-minute load averages. Load averages are dimensionless and machine-dependent; compare against CPU count before interpreting them as "high".
actions.description: Reports system uptime and 1/5/15-minute load averages. Load averages are dimensionless and machine-dependent; compare against CPU count before interpreting them as "high".
side_effects: Reads /proc/loadavg and /proc/uptime via the uptime utility.

actions.title: Currently logged-in users
actions.summary: `who` + `w` — usernames, terminals, login times, source IPs, and what each session is currently running. Use to confirm whether a human is on the box during an incident. Read-only.
actions.description: `who` + `w` — usernames, terminals, login times, source IPs, and what each session is currently running. Use to confirm whether a human is on the box during an incident. Read-only.

packs.name: Memcached cache server
packs.description: Read-only memcached introspection via the `stats` ASCII protocol — general stats, slab usage, item counts, sizes. Plus FLUSH_ALL as a critical mutator. Target host/port via MEMCACHED_HOST + MEMCACHED_PORT env vars on the runner host (default 127.0.0.1:11211).

actions.title: flush_all
actions.summary: Invalidates every key. Cold cache → origin load spike.
actions.description: Invalidates every key. Cold cache → origin load spike.
side_effects: First miss hits origin for every cached value.

actions.title: stats (general)
actions.summary: Show uptime, current connections, get/set rates, hit/miss, bytes, evictions.
actions.description: Show uptime, current connections, get/set rates, hit/miss, bytes, evictions.

actions.title: stats items
actions.summary: Show per-slab item counts + age.
actions.description: Show per-slab item counts + age.

actions.title: stats settings
actions.summary: Show daemon configuration at boot.
actions.description: Show daemon configuration at boot.

actions.title: stats sizes
actions.summary: Show a histogram of item sizes. WARNING: scans all items — can be slow on busy servers.
actions.description: Show a histogram of item sizes. WARNING: scans all items — can be slow on busy servers.
side_effects: Scans every item — high latency hit on busy servers.

actions.title: stats slabs
actions.summary: Show per-slab allocation + utilization.
actions.description: Show per-slab allocation + utilization.

actions.title: version
actions.summary: Show the daemon version.
actions.description: Show the daemon version.

packs.name: MinIO operations
packs.description: Cluster + bucket + user introspection plus operator surface (user enable/disable, bucket heal, cluster service restart). Talks to mc CLI configured via MC_HOST_<alias> env var on the runner host.

actions.title: mc admin info
actions.summary: Show cluster info: nodes, storage, network, uptime.
actions.description: Show cluster info: nodes, storage, network, uptime.

actions.title: mc admin top locks
actions.summary: List the top object locks held across the cluster — spots stuck operations.
actions.description: List the top object locks held across the cluster — spots stuck operations.

actions.title: mc admin trace (5s window)
actions.summary: Trace S3 API calls hitting the cluster for 5 seconds.
actions.description: Trace S3 API calls hitting the cluster for 5 seconds.

argv: timeout 5 mc admin trace -v "{{ args.alias }}" || true

actions.title: mc du (bucket)
actions.summary: Show object count + size for one bucket.
actions.description: Show object count + size for one bucket.
side_effects: One scan; can be heavy on huge buckets.

actions.title: mc admin heal -r <alias>/<bucket>
actions.summary: Heal one bucket recursively. Scans every object, repairs inconsistent erasure-coded shards, restores missing replicas. CPU + I/O heavy; run during low-traffic windows.
actions.description: Heal one bucket recursively. Scans every object, repairs inconsistent erasure-coded shards, restores missing replicas. CPU + I/O heavy; run during low-traffic windows.
side_effects: All objects under the bucket scanned.
CPU + disk I/O spike for the duration of the heal.

actions.title: mc admin heal --recursive (cluster-wide)
actions.summary: Triggers a cluster-wide healing scan that repairs objects (not a dry-run). Does NOT use --remove. May be IO-heavy.
actions.description: Triggers a cluster-wide healing scan that repairs objects (not a dry-run). Does NOT use --remove. May be IO-heavy.
side_effects: Heavy disk + CPU during scan on each node.
May actually heal objects (not just dry-run).

actions.title: mc admin policy ls
actions.summary: List all IAM policy names.
actions.description: List all IAM policy names.

actions.title: mc admin user list
actions.summary: List all IAM users + their status + attached policies.
actions.description: List all IAM users + their status + attached policies.

actions.title: mc ls (root)
actions.summary: List all buckets in one alias.
actions.description: List all buckets in one alias.

actions.title: mc admin service restart <alias>
actions.summary: Restart MinIO across the cluster. Coordinated rolling restart — one node at a time so the cluster stays available. Use after a config change that requires restart.
actions.description: Restart MinIO across the cluster. Coordinated rolling restart — one node at a time so the cluster stays available. Use after a config change that requires restart.
side_effects: Brief unavailability per node; cluster-wide writes still served.

actions.title: mc admin user disable
actions.summary: Disables one IAM user. They can't authenticate until re-enabled.
actions.description: Disables one IAM user. They can't authenticate until re-enabled.

actions.description: Re-enables one IAM user.

packs.name: MongoDB operations
packs.description: Replica-set + shard-cluster introspection, slow-query identification, collection stats, plus remediation surface (killOp, replSet stepDown, collection compact, dropIndex). Authenticates via MONGO_URI env var on the runner host.

actions.title: sh.getBalancerState()
actions.summary: Show whether the balancer is enabled + currently running.
actions.description: Show whether the balancer is enabled + currently running.

actions.title: buildInfo
actions.summary: Show server version + build flags.
actions.description: Show server version + build flags.

actions.title: db.getCollectionNames()
actions.summary: List collections in one database.
actions.description: List collections in one database.

actions.title: db.<coll>.stats()
actions.summary: Show per-collection totals — count, size, storage, indexes.
actions.description: Show per-collection totals — count, size, storage, indexes.

actions.title: db.runCommand({compact:"<coll>"})
actions.summary: Compact one collection on the connected node. Reclaims disk for WiredTiger after large deletes. BLOCKS reads/writes on the collection during compaction. Run during a low-traffic window; prefer running on a secondary first.
actions.description: Compact one collection on the connected node. Reclaims disk for WiredTiger after large deletes. BLOCKS reads/writes on the collection during compaction. Run during a low-traffic window; prefer running on a secondary first.
side_effects: Collection's reads/writes blocked until compaction finishes.

actions.title: Read/write tickets (concurrency saturation)
actions.summary: Show read/write ticket pools — out vs available. Queued/exhausted tickets are the classic sign of storage-engine overload. Version-tolerant: reads queues.execution on MongoDB 8.0+, else wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions (<= 7.0).
actions.description: Show read/write ticket pools — out vs available. Queued/exhausted tickets are the classic sign of storage-engine overload. Version-tolerant: reads queues.execution on MongoDB 8.0+, else wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions (<= 7.0).

actions.title: Incoming connection saturation (serverStatus.connections)
actions.summary: Show incoming connection counts — current, available, active, totalCreated. Approaching the available limit means new clients will be refused. Distinct from connections (connPoolStats), which is the outgoing pool.
actions.description: Show incoming connection counts — current, available, active, totalCreated. Approaching the available limit means new clients will be refused. Distinct from connections (connPoolStats), which is the outgoing pool.

actions.title: connPoolStats
actions.summary: Show connection pool stats — totals, active, available, by host.
actions.description: Show connection pool stats — totals, active, available, by host.

actions.title: db.currentOp()
actions.summary: List in-flight operations. The canonical "what is the server doing right now?" check.
actions.description: List in-flight operations. The canonical "what is the server doing right now?" check.

actions.title: Cursor saturation / timed-out cursors (metrics.cursor)
actions.summary: Show cursor counts — open.total, open.noTimeout, open.pinned, timedOut, totalOpened. A growing timedOut count usually means an application is leaking cursors.
actions.description: Show cursor counts — open.total, open.noTimeout, open.pinned, timedOut, totalOpened. A growing timedOut count usually means an application is leaking cursors.

actions.title: listDatabases
actions.summary: List all databases with size + empty flag.
actions.description: List all databases with size + empty flag.

actions.title: db.stats()
actions.summary: Show database-level totals — collections, indexes, data size, storage size.
actions.description: Show database-level totals — collections, indexes, data size, storage size.

actions.title: Default read/write concern (getDefaultRWConcern)
actions.summary: Show cluster default read and write concern (e.g. w:majority) and its source. The durability/consistency config for the replica set — a weakened default write concern is a common cause of surprising data loss on failover.
actions.description: Show cluster default read and write concern (e.g. w:majority) and its source. The durability/consistency config for the replica set — a weakened default write concern is a common cause of surprising data loss on failover.

actions.title: db.<coll>.dropIndex("<name>")
actions.summary: Drop one index from a collection. Use to remove a hot or unused index discovered via index_stats. Queries that relied on the index degrade to COLLSCAN until rebuilt or replaced.
actions.description: Drop one index from a collection. Use to remove a hot or unused index discovered via index_stats. Queries that relied on the index degrade to COLLSCAN until rebuilt or replaced.
side_effects: Queries that used the index switch to other indexes or full scan.

actions.title: Election / stepdown history (electionMetrics)
actions.summary: Show cumulative election and stepdown counters for this member — stepUpCmd, priorityTakeover, electionTimeout, catchUpTakeover, numStepDownsCausedBy HigherTerm, numCatchUps. Use to see how often the set has been re-electing.
actions.description: Show cumulative election and stepdown counters for this member — stepUpCmd, priorityTakeover, electionTimeout, catchUpTakeover, numStepDownsCausedBy HigherTerm, numCatchUps. Use to see how often the set has been re-electing.

actions.title: explain a find()
actions.summary: Show the execution plan for `find(filter)` on one collection at `executionStats` verbosity.
actions.description: Show the execution plan for `find(filter)` on one collection at `executionStats` verbosity.

actions.title: getParameter '*'
actions.summary: List all server parameters (read-only view of the current config).
actions.description: List all server parameters (read-only view of the current config).

actions.title: Global lock queue / active clients (globalLock)
actions.summary: Show global lock contention — currentQueue (total/readers/writers) waiting on the lock, and activeClients (total/readers/writers). A growing queue points to lock contention or an overloaded server.
actions.description: Show global lock contention — currentQueue (total/readers/writers) waiting on the lock, and activeClients (total/readers/writers). A growing queue points to lock contention or an overloaded server.

actions.title: hostInfo
actions.summary: Show host CPU, memory, NUMA layout, OS.
actions.description: Show host CPU, memory, NUMA layout, OS.

actions.title: In-progress index builds (with progress)
actions.summary: List active index-build operations with progress (done/total), via $currentOp. The canonical "is an index build stuck / how far along is it" check across the deployment.
actions.description: List active index-build operations with progress (done/total), via $currentOp. The canonical "is an index build stuck / how far along is it" check across the deployment.

actions.description: Show per-index access counts since server start. Zeros usually mean unused indexes.

actions.description: Show oplog size + first/last timestamps + estimated time window. The canonical "how far can a secondary fall behind?" check.

actions.summary: Read the database profiler collection (system.profile) for the slowest recent ops over a millis threshold, newest first. Requires profiling to be enabled on the database already (see profiler_status); does not change it.
actions.description: Read the database profiler collection (system.profile) for the slowest recent ops over a millis threshold, newest first. Requires profiling to be enabled on the database already (see profiler_status); does not change it.

actions.title: db.getProfilingStatus()
actions.summary: Show profiler level + slow-op threshold for one database.
actions.description: Show profiler level + slow-op threshold for one database.

actions.summary: Show oplog time window — logSizeMB, usedMB, timeDiff / timeDiffHours, tFirst, tLast. How much history the oplog retains, which bounds how long a secondary can be down before it needs a full resync. Distinct from oplog size.
actions.description: Show oplog time window — logSizeMB, usedMB, timeDiff / timeDiffHours, tFirst, tLast. How much history the oplog retains, which bounds how long a secondary can be down before it needs a full resync. Distinct from oplog size.

actions.title: rs.conf()
actions.summary: Show replica set configuration — members, votes, priorities, settings.
actions.description: Show replica set configuration — members, votes, priorities, settings.

actions.summary: Show per-member seconds behind the primary, computed from rs.status() optimeDate (with state, health, sync source, ping). The canonical replica-set lag check.
actions.description: Show per-member seconds behind the primary, computed from rs.status() optimeDate (with state, health, sync source, ping). The canonical replica-set lag check.

actions.description: Show replica set member health, lag, last applied optime.

actions.summary: Force the current primary to step down. Replica set elects a new primary; ~5–15s of write unavailability is typical. Use during planned failover when one secondary is known healthier.
actions.description: Force the current primary to step down. Replica set elects a new primary; ~5–15s of write unavailability is typical. Use during planned failover when one secondary is known healthier.
side_effects: Connected drivers reconnect to the new primary.
Primary refuses primary status for the specified period.

args.description: How long the stepped-down node refuses primary.

actions.summary: Refreshes the optimizer statistics for one table. Per-table only; cannot wildcard.

actions.description: Show connected vs max_connections + breakdown by host. Use to check "are we close to connection exhaustion?"

argv: SHOW GLOBAL STATUS LIKE 'Threads_%'; SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES LIKE 'max_connections'; SELECT SUBSTRING_INDEX(host, ':', 1) AS client, count(*) AS conns FROM information_schema.processlist GROUP BY client ORDER BY conns DESC;

actions.title: Database sizes
actions.summary: Show per-database data + index size summary. Read-only.
actions.description: Show per-database data + index size summary. Read-only.

argv: SELECT table_schema, ROUND(SUM(data_length)/1024/1024, 2) AS data_mb, ROUND(SUM(index_length)/1024/1024, 2) AS idx_mb, ROUND(SUM(data_length+index_length)/1024/1024, 2) AS total_mb FROM information_schema.tables GROUP BY table_schema ORDER BY SUM(data_length+index_length) DESC;

actions.title: SHOW ENGINES
actions.summary: Lists available storage engines and which is default. Read-only.
actions.description: Lists available storage engines and which is default. Read-only.

actions.title: FLUSH LOGS
actions.summary: Closes and reopens all log files (general, slow, error, binary). Use after rotating with logrotate.
actions.description: Closes and reopens all log files (general, slow, error, binary). Use after rotating with logrotate.

actions.title: FLUSH STATUS
actions.summary: Resets the GLOBAL STATUS counters to zero. Use before a workload to get a clean rate measurement window.
actions.description: Resets the GLOBAL STATUS counters to zero. Use before a workload to get a clean rate measurement window.
side_effects: Most GLOBAL STATUS counters reset to 0.

actions.title: GTID executed set
actions.summary: `SELECT @@GLOBAL.gtid_executed` — every GTID this server has applied. Use to compare primary vs replica progress.
actions.description: `SELECT @@GLOBAL.gtid_executed` — every GTID this server has applied. Use to compare primary vs replica progress.

actions.title: Unused indexes
actions.summary: List indexes with zero reads since uptime — candidates for dropping (frees disk + speeds writes). Read-only.
actions.description: List indexes with zero reads since uptime — candidates for dropping (frees disk + speeds writes). Read-only.

argv: SELECT object_schema, object_name, index_name FROM performance_schema.table_io_waits_summary_by_index_usage WHERE index_name IS NOT NULL AND count_star = 0 AND object_schema NOT IN ('mysql','performance_schema') ORDER BY object_schema, object_name;

actions.title: SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS
actions.summary: Show full InnoDB engine status — buffer pool, latest deadlock detected, longest waiting transaction, undo space, log sequence number. The canonical "InnoDB is sick" diagnostic.
actions.description: Show full InnoDB engine status — buffer pool, latest deadlock detected, longest waiting transaction, undo space, log sequence number. The canonical "InnoDB is sick" diagnostic.

actions.title: KILL (terminate connection)
actions.summary: Terminates one connection. Use to clean up a stuck/idle session. Client sees "lost connection" and must reconnect.
actions.description: Terminates one connection. Use to clean up a stuck/idle session. Client sees "lost connection" and must reconnect.
side_effects: In-flight transaction on it rolls back.

actions.title: KILL QUERY (cancel statement)
actions.summary: Cancels the currently-executing query on one connection. Connection survives — pair with `kill_connection` to terminate the session entirely.
actions.description: Cancels the currently-executing query on one connection. Connection survives — pair with `kill_connection` to terminate the session entirely.
side_effects: Target connection's current query is aborted.
Connection stays open; subsequent queries can be issued.

actions.title: Lock-wait graph
actions.summary: Joins performance_schema.data_lock_waits with data_locks to show who is blocking whom. Use before a kill_query — you want the blocker, not the victim.
actions.description: Joins performance_schema.data_lock_waits with data_locks to show who is blocking whom. Use before a kill_query — you want the blocker, not the victim.

argv: SELECT r.trx_id AS waiting_trx, r.trx_mysql_thread_id AS waiting_thread, r.trx_query AS waiting_query, b.trx_id AS blocking_trx, b.trx_mysql_thread_id AS blocking_thread, b.trx_query AS blocking_query FROM performance_schema.data_lock_waits w JOIN information_schema.innodb_trx r ON w.REQUESTING_ENGINE_TRANSACTION_ID = r.trx_id JOIN information_schema.innodb_trx b ON w.BLOCKING_ENGINE_TRANSACTION_ID = b.trx_id;

actions.title: SHOW MASTER STATUS
actions.summary: Show current binary log file, position, and GTID set on the primary. Read-only.
actions.description: Show current binary log file, position, and GTID set on the primary. Read-only.

actions.title: SHOW OPEN TABLES
actions.summary: List tables currently in the table cache. Read-only.
actions.description: List tables currently in the table cache. Read-only.

actions.title: OPTIMIZE TABLE
actions.summary: Rebuilds the table to reclaim space and re-sort the clustered index. **Holds an exclusive lock for the duration** on non-InnoDB engines; InnoDB tables get an online ALTER. Per-table only.
actions.description: Rebuilds the table to reclaim space and re-sort the clustered index. **Holds an exclusive lock for the duration** on non-InnoDB engines; InnoDB tables get an online ALTER. Per-table only.

actions.title: SHOW FULL PROCESSLIST
actions.summary: List every active connection with its current command, state, and full query. The first stop for "what is the DB doing?"
actions.description: List every active connection with its current command, state, and full query. The first stop for "what is the DB doing?"

actions.title: SHOW REPLICA STATUS
actions.summary: Reads SHOW REPLICA STATUS (or SHOW SLAVE STATUS on old versions). Surfaces lag, last error, IO and SQL thread state, and binlog position. Returns 'not a replica' cleanly when this is a primary.
actions.description: Reads SHOW REPLICA STATUS (or SHOW SLAVE STATUS on old versions). Surfaces lag, last error, IO and SQL thread state, and binlog position. Returns 'not a replica' cleanly when this is a primary.

argv: mysql -e 'SHOW REPLICA STATUS\G' 2>/dev/null || mysql -e 'SHOW SLAVE STATUS\G' 2>/dev/null

actions.title: Top slow query digests
actions.summary: Reads performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest — top N statement fingerprints by mean execution time. Requires performance_schema enabled.
actions.description: Reads performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest — top N statement fingerprints by mean execution time. Requires performance_schema enabled.

argv: SELECT COUNT_STAR AS calls, ROUND(AVG_TIMER_WAIT/1e9, 2) AS avg_ms, ROUND(SUM_TIMER_WAIT/1e9, 2) AS total_ms, LEFT(DIGEST_TEXT, 200) AS query FROM performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest WHERE DIGEST_TEXT IS NOT NULL ORDER BY AVG_TIMER_WAIT DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }};

actions.title: SHOW GLOBAL STATUS (filtered)
actions.summary: Show global counters filtered by glob pattern. Default returns connection + thread counters.
actions.description: Show global counters filtered by glob pattern. Default returns connection + thread counters.

actions.title: Top tables by IO wait
actions.summary: List top 30 tables ordered by total I/O wait time. Identifies tables driving disk pressure.
actions.description: List top 30 tables ordered by total I/O wait time. Identifies tables driving disk pressure.

argv: SELECT object_schema, object_name, count_read, count_write, ROUND(sum_timer_wait/1e9, 2) AS total_ms FROM performance_schema.table_io_waits_summary_by_table WHERE object_schema NOT IN ('mysql','performance_schema') ORDER BY sum_timer_wait DESC LIMIT 30;

actions.title: Top tables by total size
actions.summary: List top N tables ordered by (data + index) size in bytes. Read-only.
actions.description: List top N tables ordered by (data + index) size in bytes. Read-only.

argv: SELECT table_schema, table_name, table_rows, ROUND(data_length/1024/1024, 2) AS data_mb, ROUND(index_length/1024/1024, 2) AS idx_mb, ROUND((data_length+index_length)/1024/1024, 2) AS total_mb FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema NOT IN ('mysql','information_schema','performance_schema','sys') ORDER BY data_length+index_length DESC LIMIT {{ args.limit }};

actions.title: Uptime + version
actions.summary: Returns SELECT VERSION() and uptime from SHOW STATUS. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns SELECT VERSION() and uptime from SHOW STATUS. Read-only.

actions.title: List users + grants summary
actions.summary: List user + host pairs from mysql.user. Doesn't dump grants (volume) — use for audit "who has accounts?"
actions.description: List user + host pairs from mysql.user. Doesn't dump grants (volume) — use for audit "who has accounts?"

argv: SELECT user, host, plugin, account_locked, password_last_changed FROM mysql.user ORDER BY user, host

actions.title: SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES (filtered)
actions.summary: Show global variables filtered by glob pattern. Default returns InnoDB + buffer settings.
actions.description: Show global variables filtered by glob pattern. Default returns InnoDB + buffer settings.

packs.name: Network + TLS diagnostics
packs.description: DNS lookups, reachability probes, TLS certificate inspection, and HTTP timing tests. All read-only. Use for "is X reachable?" and "what's the cert expiry on Y?" questions.

actions.title: dig +dnssec (DNSSEC validation)
actions.summary: Issues a DNSSEC-validating query. Returns RRSIG records and the AD (authenticated-data) flag if validation succeeded.
actions.description: Issues a DNSSEC-validating query. Returns RRSIG records and the AD (authenticated-data) flag if validation succeeded.
side_effects: One DNS query with DO bit set.

actions.title: dig (DNS lookup)
actions.summary: `dig +short <type> <name> @<resolver>` — look up one DNS record. Type defaults to A; specify AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, SRV. Resolver defaults to system default.
actions.description: `dig +short <type> <name> @<resolver>` — look up one DNS record. Type defaults to A; specify AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, SRV. Resolver defaults to system default.

actions.title: dig -x (reverse DNS)
actions.summary: `dig -x <ip>` — reverse PTR lookup for an IPv4 or IPv6 address.
actions.description: `dig -x <ip>` — reverse PTR lookup for an IPv4 or IPv6 address.

actions.title: dig +trace (full delegation chain)
actions.summary: `dig +trace` — follow the DNS delegation chain from the root to the authoritative server. Use to debug "the wrong nameservers are answering."
actions.description: `dig +trace` — follow the DNS delegation chain from the root to the authoritative server. Use to debug "the wrong nameservers are answering."
side_effects: Multiple DNS queries down the delegation tree.

actions.title: HTTP response headers (curl -I)
actions.summary: Dumps response headers from a URL, following redirects. Use to confirm cache-control / hsts / set-cookie / cors setup. Set insecure=true to skip TLS verification for an internal or self-signed certificate.
actions.description: Dumps response headers from a URL, following redirects. Use to confirm cache-control / hsts / set-cookie / cors setup. Set insecure=true to skip TLS verification for an internal or self-signed certificate.

args.name: insecure
args.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (curl -k). Use for internal or self-signed certs.

actions.title: HTTP probe with timing
actions.summary: Probes a URL with curl, returning HTTP status, DNS / TCP / TLS / total timings, and bytes transferred. Use as a synthetic check. Set insecure=true to skip TLS verification when the endpoint serves an internal or self-signed certificate (otherwise such a probe reports http_code=000).
actions.description: Probes a URL with curl, returning HTTP status, DNS / TCP / TLS / total timings, and bytes transferred. Use as a synthetic check. Set insecure=true to skip TLS verification when the endpoint serves an internal or self-signed certificate (otherwise such a probe reports http_code=000).

args.name: insecure
args.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (curl -k). Use for internal or self-signed certs.

examples.title: Probe an internal endpoint with a self-signed cert

actions.title: ipinfo (geo + ASN for an IP)
actions.summary: Looks up an IP at ipinfo.io. Returns city/region/country/org/asn. Outbound HTTP to ipinfo.io required.
actions.description: Looks up an IP at ipinfo.io. Returns city/region/country/org/asn. Outbound HTTP to ipinfo.io required.

actions.title: ping (with count + timeout)
actions.summary: Sends N ICMP echo requests with explicit per-probe timeout.
actions.description: Sends N ICMP echo requests with explicit per-probe timeout.

actions.title: TLS cert expiry probe
actions.summary: Connects to host:port with SNI and dumps cert subject, issuer, notBefore, notAfter. Use for "when does this cert expire?" alerts.
actions.description: Connects to host:port with SNI and dumps cert subject, issuer, notBefore, notAfter. Use for "when does this cert expire?" alerts.

argv: openssl s_client -connect {{ args.host }}:{{ args.port }} -servername ${SNI:-{{ args.host }}} </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -issuer

actions.title: TLS chain dump
actions.summary: Dumps the full certificate chain a server returns. Use to debug "browsers see incomplete chain" errors.
actions.description: Dumps the full certificate chain a server returns. Use to debug "browsers see incomplete chain" errors.

actions.title: TLS protocol support check
actions.summary: Probes TLS 1.0/1.1/1.2/1.3 support on host:port one at a time, reporting which versions the server accepts. Use for compliance audits ("is TLS 1.0 still on?"). Runs with OPENSSL_CONF=/dev/null so the host's system MinProtocol floor (TLSv1.2 on Debian) doesn't pre-fail the 1.0/1.1 probes — each result reflects the server, not the client.
actions.description: Probes TLS 1.0/1.1/1.2/1.3 support on host:port one at a time, reporting which versions the server accepts. Use for compliance audits ("is TLS 1.0 still on?"). Runs with OPENSSL_CONF=/dev/null so the host's system MinProtocol floor (TLSv1.2 on Debian) doesn't pre-fail the 1.0/1.1 probes — each result reflects the server, not the client.

argv: for v in tls1 tls1_1 tls1_2 tls1_3; do printf '%s: ' "$v"; openssl s_client -connect {{ args.host }}:{{ args.port }} -servername {{ args.host }} -$v </dev/null 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'BEGIN CERT' && echo OK || echo FAIL; done

actions.title: mtr report (route + loss statistics)
actions.summary: `mtr --report` — 5 cycles of traceroute with per-hop packet loss and latency. The combined traceroute + ping report.
actions.description: `mtr --report` — 5 cycles of traceroute with per-hop packet loss and latency. The combined traceroute + ping report.

actions.title: whois (registrar + expiry)
actions.summary: Show filtered whois output — registrar, dates, name servers. Use to check "is this domain about to expire?"
actions.description: Show filtered whois output — registrar, dates, name servers. Use to check "is this domain about to expire?"
side_effects: One whois query to the registry.

argv: whois {{ args.domain }} | grep -iE 'registrar|registry|domain name|expir|name server|status' | head -30

packs.name: NFS server + client
packs.description: Inspect a host's NFS exports, mounted shares, RPC state, active client connections. Plus two narrow operator actions: exportfs -r (re-export) and exportfs -u (unexport one client:path — evicts that client). Read-only otherwise.

actions.title: cat /var/lib/nfs/etab
actions.summary: Show the effective export table (what kernel actually serves).
actions.description: Show the effective export table (what kernel actually serves).

actions.title: exportfs -r
actions.summary: Re-export everything in /etc/exports. New mounts work; established clients may stall briefly.
actions.description: Re-export everything in /etc/exports. New mounts work; established clients may stall briefly.
side_effects: Existing exports table is re-synced from /etc/exports.
Brief stall possible on existing NFS clients.

actions.title: exportfs -u <client>:<path>
actions.summary: Stop exporting one path to one client. Active mounts on that client start failing operations. Use to evict a misbehaving client without restarting the NFS server.
actions.description: Stop exporting one path to one client. Active mounts on that client start failing operations. Use to evict a misbehaving client without restarting the NFS server.
side_effects: Named client can no longer mount the path.
Active mounts return ESTALE for new operations.

actions.title: exportfs -v
actions.summary: List currently-exported NFS shares + options.
actions.description: List currently-exported NFS shares + options.

actions.title: NFS mounts on this host
actions.summary: List all NFS-typed mounts currently visible.
actions.description: List all NFS-typed mounts currently visible.

actions.title: nfsstat -c (client)
actions.summary: Show client-side RPC + NFS op counts.
actions.description: Show client-side RPC + NFS op counts.

actions.title: nfsstat -s (server)
actions.summary: Show server-side RPC + NFS op counts.
actions.description: Show server-side RPC + NFS op counts.

actions.title: rpcinfo -p
actions.summary: List RPC services registered with rpcbind.
actions.description: List RPC services registered with rpcbind.

actions.title: showmount -e
actions.summary: List exports as seen via rpcbind (what NFS clients discover). On NFSv4-only hosts (no mountd/rpcbind) it returns "RPC: Program not registered" — use nfs.exportfs_v for the authoritative export list there.
actions.description: List exports as seen via rpcbind (what NFS clients discover). On NFSv4-only hosts (no mountd/rpcbind) it returns "RPC: Program not registered" — use nfs.exportfs_v for the authoritative export list there.

packs.name: Nginx operations pack
packs.description: Operator pack for nginx — read-only status + access-log analysis, TLS cert probes, and narrow operator actions (test_config, reload, graceful quit, stop). Full restart is intentionally not included; use systemd for that.

actions.title: Top client IPs from access log
actions.summary: Tails the access log and returns the top N source IPs by hit count. Assumes the default combined log format (client IP in column 1). Use to spot abusive crawlers or single-host traffic spikes that hint at a misconfigured client. Read-only.
actions.description: Tails the access log and returns the top N source IPs by hit count. Assumes the default combined log format (client IP in column 1). Use to spot abusive crawlers or single-host traffic spikes that hint at a misconfigured client. Read-only.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many tail lines to analyze.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} {{ args.log_path }} | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -n {{ args.limit }}

actions.title: Top URLs from access log
actions.summary: Tails the access log, extracts the request path, and returns the top N by hit count. Assumes the default combined log format. Use to spot a noisy endpoint, a misbehaving crawler, or to size a cache. Read-only.
actions.description: Tails the access log, extracts the request path, and returns the top N by hit count. Assumes the default combined log format. Use to spot a noisy endpoint, a misbehaving crawler, or to size a cache. Read-only.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many tail lines to analyze.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} {{ args.log_path }} | awk '{print $7}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -n {{ args.limit }}

actions.title: Active nginx version + build
actions.summary: Runs `nginx -V` and returns the version + compile-time flags. Use to confirm which modules are compiled in (e.g. ngx_http_v2, ngx_http_realip) before recommending a config that depends on one. Read-only.
actions.description: Runs `nginx -V` and returns the version + compile-time flags. Use to confirm which modules are compiled in (e.g. ngx_http_v2, ngx_http_realip) before recommending a config that depends on one. Read-only.

actions.title: Top URLs by total bytes sent
actions.summary: List top N URLs ordered by `$body_bytes_sent` summed per URL (column 10). Read-only.
actions.description: List top N URLs ordered by `$body_bytes_sent` summed per URL (column 10). Read-only.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many tail lines to scan.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} /var/log/nginx/access.log | awk '{bytes[$7]+=$10} END{for(u in bytes) printf "%d %s\n", bytes[u], u}' | sort -rn | head -n {{ args.limit }}

actions.title: Compiled-in nginx modules
actions.summary: Parses `nginx -V` to list `--with-*` and `--add-module` flags. Use to confirm a needed module (http_v2, realip, brotli) is present.
actions.description: Parses `nginx -V` to list `--with-*` and `--add-module` flags. Use to confirm a needed module (http_v2, realip, brotli) is present.

actions.title: Full loaded config (nginx -T)
actions.summary: Dumps the loaded config — main + all includes — via `nginx -T`. Sensitive content (passwords in auth_basic_user_file, API keys); output passes through redaction.
actions.description: Dumps the loaded config — main + all includes — via `nginx -T`. Sensitive content (passwords in auth_basic_user_file, API keys); output passes through redaction.

actions.title: Active connections (stub_status)
actions.summary: Hits the stub_status endpoint to get active/reading/writing/waiting counts. Cheaper than `nginx.status` (less parsing). Requires `stub_status on;` enabled. Loopback-only — the host is pinned to 127.0.0.1/localhost/[::1]; only the port and path vary.
actions.description: Hits the stub_status endpoint to get active/reading/writing/waiting counts. Cheaper than `nginx.status` (less parsing). Requires `stub_status on;` enabled. Loopback-only — the host is pinned to 127.0.0.1/localhost/[::1]; only the port and path vary.
side_effects: One curl to the local nginx over loopback.

args.name: url
args.description: stub_status URL. Host is pinned to loopback (127.0.0.1, localhost, or [::1]); pass a non-default port/path here.

actions.title: Tail nginx error log
actions.summary: Returns the last N lines from /var/log/nginx/error.log. Use to spot upstream connect failures, SSL handshake errors, or worker crashes immediately after a reload. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the last N lines from /var/log/nginx/error.log. Use to spot upstream connect failures, SSL handshake errors, or worker crashes immediately after a reload. Read-only.

actions.title: Recent 4xx responses from access log
actions.summary: Greps the access log for 4xx status codes. Read-only.
actions.description: Greps the access log for 4xx status codes. Read-only.

actions.title: Recent 5xx responses from access log
actions.summary: Greps the access log for 5xx status codes (assumes combined log format). Read-only.
actions.description: Greps the access log for 5xx status codes (assumes combined log format). Read-only.

actions.title: nginx master PID
actions.summary: Reads /run/nginx.pid (or the configured pid path). Read-only.
actions.description: Reads /run/nginx.pid (or the configured pid path). Read-only.

actions.title: nginx graceful quit
actions.summary: `nginx -s quit` — workers finish in-flight requests, then exit. No new connections accepted. Use to drain before host shutdown.
actions.description: `nginx -s quit` — workers finish in-flight requests, then exit. No new connections accepted. Use to drain before host shutdown.
side_effects: In-flight requests complete; workers exit when done.

actions.title: nginx reload
actions.summary: Sends SIGHUP to the master process. Master parses the new config, spawns new workers, gracefully drains old workers. If the new config is invalid the master logs the error and keeps the old workers — the request is non-fatal. ALWAYS run `nginx.test_config` first.
actions.description: Sends SIGHUP to the master process. Master parses the new config, spawns new workers, gracefully drains old workers. If the new config is invalid the master logs the error and keeps the old workers — the request is non-fatal. ALWAYS run `nginx.test_config` first.
side_effects: Old workers drain in-flight requests then exit.
Listen-socket changes (new ports, removed ports) take effect.

actions.title: Slowest requests (last N lines)
actions.summary: List top N requests sorted by `$request_time`. Requires a log_format that includes `$request_time` as a specific column; default assumes column 11. Read-only.
actions.description: List top N requests sorted by `$request_time`. Requires a log_format that includes `$request_time` as a specific column; default assumes column 11. Read-only.

args.name: lines
args.description: How many tail lines to scan.

argv: tail -n {{ args.lines }} /var/log/nginx/access.log | awk -v c={{ args.column }} '{print $c, $0}' | sort -rn | head -n {{ args.limit }}

actions.title: TLS cert expiry for one vhost
actions.summary: Probes localhost:443 with SNI to read the cert expiry. Returns notBefore/notAfter and subject/issuer.
actions.description: Probes localhost:443 with SNI to read the cert expiry. Returns notBefore/notAfter and subject/issuer.

args.name: sni
args.description: Server Name Indication (the vhost to probe).

argv: openssl s_client -connect 127.0.0.1:{{ args.port }} -servername {{ args.sni }} </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -issuer

actions.title: TLS chain dump for one vhost
actions.summary: Probes localhost:443 with SNI and dumps the certificate chain. Use to confirm the intermediate cert is being served.
actions.description: Probes localhost:443 with SNI and dumps the certificate chain. Use to confirm the intermediate cert is being served.

actions.title: Nginx stub_status
actions.summary: Fetches the nginx stub_status endpoint over loopback. Returns active connections, total requests, reading/writing/waiting counts. Requires `stub_status on;` in a location block reachable from 127.0.0.1. Loopback-only — the host is pinned to 127.0.0.1/localhost/[::1]; only the port and path vary. Read-only.
actions.description: Fetches the nginx stub_status endpoint over loopback. Returns active connections, total requests, reading/writing/waiting counts. Requires `stub_status on;` in a location block reachable from 127.0.0.1. Loopback-only — the host is pinned to 127.0.0.1/localhost/[::1]; only the port and path vary. Read-only.
side_effects: One curl to the local nginx over loopback.

args.name: url
args.description: stub_status URL on the runner host. Host is pinned to loopback (127.0.0.1, localhost, or [::1]); pass a non-default port/path here.

actions.title: nginx stop (immediate)
actions.summary: `nginx -s stop` — workers stop NOW. In-flight requests are dropped mid-byte. Prefer `nginx.quit_graceful` unless the server is hung.
actions.description: `nginx -s stop` — workers stop NOW. In-flight requests are dropped mid-byte. Prefer `nginx.quit_graceful` unless the server is hung.

actions.title: nginx -t
actions.summary: Runs `nginx -t`. Validates the loaded config without applying. Use before every `nginx.reload`. A failing config returns non-zero exit and the error lines go to stderr.
actions.description: Runs `nginx -t`. Validates the loaded config without applying. Use before every `nginx.reload`. A failing config returns non-zero exit and the error lines go to stderr.
side_effects: Opens files referenced in the config (logs, modules).

actions.title: List configured upstream blocks
actions.summary: Greps `nginx -T` for `upstream` + `server` directives inside upstream blocks. Read-only.
actions.description: Greps `nginx -T` for `upstream` + `server` directives inside upstream blocks. Read-only.

argv: nginx -T 2>/dev/null | awk '/upstream /{p=1; print; next} p && /\}/{p=0; print; next} p'

actions.title: List configured server_name + listen blocks
actions.summary: Greps `nginx -T` output for `server_name` and `listen` directives — a compact view of every virtual host this nginx serves. Read-only.
actions.description: Greps `nginx -T` output for `server_name` and `listen` directives — a compact view of every virtual host this nginx serves. Read-only.

actions.title: Live worker process count
actions.summary: Counts the workers spawned by the master via pgrep. Read-only.
actions.description: Counts the workers spawned by the master via pgrep. Read-only.

packs.name: NIC / ethtool diagnostics
packs.description: Inspect physical network interface cards via ethtool: driver and firmware versions across every NIC (the "what firmware is my i40e fleet running?" check), plus per-interface link settings, hardware counters, offload features, and ring-buffer sizes. Read-only — no ethtool SET operations.

actions.title: ethtool -i <iface>
actions.summary: Show full driver info for one NIC — driver, driver version, firmware-version, expansion-ROM version, bus-info, and the supports-* capability flags.
actions.description: Show full driver info for one NIC — driver, driver version, firmware-version, expansion-ROM version, bus-info, and the supports-* capability flags.

actions.title: ethtool -k <iface>
actions.summary: Show offload feature state for one NIC — checksum offload, GRO/GSO/TSO, scatter-gather, RX/TX hashing, and which are fixed vs tunable. Useful when chasing throughput or checksum-related issues.
actions.description: Show offload feature state for one NIC — checksum offload, GRO/GSO/TSO, scatter-gather, RX/TX hashing, and which are fixed vs tunable. Useful when chasing throughput or checksum-related issues.

actions.title: NIC driver + firmware versions (all interfaces)
actions.summary: List driver, driver version, firmware-version, and PCI bus address for every physical NIC on the host — one line each, from `ethtool -i`. Optionally filter to a single driver (e.g. i40e). The fleet "what firmware is each NIC running?" check.
actions.description: List driver, driver version, firmware-version, and PCI bus address for every physical NIC on the host — one line each, from `ethtool -i`. Optionally filter to a single driver (e.g. i40e). The fleet "what firmware is each NIC running?" check.

args.name: driver
args.description: Only report NICs using this driver (e.g. i40e). Empty = all drivers.

argv:
for d in /sys/class/net/*; do iface=${d##*/}; [ -e "$d/device" ] || continue; info=$(ethtool -i "$iface" 2>/dev/null) || continue; drv=$(printf "%s
" "$info" | sed -n "s/^driver: //p"); [ -n "$DRIVER" ] && [ "$drv" != "$DRIVER" ] && continue; ver=$(printf "%s
" "$info" | sed -n "s/^version: //p"); fw=$(printf "%s
" "$info" | sed -n "s/^firmware-version: //p"); bus=$(printf "%s
" "$info" | sed -n "s/^bus-info: //p"); printf "%-14s driver=%-10s version=%-16s firmware=%-26s bus=%s
" "$iface" "$drv" "$ver" "$fw" "$bus"; done

actions.title: ethtool <iface>
actions.summary: Show link settings for one NIC — negotiated speed and duplex, autoneg, port/medium, and the link-detected flag. The "is this NIC up and at the speed I expect?" check.
actions.description: Show link settings for one NIC — negotiated speed and duplex, autoneg, port/medium, and the link-detected flag. The "is this NIC up and at the speed I expect?" check.

actions.title: ethtool -g <iface>
actions.summary: Show RX/TX ring-buffer sizes for one NIC — the hardware preset maximums and the current settings. Small rings under bursty load show up as rx_dropped in nic.stats.
actions.description: Show RX/TX ring-buffer sizes for one NIC — the hardware preset maximums and the current settings. Small rings under bursty load show up as rx_dropped in nic.stats.

actions.title: ethtool -S <iface>
actions.summary: Show hardware + driver counters for one NIC — per-queue and total rx/tx packets, bytes, errors, drops, and discards. The place to look for packet loss that ip/ifconfig counters don't break out.
actions.description: Show hardware + driver counters for one NIC — per-queue and total rx/tx packets, bytes, errors, drops, and discards. The place to look for packet loss that ip/ifconfig counters don't break out.
side_effects: Read-only (a GET; the kernel allows it without CAP_NET_ADMIN).

actions.title: pm2 describe <name>

actions.description: Show config + runtime details for one process (status, restarts, uptime, script path, resources). Read-only. Per-process environment is projected out — use `pm2.env` for one process's effective env.

actions.description: Tails the last N stderr-only lines for one process.

argv: pm2 jlist | jq 'map({name, pm_id, pid, monit, status: .pm2_env.status, uptime: .pm2_env.pm_uptime, restarts: .pm2_env.restart_time, mode: .pm2_env.exec_mode})'

argv: pm2 jlist | jq 'map({name, pm_id, pid, monit, status: .pm2_env.status, uptime: .pm2_env.pm_uptime, restarts: .pm2_env.restart_time})'

actions.description: Zero-downtime reload — only safe for cluster-mode apps. For fork-mode, behaves like restart.

Fork mode: equivalent to restart — drops in-flight requests.

side_effects: PM2 adds or removes worker processes.

actions.description: Stops one process (PM2 won't auto-restart until you `start`/`restart`).

packs.name: HashiCorp Nomad
packs.description: Deep Nomad operations — full job lifecycle (inspect, history, scale horizontally + vertically, dispatch, revert, promote, stop), per-task CPU/memory resource read + set, allocation introspection + health checks + filesystem ls/stat (metadata only, never file contents) + fixed in-container Redis probes (ping/info via alloc exec, no freeform command) + restart + signal + stop, native service discovery (no Consul) — the high-use reads take optional namespace/region for cross-namespace incident debugging — node fleet management (drain, eligibility, purge), evaluation + deployment status, operator raft + autopilot, CSI + host volumes, ACL policies + tokens, namespaces + quotas. Authenticates via NOMAD_ADDR + NOMAD_TOKEN on the runner host.

actions.description: List all ACL policy names.

actions.description: Show the runner's own token — name, type, policies, expiration. The Secret ID the CLI prints is redacted from the output.

actions.title: nomad alloc checks <id>
actions.summary: Show the Nomad-native service health-check results for one allocation — each check's name, group/task/service, status (success | failure | pending), and output. This is the Nomad-side health view with no Consul: is the alloc's service actually passing its checks, or failing one? Needs the allocation ID (get it from nomad.job_allocations or nomad.alloc_status). Requires a Nomad token with the read-job capability on the namespace.
actions.description: Show the Nomad-native service health-check results for one allocation — each check's name, group/task/service, status (success | failure | pending), and output. This is the Nomad-side health view with no Consul: is the alloc's service actually passing its checks, or failing one? Needs the allocation ID (get it from nomad.job_allocations or nomad.alloc_status). Requires a Nomad token with the read-job capability on the namespace.

actions.title: Show Redis INFO inside an allocation (nomad alloc exec redis-cli info)
actions.summary: Show one section of Redis INFO from inside an allocation's task — runs the fixed command `redis-cli info <section>` via `nomad alloc exec`, with section bounded to the INFO enum.
actions.description: Show one section of Redis INFO from inside an allocation's task — runs the fixed command `redis-cli info <section>` via `nomad alloc exec`, with section bounded to the INFO enum. This is for the incident where Redis is only reachable inside the alloc (no direct REDIS_URL from the runner); use the redis pack's redis.info when you can reach it directly. The command is fixed except the enum section — no freeform command, shell, host, port, or password is accepted, so it reads the task's local redis-cli default (127.0.0.1:6379) and returns NOAUTH on a password-protected instance. risk:medium, not low: `nomad alloc exec` runs inside the running container and INFO exposes memory/stats/ replication topology, so it is policy-gated even though the command only reads. Requires the alloc-exec namespace capability (alloc-node-exec for raw_exec/ raw-driver tasks).
side_effects: Executes the fixed read-only command `redis-cli info <section>` inside the task container.

args.name: alloc_id
args.description: Allocation ID or prefix (from nomad.job_allocations / nomad.alloc_status).

args.name: task
args.description: Task name inside the allocation that runs Redis.

examples.title: Default INFO of Redis in the cache task

actions.title: Ping Redis inside an allocation (nomad alloc exec redis-cli ping)
actions.summary: Check whether the Redis inside one allocation's task is alive — runs the fixed command `redis-cli ping` via `nomad alloc exec` and returns PONG.
actions.description: Check whether the Redis inside one allocation's task is alive — runs the fixed command `redis-cli ping` via `nomad alloc exec` and returns PONG. This is for the incident where Redis is only reachable inside the alloc (no direct REDIS_URL from the runner); use the redis pack's redis.ping when you can reach it directly. The command is fixed — no freeform command, shell, host, port, or password is accepted, so it connects to the task's local redis-cli default (127.0.0.1:6379) and returns NOAUTH on a password-protected instance (itself a signal). risk:medium, not low: `nomad alloc exec` runs inside the running container and can expose internal state, so it is policy-gated even though the command only reads. Requires the alloc-exec namespace capability (alloc-node-exec for raw_exec/raw-driver tasks).
side_effects: Executes the fixed read-only command `redis-cli ping` inside the task container.

args.name: alloc_id
args.description: Allocation ID or prefix (from nomad.job_allocations / nomad.alloc_status).

args.name: task
args.description: Task name inside the allocation that runs Redis.

actions.title: List a directory in an allocation (nomad fs/ls API)
actions.summary: List a directory inside one allocation's filesystem — each entry's name, IsDir, size, file mode, and modtime.
actions.description: List a directory inside one allocation's filesystem — each entry's name, IsDir, size, file mode, and modtime. This is the "what files did my task write to local/ or alloc/logs" read for debugging, with no local CLI session. It calls Nomad's fs/ls API endpoint, which returns directory metadata only and NEVER streams file contents, so it cannot leak a rendered-secret template the way `nomad alloc fs <file>` (cat) would. path defaults to the alloc root (/), is relative to it (Nomad contains it to the alloc dir), and rejects "..", absolute host paths, and shell metacharacters. Requires a Nomad token with the read-fs capability on the namespace.
side_effects: Read-only (directory metadata only — no file contents).

args.name: alloc_id
args.description: Allocation ID or prefix (from nomad.job_allocations / nomad.alloc_status).

args.name: path
args.description: Directory to list, relative to the alloc root (e.g. local, alloc/logs, secrets). Defaults to the alloc root "/". No "..", absolute host paths, or shell metacharacters.

actions.title: Stat a path in an allocation (nomad fs/stat API)
actions.summary: Show the stat metadata of one path inside an allocation's filesystem — name, IsDir, size, file mode, modtime, and content type.
actions.description: Show the stat metadata of one path inside an allocation's filesystem — name, IsDir, size, file mode, modtime, and content type. This is the "does this file exist / how big is it / when was it written" read. It calls Nomad's fs/stat API endpoint, which returns metadata only and NEVER streams file contents, so it cannot leak a rendered-secret template the way `nomad alloc fs <file>` (cat) would. path is relative to the alloc root (Nomad contains it to the alloc dir) and rejects "..", absolute host paths, and shell metacharacters. Requires a Nomad token with the read-fs capability on the namespace.
side_effects: Read-only (file metadata only — no file contents).

args.name: alloc_id
args.description: Allocation ID or prefix (from nomad.job_allocations / nomad.alloc_status).

args.name: path
args.description: Path to stat, relative to the alloc root (e.g. local/app.log, secrets/.env). No "..", absolute host paths, or shell metacharacters.

actions.title: Snapshot recent Nomad events (bounded /v1/event/stream)
actions.summary: Show a bounded snapshot of the Nomad event stream — recent Job, Allocation, Evaluation, Deployment, and Node events for incident triage, with no local CLI session.
actions.description: Show a bounded snapshot of the Nomad event stream — recent Job, Allocation, Evaluation, Deployment, and Node events for incident triage, with no local CLI session. Nomad has no "last N events" query: /v1/event/stream is a forward feed whose only history is the broker's replay buffer (~100 events). This reads it from index=1 (replays the buffer, then live events) for `seconds` seconds, capped at 256 KiB, then returns — so it never hangs. Optionally filter to one `topic` or one `namespace`. Output is NDJSON: one {Index, Events:[...]} batch per line. Requires a token that can read the event stream.
side_effects: Opens the Nomad event stream for a bounded time window.

args.name: seconds
args.description: How long to read the stream — the snapshot window, in seconds.

args.name: topic
args.description: Filter to one event topic (empty = all topics). One of Job, Allocation, Evaluation, Deployment, Node, Service, or an ACL topic.

args.name: namespace
args.description: Filter to one namespace (empty = the runner's ambient/default).
