actions.description: Show aggregated memory stats — Rss, Pss, Shared, Private. Better than RSS for diagnosing memory pressure.

actions.title: /proc/PID/status
actions.summary: Show full /proc/PID/status — uid/gid, capability set, signal state, oom score.
actions.description: Show full /proc/PID/status — uid/gid, capability set, signal state, oom score.

actions.title: /proc/PID/syscall
actions.summary: Show current syscall (or "running" if user-space). Snapshot of where the kernel is for one thread.
actions.description: Show current syscall (or "running" if user-space). Snapshot of where the kernel is for one thread.

actions.title: Per-thread state for one PID
actions.summary: Tabulates each thread's state (R/S/D/Z) from /proc/PID/task/*/status.
actions.description: Tabulates each thread's state (R/S/D/Z) from /proc/PID/task/*/status.

argv: for f in /proc/{{ args.pid }}/task/*/status; do tid=$(basename $(dirname $f)); awk -v tid=$tid '/^State:/ {print tid, $2, $3}' $f; done | sort -k2

actions.title: strace -p <pid> (5 seconds)
actions.summary: Attach strace to one PID for 5 seconds. Slows the target process significantly while attached. Detaches automatically.
actions.description: Attach strace to one PID for 5 seconds. Slows the target process significantly while attached. Detaches automatically.
side_effects: Target process runs ~5-20x slower during the strace window.
On detach, target resumes normal speed.

actions.title: strace -c -p <pid> (5 seconds)
actions.summary: Attach strace in counting mode for 5 seconds — outputs a syscall-frequency table. Slows target less than -y mode but still significant.
actions.description: Attach strace in counting mode for 5 seconds — outputs a syscall-frequency table. Slows target less than -y mode but still significant.
side_effects: Target process runs ~3-10x slower during the window.

packs.name: Prometheus operations
packs.description: Server status, target health, alertmanager linkage, instant + range queries, rule listing, TSDB stats, plus admin-API actions for remediation: reload config, take snapshot, clean tombstones, delete series. Admin endpoints require --web.enable-admin-api + --web.enable-lifecycle.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/alertmanagers
actions.summary: List configured Alertmanager endpoints + their reachability.
actions.description: List configured Alertmanager endpoints + their reachability.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/alerts
actions.summary: List currently active alerts (pending or firing).
actions.description: List currently active alerts (pending or firing).

actions.title: GET /api/v1/status/buildinfo
actions.summary: Show Prometheus version + build details.
actions.description: Show Prometheus version + build details.

actions.title: POST /api/v1/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones
actions.summary: Reclaim disk space after a delete_series call. Tombstones are removed and the data they marked is permanently deleted.
actions.description: Reclaim disk space after a delete_series call. Tombstones are removed and the data they marked is permanently deleted.
side_effects: Tombstoned series permanently deleted from disk.

actions.title: POST /api/v1/admin/tsdb/delete_series
actions.summary: Mark series matching the matcher as deleted (tombstoned). Series no longer return from queries. Run clean_tombstones afterwards to actually reclaim disk. Use to remove cardinality-explosion mistakes or accidentally-recorded secrets. Match pattern is restricted to safe label-selector characters.
actions.description: Mark series matching the matcher as deleted (tombstoned). Series no longer return from queries. Run clean_tombstones afterwards to actually reclaim disk. Use to remove cardinality-explosion mistakes or accidentally-recorded secrets. Match pattern is restricted to safe label-selector characters.

args.name: matcher
args.description: Label matcher (URL-encoded if it contains special chars).

actions.title: GET /api/v1/status/flags
actions.summary: Show server command-line flags.
actions.description: Show server command-line flags.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/query
actions.summary: Run an instant PromQL query. Use to answer 'what's X right now?'.
actions.description: Run an instant PromQL query. Use to answer 'what's X right now?'.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/query_range
actions.summary: Run a range PromQL query (one expr, over a window). Use for trends.
actions.description: Run a range PromQL query (one expr, over a window). Use for trends.
side_effects: One PromQL range query — can be expensive on large series.

argv: U=${W##*[0-9]}; N=${W%[smhd]}; case $U in s) M=1;; m) M=60;; h) M=3600;; d) M=86400;; esac; END=$(date -u +%s); START=$((END - N*M)); curl -sS -G --data-urlencode "query=$Q" --data-urlencode "start=$START" --data-urlencode "end=$END" --data-urlencode "step=$S" "${PROM_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:9090}/api/v1/query_range"

actions.title: POST /-/reload
actions.summary: Trigger Prometheus to reload its config + rule files in place. Requires --web.enable-lifecycle. If the new config is invalid, reload fails and Prometheus keeps the old one.
actions.description: Trigger Prometheus to reload its config + rule files in place. Requires --web.enable-lifecycle. If the new config is invalid, reload fails and Prometheus keeps the old one.
side_effects: Failure leaves the old config in place.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/rules
actions.summary: List all loaded recording + alerting rules with last evaluation result.
actions.description: List all loaded recording + alerting rules with last evaluation result.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/status/runtimeinfo
actions.summary: Show uptime, last config reload, chunk count, WAL corrected/unprocessed.
actions.description: Show uptime, last config reload, chunk count, WAL corrected/unprocessed.

actions.title: POST /api/v1/admin/tsdb/snapshot
actions.summary: Trigger an on-disk snapshot of the TSDB into data/snapshots/. Requires --web.enable-admin-api. Used before risky upgrades or for offline diff. Uses hardlinks initially — no extra disk until blocks rotate.
actions.description: Trigger an on-disk snapshot of the TSDB into data/snapshots/. Requires --web.enable-admin-api. Used before risky upgrades or for offline diff. Uses hardlinks initially — no extra disk until blocks rotate.
side_effects: Disk usage grows as blocks change over time.

args.name: skip_head
args.description: Skip the in-memory head block (faster, less complete).

actions.title: GET /api/v1/targets
actions.summary: List all scrape targets with state (up/down) and last error.
actions.description: List all scrape targets with state (up/down) and last error.

actions.title: GET /api/v1/status/tsdb
actions.summary: Show the top-10 cardinal label/value pairs in the TSDB. Use to spot cardinality explosion.
actions.description: Show the top-10 cardinal label/value pairs in the TSDB. Use to spot cardinality explosion.

packs.name: Pure Storage FlashArray (Purity//FA REST)
packs.description: Read-only access to a Pure Storage FlashArray over its Purity//FA REST API 2.x: array identity / capacity / performance, alerts, controller and hardware / drive health, volumes and per-volume space, the host -> volume -> LUN connection map, host and target-port identities, per-controller network interface state, and replication / array-connection status. Drives a remote array via curl; every call is a GET.

actions.title: GET /alerts
actions.summary: List open (unresolved) array alerts, filtered to state='open' — each with its severity (info / warning / critical), component, and summary. Use to answer "what is the array complaining about right now?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per open alert.
actions.description: List open (unresolved) array alerts, filtered to state='open' — each with its severity (info / warning / critical), component, and summary. Use to answer "what is the array complaining about right now?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per open alert.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes, acknowledges, or clears alerts.

actions.title: GET /array-connections
actions.summary: List connected-array / replication status — each peer array this array is connected to, the connection type (async / sync replication), and its status. Use to confirm replication peers are connected and healthy. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per connected array.
actions.description: List connected-array / replication status — each peer array this array is connected to, the connection type (async / sync replication), and its status. Use to confirm replication peers are connected and healthy. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per connected array.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /arrays
actions.summary: Show array identity and top-line health — array name, id, Purity//FA OS version, and the headline capacity fields. The starting point for "which array is this and is it healthy?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}; one item per array.
actions.description: Show array identity and top-line health — array name, id, Purity//FA OS version, and the headline capacity fields. The starting point for "which array is this and is it healthy?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}; one item per array.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /arrays/performance
actions.summary: Show array-wide performance — the latest sample of read/write/mirrored latency, IOPS, and bandwidth. Use to answer "is the array slow right now?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}; the single item is the most recent real-time sample for the array.
actions.description: Show array-wide performance — the latest sample of read/write/mirrored latency, IOPS, and bandwidth. Use to answer "is the array slow right now?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}; the single item is the most recent real-time sample for the array.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /arrays/space
actions.summary: Show array-wide space accounting — total and used capacity, plus the data reduction and thin-provisioning ratios behind it. Use to answer "how full is the array and what is the effective reduction?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...} with a space breakdown per array.
actions.description: Show array-wide space accounting — total and used capacity, plus the data reduction and thin-provisioning ratios behind it. Use to answer "how full is the array and what is the effective reduction?". Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...} with a space breakdown per array.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /connections
actions.summary: Show the host -> volume -> LUN map: every connection between a host (or host group) and a volume, with the LUN the volume is presented at. This is the authoritative answer to "which host sees which volume, at which LUN?" — the first stop for any "my server can't see its LUN" or "is this volume even mapped?" question. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per host/volume connection; can be large on a busy array.
actions.description: Show the host -> volume -> LUN map: every connection between a host (or host group) and a volume, with the LUN the volume is presented at. This is the authoritative answer to "which host sees which volume, at which LUN?" — the first stop for any "my server can't see its LUN" or "is this volume even mapped?" question. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per host/volume connection; can be large on a busy array.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /controllers
actions.summary: List the array's controllers — for each, its mode (primary / secondary), model, running Purity//FA version, and status. Use to confirm the HA pair is healthy and which controller is primary. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per controller.
actions.description: List the array's controllers — for each, its mode (primary / secondary), model, running Purity//FA version, and status. Use to confirm the HA pair is healthy and which controller is primary. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per controller.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /drives
actions.summary: List flash and NVRAM modules — for each drive, its type, capacity, and status (healthy / unhealthy / evacuating / unused). Use to find a failed or evacuating module. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per drive.
actions.description: List flash and NVRAM modules — for each drive, its type, capacity, and status (healthy / unhealthy / evacuating / unused). Use to find a failed or evacuating module. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per drive.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /hardware
actions.summary: Show physical component health — chassis, power supplies, fans, temperature sensors, and other hardware items, each with its status and (where applicable) reading. Use to find a failed PSU/fan or a hot sensor. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per component.
actions.description: Show physical component health — chassis, power supplies, fans, temperature sensors, and other hardware items, each with its status and (where applicable) reading. Use to find a failed PSU/fan or a hot sensor. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per component.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /hosts
actions.summary: List the host inventory — each host object and the initiator identities registered to it: iSCSI IQNs, Fibre Channel WWNs, and NVMe NQNs. Use to confirm an initiator is registered to the expected host. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per host.
actions.description: List the host inventory — each host object and the initiator identities registered to it: iSCSI IQNs, Fibre Channel WWNs, and NVMe NQNs. Use to confirm an initiator is registered to the expected host. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per host.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /network-interfaces
actions.summary: List per-controller network interfaces — each interface's enabled / up-or-down state, speed, address, and the services it carries (management, iSCSI, replication, NVMe-oF). Use this to answer "is the target port up?" before chasing a host-side path problem. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per interface.
actions.description: List per-controller network interfaces — each interface's enabled / up-or-down state, speed, address, and the services it carries (management, iSCSI, replication, NVMe-oF). Use this to answer "is the target port up?" before chasing a host-side path problem. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per interface.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /ports
actions.summary: List target-port identities — the array-side iSCSI IQNs, Fibre Channel WWNs, and NVMe NQNs, with iSCSI portal IPs where applicable. Use to learn what addresses a host should be connecting to. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per target port.
actions.description: List target-port identities — the array-side iSCSI IQNs, Fibre Channel WWNs, and NVMe NQNs, with iSCSI portal IPs where applicable. Use to learn what addresses a host should be connecting to. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per target port.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

examples.title: iSCSI / FC / NVMe target port identities

actions.title: GET /volumes
actions.summary: List the volume inventory — each volume's name, id, provisioned (virtual) size, and whether it is destroyed / pending eradication. Use to confirm a volume exists and its size. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per volume; can be large on arrays with many volumes.
actions.description: List the volume inventory — each volume's name, id, provisioned (virtual) size, and whether it is destroyed / pending eradication. Use to confirm a volume exists and its size. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per volume; can be large on arrays with many volumes.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

actions.title: GET /volumes/space
actions.summary: Show per-volume space accounting — for each volume, the physical space used by its unique data and by snapshots, plus its data-reduction and total-reduction ratios. Use to find which volumes consume the most capacity. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per volume.
actions.description: Show per-volume space accounting — for each volume, the physical space used by its unique data and by snapshots, plus its data-reduction and total-reduction ratios. Use to find which volumes consume the most capacity. Returns {items:[...], more_items_remaining, ...}, one item per volume.
side_effects: One read-only HTTP GET to the FlashArray REST API.
Read-only — never writes or mutates array state.

packs.name: Python application runtime
packs.description: Inspect a Python deployment — interpreter + venv state, pip inventory + freeze, dependency conflicts, outdated packages, sys.path. Read-only. Most actions act on the venv at PY_VENV env var (default /opt/app/venv).

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip cache info
actions.summary: Show pip cache size + location.
actions.description: Show pip cache size + location.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip check
actions.summary: Check for dependency conflicts. Empty output means clean.
actions.description: Check for dependency conflicts. Empty output means clean.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip freeze
actions.summary: Dump installed packages in requirements format.
actions.description: Dump installed packages in requirements format.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip list
actions.summary: List installed packages with versions.
actions.description: List installed packages with versions.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip list --outdated
actions.summary: List packages with newer versions available.
actions.description: List packages with newer versions available.
side_effects: Queries PyPI (or configured index) for latest versions.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/pip show <pkg>
actions.summary: Show details for one package (version, deps, location).
actions.description: Show details for one package (version, deps, location).

actions.title: python3 --version (system)
actions.summary: Show the system python interpreter version.
actions.description: Show the system python interpreter version.

actions.title: du -sh site-packages/*
actions.summary: Show disk usage per installed package — find bloated installs.
actions.description: Show disk usage per installed package — find bloated installs.
side_effects: One file-system traversal of the venv's site-packages.

argv: V=${PY_VENV:-/opt/app/venv}; SP=$("$V/bin/python" -c 'import site; print(site.getsitepackages()[0])'); du -sh "$SP"/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -50

actions.title: python -c 'import sys; print(sys.path)'
actions.summary: Show the effective sys.path for the venv interpreter.
actions.description: Show the effective sys.path for the venv interpreter.

actions.title: $PY_VENV/bin/python --version
actions.summary: Show the interpreter version inside the configured venv.
actions.description: Show the interpreter version inside the configured venv.

packs.name: RabbitMQ operations
packs.description: Cluster, queue, exchange, binding, connection, channel, and consumer inventory plus narrow operator actions (purge_queue, set_policy, force_close_connection). Uses rabbitmqctl + rabbitmqadmin on the runner host.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl close_connection
actions.summary: Forcibly closes one AMQP connection. Whatever client owned it must reconnect.
actions.description: Forcibly closes one AMQP connection. Whatever client owned it must reconnect.
side_effects: Connection is dropped with the given reason.
In-flight unacked messages on its channels go back to the queue.

args.name: note
args.description: Explanation recorded with the connection close.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl cluster_status
actions.summary: Show cluster summary: nodes, alarms, partitions.
actions.description: Show cluster summary: nodes, alarms, partitions.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_bindings
actions.summary: List all bindings between exchanges and queues.
actions.description: List all bindings between exchanges and queues.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_channels
actions.summary: List all open channels with consumer + prefetch + ack stats.
actions.description: List all open channels with consumer + prefetch + ack stats.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_connections
actions.summary: List all open AMQP connections with client + user + channels.
actions.description: List all open AMQP connections with client + user + channels.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_consumers
actions.summary: List all active consumers + their queue + ack-mode + prefetch.
actions.description: List all active consumers + their queue + ack-mode + prefetch.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_exchanges
actions.summary: List all exchanges with type + durability.
actions.description: List all exchanges with type + durability.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_nodes
actions.summary: List all known cluster nodes.
actions.description: List all known cluster nodes.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_parameters
actions.summary: List runtime parameters (federation upstreams, shovel definitions, etc).
actions.description: List runtime parameters (federation upstreams, shovel definitions, etc).

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_policies
actions.summary: List all set policies (HA, federation, shovel, etc).
actions.description: List all set policies (HA, federation, shovel, etc).

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_queues
actions.summary: List all queues with name, messages_ready, messages_unacknowledged, consumers, memory. The canonical "is anything backing up?" check.
actions.description: List all queues with name, messages_ready, messages_unacknowledged, consumers, memory. The canonical "is anything backing up?" check.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_users
actions.summary: List all users + tags.
actions.description: List all users + tags.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl list_vhosts
actions.summary: List all virtual hosts.
actions.description: List all virtual hosts.

actions.title: rabbitmq-diagnostics check_running
actions.summary: Check health — confirms the local node is running and reachable.
actions.description: Check health — confirms the local node is running and reachable.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl purge_queue
actions.summary: Drops every message in one queue. Unprocessed messages are lost.
actions.description: Drops every message in one queue. Unprocessed messages are lost.
side_effects: All messages in the queue (including unacked-in-flight) are deleted.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl start_app
actions.summary: Start the RabbitMQ application within an already-running node. Companion to stop_app, used during cluster joins/leaves.
actions.description: Start the RabbitMQ application within an already-running node. Companion to stop_app, used during cluster joins/leaves.
side_effects: RabbitMQ app accepts client connections again.
Queues and exchanges from disk become available.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl stop_app
actions.summary: Stop the RabbitMQ application while keeping the Erlang node running. Required before reset/join_cluster. All clients disconnect.
actions.description: Stop the RabbitMQ application while keeping the Erlang node running. Required before reset/join_cluster. All clients disconnect.
side_effects: Producers fail; consumers reconnect when start_app runs.

actions.title: rabbitmqctl sync_queue
actions.summary: Force a mirrored classic queue to sync with its master. Producers and consumers continue to operate; the sync runs in the background. Use when an unsynced mirror is blocking a master failover.
actions.description: Force a mirrored classic queue to sync with its master. Producers and consumers continue to operate; the sync runs in the background. Use when an unsynced mirror is blocking a master failover.
side_effects: Mirror catches up with the master.

packs.name: Redis operations pack
packs.description: Deep Redis ops — INFO + memory accounting, slowlog + per-event latency, command stats, client list/kill/pause, keyspace introspection (SCAN, TYPE, TTL, OBJECT encoding/refcount/idle/freq, MEMORY USAGE), config get/set/rewrite/resetstat, ACL list/whoami/getuser, cluster topology (info, nodes, slots, slot-count, check), cluster operators (failover, forget, replicaof), Sentinel HA topology (masters, master, replicas, sentinels, get-master-addr, ckquorum, is-master-down, Sentinel INFO) and operators (failover, reset), persistence (lastsave, bgsave, bgrewriteaof, memory purge/doctor), streams + pub/sub introspection, script cache flush, and tier-critical actions (flush_db, flushall, swapdb, shutdown_nosave). Sentinel actions target the Sentinel on port 26379; all others default to 127.0.0.1:6379. Authenticates via REDISCLI_AUTH / REDIS_* env vars on the runner host — never via per-call credentials.

actions.title: ACL GETUSER
actions.summary: Get the full rule listing for one ACL user — categories, commands, key patterns, channel patterns, flags. Read-only.
actions.description: Get the full rule listing for one ACL user — categories, commands, key patterns, channel patterns, flags. Read-only.

actions.title: ACL LIST
actions.summary: List all ACL users and their rules. Read-only. Use to audit access before changing a key namespace.
actions.description: List all ACL users and their rules. Read-only. Use to audit access before changing a key namespace.

actions.title: ACL WHOAMI
actions.summary: Show the ACL username of the current connection. Use to verify the runner credential is the expected service principal.
actions.description: Show the ACL username of the current connection. Use to verify the runner credential is the expected service principal.

actions.title: BGREWRITEAOF
actions.summary: Rewrite the AOF file in the background, compacting it to the smallest equivalent command set. Reduces AOF size and recovery time. Brief fork. Concurrent BGSAVE will queue.
actions.description: Rewrite the AOF file in the background, compacting it to the smallest equivalent command set. Reduces AOF size and recovery time. Brief fork. Concurrent BGSAVE will queue.
side_effects: One fork; brief CPU + memory spike.
Writes a new AOF file then renames into place.
Concurrent BGSAVE will queue behind this.

actions.title: BGSAVE
actions.summary: Trigger a background RDB snapshot. The fork briefly doubles RSS (copy-on-write). Returns immediately — completion shows in INFO rdb_last_bgsave_status. Concurrent BGREWRITEAOF will queue.
actions.description: Trigger a background RDB snapshot. The fork briefly doubles RSS (copy-on-write). Returns immediately — completion shows in INFO rdb_last_bgsave_status. Concurrent BGREWRITEAOF will queue.
side_effects: One fork; brief CPU + memory spike.
Writes an RDB file to disk.
Concurrent BGREWRITEAOF will queue behind this.

actions.title: CLIENT KILL
actions.summary: Disconnects one client by addr or by id. Use to evict a stuck client with a runaway output buffer, an abandoned subscriber, or a client exceeding the slowlog. Confirm the target via `client_list` first — killing the wrong client can disrupt a critical caller.
actions.description: Disconnects one client by addr or by id. Use to evict a stuck client with a runaway output buffer, an abandoned subscriber, or a client exceeding the slowlog. Confirm the target via `client_list` first — killing the wrong client can disrupt a critical caller.
side_effects: In-flight commands on that connection are not replied to.

args.name: mode
args.description: Whether `target` is an ADDR or an ID.

actions.title: CLIENT LIST
actions.summary: Returns every connected client with id, addr, name, age, idle, db, sub, psub, multi, qbuf, obl, oll, omem, cmd, fd. Use to find the client holding a long subscription, a runaway pipeline (large omem), or to identify candidate connections for CLIENT KILL.
actions.description: Returns every connected client with id, addr, name, age, idle, db, sub, psub, multi, qbuf, obl, oll, omem, cmd, fd. Use to find the client holding a long subscription, a runaway pipeline (large omem), or to identify candidate connections for CLIENT KILL.

actions.title: CLIENT PAUSE
actions.summary: Pauses all clients for N milliseconds. Used during safe failover windows to drain in-flight writes before flipping a master. Long pauses block all traffic — anything over 1s is a real outage. Replicas keep streaming during the pause.
actions.description: Pauses all clients for N milliseconds. Used during safe failover windows to drain in-flight writes before flipping a master. Long pauses block all traffic — anything over 1s is a real outage. Replicas keep streaming during the pause.
side_effects: All clients stalled at the server side.
Memory may grow if clients keep sending.

actions.title: redis-cli --cluster check
actions.summary: `redis-cli --cluster check` against the given host:port — verifies slot coverage, master/replica health, epoch consistency. Use after any topology change.
actions.description: `redis-cli --cluster check` against the given host:port — verifies slot coverage, master/replica health, epoch consistency. Use after any topology change.
side_effects: Talks to every cluster node briefly.

actions.title: CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT
actions.summary: Count keys mapped to a single hash slot. Use when a slot appears hot in monitoring to size the blast radius of a migration or to verify a tagged keyspace is collocated.
actions.description: Count keys mapped to a single hash slot. Use when a slot appears hot in monitoring to size the blast radius of a migration or to verify a tagged keyspace is collocated.

actions.title: CLUSTER FAILOVER
actions.summary: Promotes the connected REPLICA to master. Default mode (empty) waits for replication parity — the only safe choice during a planned failover. FORCE skips parity (use only when master is suspected dead). TAKEOVER bypasses cluster consensus entirely (split-brain recovery). Run while connected to a REPLICA, not a master.
actions.description: Promotes the connected REPLICA to master. Default mode (empty) waits for replication parity — the only safe choice during a planned failover. FORCE skips parity (use only when master is suspected dead). TAKEOVER bypasses cluster consensus entirely (split-brain recovery). Run while connected to a REPLICA, not a master.
side_effects: The previous master becomes a replica.
Brief slot-ownership flip; some commands may transiently fail.
Other replicas reconfigure to follow the new master.

actions.title: CLUSTER FORGET
actions.summary: Remove a node from the connected node's cluster view for 60s. Used during node replacement. Run on every reachable node to fully evict the old member. Wrong target id produces a brief split view until gossip refreshes.
actions.description: Remove a node from the connected node's cluster view for 60s. Used during node replacement. Run on every reachable node to fully evict the old member. Wrong target id produces a brief split view until gossip refreshes.
side_effects: The connected node drops the named member from its view.
Effect lasts 60 seconds; gossip may re-discover an alive node.
Run on every node to permanently evict.

actions.title: CLUSTER INFO
actions.summary: Show cluster status — state ok/fail, slots assigned/ok/pfail/fail, known nodes, size, epoch, my epoch, messages sent/received.
actions.description: Show cluster status — state ok/fail, slots assigned/ok/pfail/fail, known nodes, size, epoch, my epoch, messages sent/received.

actions.title: CLUSTER NODES
actions.summary: List the cluster nodes — id, addr, flags (master/replica/myself/fail), master id, last ping/pong, config epoch, link state, slot ranges. Use to see who owns which slot ranges and which replicas serve which masters.
actions.description: List the cluster nodes — id, addr, flags (master/replica/myself/fail), master id, last ping/pong, config epoch, link state, slot ranges. Use to see who owns which slot ranges and which replicas serve which masters.

actions.title: CLUSTER SLOTS
actions.summary: Show the slot-range → master/replica mapping. Use to confirm a given hash slot is owned by the expected shard and to see replica addresses for read traffic.
actions.description: Show the slot-range → master/replica mapping. Use to confirm a given hash slot is owned by the expected shard and to see replica addresses for read traffic.

actions.title: INFO commandstats
actions.summary: Returns per-command call count, total µs spent, and µs per call. Use to identify hot commands (e.g. an unexpectedly high KEYS rate or a SUBSCRIBE storm). Read-only.
actions.description: Returns per-command call count, total µs spent, and µs per call. Use to identify hot commands (e.g. an unexpectedly high KEYS rate or a SUBSCRIBE storm). Read-only.

actions.title: CONFIG GET
actions.summary: Reads runtime config by glob pattern. Useful before any tuning conversation: confirm `maxmemory`, `maxmemory-policy`, `appendonly`, `save`, etc. Read-only. Pattern is restricted to safe globs against Redis config keys. Secret values (requirepass, masterauth) are masked in the output — the key stays visible so you can see it is set.
actions.description: Reads runtime config by glob pattern. Useful before any tuning conversation: confirm `maxmemory`, `maxmemory-policy`, `appendonly`, `save`, etc. Read-only. Pattern is restricted to safe globs against Redis config keys. Secret values (requirepass, masterauth) are masked in the output — the key stays visible so you can see it is set.

actions.title: CONFIG RESETSTAT
actions.summary: Reset INFO stats counters (commandstats, keyspace hits/misses, evictions, etc.). Does NOT change keyspace data, but it DOES mutate server state by zeroing the counters — so it is risk:medium (policy-gated), like the other non-keyspace mutators (memory_purge, script_flush). Use to start a fresh measurement window.
actions.description: Reset INFO stats counters (commandstats, keyspace hits/misses, evictions, etc.). Does NOT change keyspace data, but it DOES mutate server state by zeroing the counters — so it is risk:medium (policy-gated), like the other non-keyspace mutators (memory_purge, script_flush). Use to start a fresh measurement window.

actions.title: CONFIG REWRITE
actions.summary: Persist all runtime CONFIG SET changes back to the on-disk redis.conf. Without this, runtime overrides revert at restart.
actions.description: Persist all runtime CONFIG SET changes back to the on-disk redis.conf. Without this, runtime overrides revert at restart.
side_effects: Rewrites redis.conf in place (preserves comments, updates values).

actions.title: CONFIG SET
actions.summary: Change one runtime config parameter. Effects are immediate and may include eviction policy, persistence mode, replication behavior. Combine with config_rewrite to persist. Value pattern is restricted to safe characters.
actions.description: Change one runtime config parameter. Effects are immediate and may include eviction policy, persistence mode, replication behavior. Combine with config_rewrite to persist. Value pattern is restricted to safe characters.
side_effects: Some parameters affect persistence, replication, eviction.
Not persisted across restart without CONFIG REWRITE.

actions.title: DBSIZE
actions.summary: Returns the number of keys in the currently selected database. O(1). Read-only. Use as a sanity check before running operations that scan the keyspace.
actions.description: Returns the number of keys in the currently selected database. O(1). Read-only. Use as a sanity check before running operations that scan the keyspace.

actions.title: FLUSHDB (single database)
actions.summary: Deletes every key in one logical database. Irreversible. Use only on caches and only when you know there is no replication consumer that treats an empty DB as a fault. ASYNC mode returns immediately and drops the data in a background thread; SYNC blocks until done. Never use against production user data — this exists for cache resets and dev environments.
actions.description: Deletes every key in one logical database. Irreversible. Use only on caches and only when you know there is no replication consumer that treats an empty DB as a fault. ASYNC mode returns immediately and drops the data in a background thread; SYNC blocks until done. Never use against production user data — this exists for cache resets and dev environments.
side_effects: All keys in the selected database are deleted.
No way to recover except by restore.

actions.title: FLUSHALL
actions.summary: Deletes EVERY key from EVERY database. Irreversible. Replicas FLUSHALL in turn. Only use on caches that can be cold-rebuilt.
actions.description: Deletes EVERY key from EVERY database. Irreversible. Replicas FLUSHALL in turn. Only use on caches that can be cold-rebuilt.

args.name: mode
args.description: SYNC (block) or ASYNC (return, drop in background).

actions.title: Redis INFO section
actions.summary: Runs `INFO <section>` and returns the raw text. Sections: server, clients, memory, persistence, stats, replication, cpu, commandstats, latencystats, cluster, keyspace, errorstats, all. Read-only.
actions.description: Runs `INFO <section>` and returns the raw text. Sections: server, clients, memory, persistence, stats, replication, cpu, commandstats, latencystats, cluster, keyspace, errorstats, all. Read-only.

actions.title: LASTSAVE
actions.summary: Show the Unix timestamp of the last successful background save (RDB). Use to confirm the persistence schedule is firing.
actions.description: Show the Unix timestamp of the last successful background save (RDB). Use to confirm the persistence schedule is firing.

actions.title: LATENCY LATEST + HISTORY
actions.summary: Runs `LATENCY LATEST` to summarize per-event latency spikes (fork, AOF write, expire, eviction, command, etc.). Requires `latency-monitor-threshold` to be set non-zero in redis.conf; if it isn't, the output is empty and that is a configuration finding worth reporting. Read-only.
actions.description: Runs `LATENCY LATEST` to summarize per-event latency spikes (fork, AOF write, expire, eviction, command, etc.). Requires `latency-monitor-threshold` to be set non-zero in redis.conf; if it isn't, the output is empty and that is a configuration finding worth reporting. Read-only.

actions.title: LATENCY HISTORY
actions.summary: Show per-event latency time series (e.g. fork, aof-write, expire). Use after LATENCY LATEST to drill into one event class.
actions.description: Show per-event latency time series (e.g. fork, aof-write, expire). Use after LATENCY LATEST to drill into one event class.

actions.title: MEMORY DOCTOR
actions.summary: Runs the built-in memory advisor. Returns a human-readable summary of suspected issues (fragmentation, oversized clients, large peak vs current).
actions.description: Runs the built-in memory advisor. Returns a human-readable summary of suspected issues (fragmentation, oversized clients, large peak vs current).

actions.title: MEMORY PURGE
actions.summary: Asks jemalloc to release unused memory back to the OS. Use after a large eviction or DEL spike when RSS hasn't dropped despite used_memory falling. Brief allocator pause possible.
actions.description: Asks jemalloc to release unused memory back to the OS. Use after a large eviction or DEL spike when RSS hasn't dropped despite used_memory falling. Brief allocator pause possible.

actions.title: MEMORY STATS
actions.summary: Runs `MEMORY STATS`. Returns the detailed memory accounting (allocator stats, fragmentation, peak, overheads, replication backlog, client and AOF buffers). Read-only. Use to diagnose OOM warnings before resizing.
actions.description: Runs `MEMORY STATS`. Returns the detailed memory accounting (allocator stats, fragmentation, peak, overheads, replication backlog, client and AOF buffers). Read-only. Use to diagnose OOM warnings before resizing.

actions.title: MEMORY USAGE key
actions.summary: Show bytes used by a single key (estimate, including value and internal overhead). Use to find a known big-value culprit before a deletion conversation.
actions.description: Show bytes used by a single key (estimate, including value and internal overhead). Use to find a known big-value culprit before a deletion conversation.
side_effects: Reads metadata but not the value.

actions.title: OBJECT ENCODING key
actions.summary: Show the internal encoding of one value (ziplist, hashtable, intset, listpack, quicklist, embstr, raw, skiplist). Use to confirm a structure is in its compact form before tuning size limits.
actions.description: Show the internal encoding of one value (ziplist, hashtable, intset, listpack, quicklist, embstr, raw, skiplist). Use to confirm a structure is in its compact form before tuning size limits.

actions.title: OBJECT FREQ key
actions.summary: Show the LFU access-frequency counter for the key. Requires an LFU maxmemory-policy. Use to compare access patterns across a candidate eviction set.
actions.description: Show the LFU access-frequency counter for the key. Requires an LFU maxmemory-policy. Use to compare access patterns across a candidate eviction set.
side_effects: Read-only metadata; does not bump the frequency counter.

actions.title: OBJECT IDLETIME key
actions.summary: Show seconds since the key was last accessed. Requires an LRU maxmemory-policy. Use to identify cold keys for manual eviction.
actions.description: Show seconds since the key was last accessed. Requires an LRU maxmemory-policy. Use to identify cold keys for manual eviction.
side_effects: Read-only metadata; does not bump LRU age.

actions.title: OBJECT REFCOUNT key
actions.summary: Show the reference count for a value. Shared integers report INT_MAX; every other value reports 1. Use to confirm the shared-integer optimization is hitting (or not).
actions.description: Show the reference count for a value. Shared integers report INT_MAX; every other value reports 1. Use to confirm the shared-integer optimization is hitting (or not).

actions.title: PUBSUB CHANNELS
actions.summary: List active pub/sub channels matching the optional pattern. Useful to see which event streams a service is producing.
actions.description: List active pub/sub channels matching the optional pattern. Useful to see which event streams a service is producing.

actions.title: PUBSUB NUMSUB
actions.summary: Count subscribers on a channel. Useful before tearing down a producer.
actions.description: Count subscribers on a channel. Useful before tearing down a producer.

actions.title: RANDOMKEY
actions.summary: Returns a random key from the current database (or nil if empty). Useful for sampling key shapes.
actions.description: Returns a random key from the current database (or nil if empty). Useful for sampling key shapes.

actions.title: REPLICAOF
actions.summary: Reconfigures replication. REPLICAOF NO ONE promotes this node to master (used in unmanaged failover). REPLICAOF host port reassigns this node as a replica of host:port and triggers a full sync — the local dataset is REPLACED. Misuse can wipe live data.
actions.description: Reconfigures replication. REPLICAOF NO ONE promotes this node to master (used in unmanaged failover). REPLICAOF host port reassigns this node as a replica of host:port and triggers a full sync — the local dataset is REPLACED. Misuse can wipe live data.
side_effects: If becoming a replica: local dataset is replaced by the new master's snapshot.
If promoted to master: existing replicas of the old master may still follow it.

args.name: host
args.description: New master host, or the literal "NO" to demote.

args.name: port
args.description: New master port (1-65535), or the literal "ONE" if host is "NO".

actions.title: ROLE
actions.summary: Reports whether this Redis is master, replica, or sentinel. Master shows replication offset and connected replicas; replica shows master addr + sync state. Use before any write-path troubleshooting.
actions.description: Reports whether this Redis is master, replica, or sentinel. Master shows replication offset and connected replicas; replica shows master addr + sync state. Use before any write-path troubleshooting.

actions.title: SCAN cursor MATCH COUNT
actions.summary: Show one SCAN page. Returns next-cursor + up to COUNT keys matching MATCH. Safe for prod — never blocks. Use to enumerate a hot namespace without KEYS.
actions.description: Show one SCAN page. Returns next-cursor + up to COUNT keys matching MATCH. Safe for prod — never blocks. Use to enumerate a hot namespace without KEYS.
side_effects: Read-only metadata; values are not fetched.

actions.title: SCRIPT FLUSH
actions.summary: Drop all cached EVAL scripts (server-side Lua). Clients using EVAL repopulate naturally; clients using EVALSHA hit NOSCRIPT until they re-cache.
actions.description: Drop all cached EVAL scripts (server-side Lua). Clients using EVAL repopulate naturally; clients using EVALSHA hit NOSCRIPT until they re-cache.
side_effects: Brief NOSCRIPT errors expected on EVALSHA callers.

actions.title: SENTINEL CKQUORUM
actions.summary: Checks whether enough Sentinels are reachable to BOTH reach the failover quorum AND form the majority needed to authorize a failover for the named master. The canonical pre-failover health check — returns OK with the counts, or an error if a failover could not currently proceed. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
actions.description: Checks whether enough Sentinels are reachable to BOTH reach the failover quorum AND form the majority needed to authorize a failover for the named master. The canonical pre-failover health check — returns OK with the counts, or an error if a failover could not currently proceed. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL CKQUORUM command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).
No Sentinel state changed, no failover triggered.

args.name: master_name
args.description: Configured master name to check quorum for (e.g. "mymaster").

examples.title: Can mymaster be failed over right now?

actions.title: SENTINEL FAILOVER
actions.summary: Forces a failover for the named master as if it were unreachable, WITHOUT asking the other Sentinels for agreement (a new configuration is still published so the peers update). Promotes a replica to master and reconfigures the rest to follow it. This is a manual, operator-initiated failover — use for a planned switchover or to recover when automatic failover is stuck. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379.
actions.description: Forces a failover for the named master as if it were unreachable, WITHOUT asking the other Sentinels for agreement (a new configuration is still published so the peers update). Promotes a replica to master and reconfigures the rest to follow it. This is a manual, operator-initiated failover — use for a planned switchover or to recover when automatic failover is stuck. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379.
side_effects: The named master is failed over; a replica is promoted to master.
Remaining replicas are reconfigured to replicate from the new master.
The new topology is published to all Sentinels; clients are redirected.
Brief write unavailability during the promotion.

args.name: master_name
args.description: Configured master name to fail over (e.g. "mymaster").

actions.title: SENTINEL GET-MASTER-ADDR-BY-NAME
actions.summary: Returns the ip and port Sentinel currently believes is the master for the named set. During or just after a failover this returns the PROMOTED replica's address — so it is the authoritative answer to "who is the master right now?" Compare against ROLE on each node to detect split-brain. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the ip and port Sentinel currently believes is the master for the named set. During or just after a failover this returns the PROMOTED replica's address — so it is the authoritative answer to "who is the master right now?" Compare against ROLE on each node to detect split-brain. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL GET-MASTER-ADDR-BY-NAME command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).

actions.title: Sentinel INFO
actions.summary: Runs INFO against the Sentinel process itself (port 26379) and returns the raw text, including the `sentinel` section: sentinel_masters, sentinel_running_scripts, sentinel_tilt, and a master0..N line per monitored master (name, status, address, slaves, sentinels). Use to read the Sentinel's own health — e.g. whether it is in TILT mode. Read-only.
actions.description: Runs INFO against the Sentinel process itself (port 26379) and returns the raw text, including the `sentinel` section: sentinel_masters, sentinel_running_scripts, sentinel_tilt, and a master0..N line per monitored master (name, status, address, slaves, sentinels). Use to read the Sentinel's own health — e.g. whether it is in TILT mode. Read-only.
side_effects: One INFO command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).
No keys read or written; no Sentinel state changed.

actions.title: SENTINEL IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR
actions.summary: Asks this Sentinel whether the master at ip:port is subjectively down from its point of view, and returns the Sentinel's current leader vote. Primarily an internal failover-consensus command, but useful read-only forensics when investigating WHY an objective-down (ODOWN) or failover did or did not fire. Pass runid "*" to only query the down-state without requesting a vote. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
actions.description: Asks this Sentinel whether the master at ip:port is subjectively down from its point of view, and returns the Sentinel's current leader vote. Primarily an internal failover-consensus command, but useful read-only forensics when investigating WHY an objective-down (ODOWN) or failover did or did not fire. Pass runid "*" to only query the down-state without requesting a vote. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).
With runid "*" it queries state only; it does not change the monitored topology.

args.name: ip
args.description: Master IPv4/IPv6 address as Sentinel knows it.

args.name: current_epoch
args.description: Current epoch to vote in; 0 to query state without a vote.

args.name: runid
args.description: Candidate Sentinel run id to vote for, or "*" to query down-state only.

examples.title: Is the master at 10.0.0.5:6379 down from this Sentinel's view?

actions.title: SENTINEL MASTER
actions.summary: Show the full state of one monitored master by its configured name (e.g. "mymaster") — ip:port, flags, quorum, num-slaves, num-other-sentinels, down-after-milliseconds, last-ping. Use to confirm a specific master's view and whether the configured quorum is met. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
actions.description: Show the full state of one monitored master by its configured name (e.g. "mymaster") — ip:port, flags, quorum, num-slaves, num-other-sentinels, down-after-milliseconds, last-ping. Use to confirm a specific master's view and whether the configured quorum is met. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL MASTER command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).

actions.title: SENTINEL MASTERS
actions.summary: Lists every master this Sentinel monitors with full state — name, ip:port, quorum, flags (master/o_down/s_down), num-slaves, num-other-sentinels. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. The first stop when mapping a replicas+Sentinel topology. Read-only.
actions.description: Lists every master this Sentinel monitors with full state — name, ip:port, quorum, flags (master/o_down/s_down), num-slaves, num-other-sentinels. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. The first stop when mapping a replicas+Sentinel topology. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL MASTERS command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).
No Sentinel state changed, no failover triggered.

actions.title: SENTINEL REPLICAS
actions.summary: Lists the replicas Sentinel has discovered for one master, each with ip:port, flags (slave/s_down/disconnected), master-link-status, slave-repl-offset, and slave-priority. Use to see which replicas are up, their replication offset (lag vs the master), and which are failover-eligible. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only. (SENTINEL REPLICAS, the modern name for the deprecated SLAVES, requires Redis >= 5.0.)
actions.description: Lists the replicas Sentinel has discovered for one master, each with ip:port, flags (slave/s_down/disconnected), master-link-status, slave-repl-offset, and slave-priority. Use to see which replicas are up, their replication offset (lag vs the master), and which are failover-eligible. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only. (SENTINEL REPLICAS, the modern name for the deprecated SLAVES, requires Redis >= 5.0.)
side_effects: One SENTINEL REPLICAS command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).

args.name: master_name
args.description: Configured master name whose replicas to list (e.g. "mymaster").

actions.title: SENTINEL RESET
actions.summary: Resets every monitored master whose name matches the glob pattern. The reset CLEARS any state for the master — including a failover in progress — and DROPS every discovered replica and peer Sentinel, so they are re-learned over the next ~10s from the current master's INFO. Use to clean up stale/removed replicas after a topology change (run on every Sentinel). A too-broad pattern (e.g. "*") resets all masters at once. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379.
actions.description: Resets every monitored master whose name matches the glob pattern. The reset CLEARS any state for the master — including a failover in progress — and DROPS every discovered replica and peer Sentinel, so they are re-learned over the next ~10s from the current master's INFO. Use to clean up stale/removed replicas after a topology change (run on every Sentinel). A too-broad pattern (e.g. "*") resets all masters at once. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379.
side_effects: All matching masters' discovered replicas and peer Sentinels are dropped and re-learned.
Any in-progress failover state for matching masters is cleared.
Must be run on every Sentinel to fully evict a removed replica.

args.name: pattern
args.description: Glob over master names; use an exact name to scope to one master.

examples.title: Reset one master to drop stale replicas

actions.title: SENTINEL SENTINELS
actions.summary: Lists the OTHER Sentinel instances this Sentinel knows about for a given master — each with ip:port, runid, flags, and last-ok-ping. Use to confirm the Sentinel set is fully meshed and agrees on membership when diagnosing why a failover quorum is not reached. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
actions.description: Lists the OTHER Sentinel instances this Sentinel knows about for a given master — each with ip:port, runid, flags, and last-ok-ping. Use to confirm the Sentinel set is fully meshed and agrees on membership when diagnosing why a failover quorum is not reached. Connects to the Sentinel on port 26379. Read-only.
side_effects: One SENTINEL SENTINELS command on the local Sentinel (port 26379).

args.name: master_name
args.description: Configured master name whose peer Sentinels to list (e.g. "mymaster").

actions.title: SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
actions.summary: Stop the Redis process without writing an RDB. Any data not already persisted is LOST. Used only for cache-only nodes or during recovery from a corrupted state. Process exit means the host's systemd/supervisor will then restart it (or not).
actions.description: Stop the Redis process without writing an RDB. Any data not already persisted is LOST. Used only for cache-only nodes or during recovery from a corrupted state. Process exit means the host's systemd/supervisor will then restart it (or not).
side_effects: In-memory data not yet persisted is lost.
Replicas detect disconnect and stop receiving updates.
Supervisor restart depends on host config.

actions.title: SLOWLOG GET
actions.summary: Returns the last N slowlog entries (commands that exceeded the slowlog threshold). Each entry shows id, timestamp, duration (µs), command + args, client addr, and client name. Read-only.
actions.description: Returns the last N slowlog entries (commands that exceeded the slowlog threshold). Each entry shows id, timestamp, duration (µs), command + args, client addr, and client name. Read-only.

args.name: limit
args.description: How many slowlog entries to return.

actions.title: SWAPDB
actions.summary: Atomically swap the contents of two logical databases. Clients connected to db i now see db j and vice versa. Used for blue/green cache rebuilds. Wrong indices flip live traffic to a stale dataset.
actions.description: Atomically swap the contents of two logical databases. Clients connected to db i now see db j and vice versa. Used for blue/green cache rebuilds. Wrong indices flip live traffic to a stale dataset.
side_effects: Two databases exchange visible contents atomically.
Connected clients immediately see the swapped dataset.

actions.title: TTL key
actions.summary: Show seconds until the key expires. -2 means missing, -1 means no expiry set. Use before any expiry-related troubleshooting.
actions.description: Show seconds until the key expires. -2 means missing, -1 means no expiry set. Use before any expiry-related troubleshooting.

actions.title: TYPE key
actions.summary: Show the datatype of one key (string, list, set, hash, zset, stream, none).
actions.description: Show the datatype of one key (string, list, set, hash, zset, stream, none).

actions.title: XINFO STREAM
actions.summary: Show one stream's length, last-generated-id, groups, first/last entry. Use to confirm producers are still appending.
actions.description: Show one stream's length, last-generated-id, groups, first/last entry. Use to confirm producers are still appending.

actions.title: XLEN
actions.summary: Count entries in a stream.
actions.description: Count entries in a stream.

packs.name: RKE2 cluster (host-level)
packs.description: RKE2-specific host introspection that kubectl can't give you — embedded-etcd live health (etcdctl endpoint health/status) and snapshot history, node certificate expiry, and the RKE2-bundled containerd via crictl (containers, pods, images). Runs on an RKE2 server node. Generic Kubernetes API reads are in the kubernetes pack; rke2-server / rke2-agent unit status and logs are covered by the linux-core / systemd-deep packs (systemctl status rke2-server, journalctl -u rke2-server).

actions.title: Check RKE2 certificate expiry (rke2 certificate check)
actions.summary: Show per-certificate subject, status, and expiry date for this node's RKE2 certs. RKE2 leaf certs default to 365 days and auto-renew on restart within 90 days of expiry; this surfaces a node whose certs are about to lapse. Read-only.
actions.description: Show per-certificate subject, status, and expiry date for this node's RKE2 certs. RKE2 leaf certs default to 365 days and auto-renew on restart within 90 days of expiry; this surfaces a node whose certs are about to lapse. Read-only.

actions.title: List images on this node (crictl images)
actions.summary: List the container images present in this node's RKE2 containerd — repository, tag, image id, and size. Use to confirm an image is actually pulled on a node (ImagePullBackOff triage) or to see disk pressure from images. Read-only.
actions.description: List the container images present in this node's RKE2 containerd — repository, tag, image id, and size. Use to confirm an image is actually pulled on a node (ImagePullBackOff triage) or to see disk pressure from images. Read-only.
side_effects: One crictl images call against the node container runtime.

actions.title: List pod sandboxes on this node (crictl pods)
actions.summary: List pod sandboxes on this node's RKE2 containerd — pod id, name, namespace, state, age. The CRI view of which pods the node actually has, independent of the API server. Read-only.
actions.description: List pod sandboxes on this node's RKE2 containerd — pod id, name, namespace, state, age. The CRI view of which pods the node actually has, independent of the API server. Read-only.
side_effects: One crictl pods call against the node container runtime.

actions.title: List containers on this node (crictl ps -a)
actions.summary: List all containers (running and stopped) on this node's RKE2 containerd — container id, image, state, name, attempt count, and pod. The CRI-level truth when kubelet/kubectl disagree about what's running on a node, and where you see crash/restart loops. Read-only.
actions.description: List all containers (running and stopped) on this node's RKE2 containerd — container id, image, state, name, attempt count, and pod. The CRI-level truth when kubelet/kubectl disagree about what's running on a node, and where you see crash/restart loops. Read-only.
side_effects: One crictl ps call against the node container runtime.

actions.title: Check embedded etcd health (etcdctl endpoint health + status)
actions.summary: Check the embedded etcd's live health and status on this RKE2 server node — `etcdctl endpoint health` (is the member committing proposals?) followed by `endpoint status` (etcd version, DB size, leader, raft term/index).
actions.description: Check the embedded etcd's live health and status on this RKE2 server node — `etcdctl endpoint health` (is the member committing proposals?) followed by `endpoint status` (etcd version, DB size, leader, raft term/index). The direct read of etcd that snapshot-list and /readyz only approximate. RKE2 ships no etcdctl on the host PATH, so this runs the etcdctl already inside the running etcd static-pod container (found via crictl), authenticating to the local client endpoint (https://127.0.0.1:2379) with RKE2's own etcd server CA and the etcd server-client cert. Read-only; exits non-zero if etcd is unhealthy.
side_effects: One crictl exec into the running etcd container, running two etcdctl reads.
Read-only; no etcd writes, no snapshots, no member changes.

argv:
set -e
CR=/var/lib/rancher/rke2/bin/crictl
CID=$("$CR" ps --name etcd --state Running -q | head -n1)
[ -n "$CID" ] || { echo "no running etcd container on this node — is this an RKE2 server (etcd) node?" >&2; exit 1; }
TLS=/var/lib/rancher/rke2/server/tls/etcd
echo "=== endpoint health ==="
"$CR" exec "$CID" etcdctl \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert="$TLS"/server-ca.crt \
  --cert="$TLS"/server-client.crt \
  --key="$TLS"/server-client.key \
  --write-out=table endpoint health
echo "=== endpoint status ==="
"$CR" exec "$CID" etcdctl \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert="$TLS"/server-ca.crt \
  --cert="$TLS"/server-client.crt \
  --key="$TLS"/server-client.key \
  --write-out=table endpoint status

actions.title: List etcd snapshots (rke2 etcd-snapshot ls)
actions.summary: List the embedded-etcd snapshots RKE2 has taken — name, location (local / S3), size, and creation time. Confirms backups are current; an empty or stale list is an etcd-recovery risk. Read-only.
actions.description: List the embedded-etcd snapshots RKE2 has taken — name, location (local / S3), size, and creation time. Confirms backups are current; an empty or stale list is an etcd-recovery risk. Read-only.

packs.name: Arbitrary shell (staging break-glass)
packs.description: STAGING-ONLY BREAK-GLASS. Runs an arbitrary operator-supplied shell script on the runner host via `/bin/sh -c`. This is the one capability emisar is built to avoid: it bypasses the declared-action model entirely — whatever the script says, runs, as the runner's user. It exists so an agent can verify a fix interactively on a staging host before that fix is encoded as a proper declared action or runbook. DO NOT install this pack on production runners and DO NOT enable it in production. Its single action is critical-risk, so the default policy denies it until an operator deliberately opts in; every run is fully audited.

actions.title: Run a shell script (/bin/sh -c)
actions.summary: STAGING BREAK-GLASS — run an arbitrary shell script on the runner host via `/bin/sh -c`.
actions.description: STAGING BREAK-GLASS — run an arbitrary shell script on the runner host via `/bin/sh -c`. Bypasses the declared-action model: whatever you pass runs verbatim, as the runner's user, with no per-argument schema and no allow/deny bounds beyond the timeout and output caps. Use this ONLY on a staging host to verify a fix before encoding it as a proper declared action or runbook — never as a substitute for one, and never in production. Keep scripts short and reviewable; the full text is recorded in the audit log.
side_effects: Executes arbitrary shell on the runner host as the runner's service user.
Can read, change, or destroy anything that user can reach — no schema, no allow/deny bounds.
Full script text is journaled locally and in the cloud audit log.

args.name: script
args.description: Shell script passed to `/bin/sh -c` as a single program. Prefer a few reviewable lines — this verifies a fix, it is not a deploy tool.

examples.title: Verify a service recovered after a fix

packs.name: Showcase pack
packs.description: Synthetic pack that demonstrates every action-schema feature in one place: all arg types, every validation, both parsers, both kinds, opts envelope bounds, and per-action redaction rules. Not a production pack — use it as a reference when authoring real ones.

actions.title: One arg of every type
actions.summary: Show a reference action: one argument of every supported type with a representative validation. Look here when you need to remember the YAML shape for `string_array`, `integer_array`, `duration`, etc.
actions.description: Show a reference action: one argument of every supported type with a representative validation. Look here when you need to remember the YAML shape for `string_array`, `integer_array`, `duration`, etc.

args.name: note
args.description: Free-form note attached to the run.

args.name: port
args.description: Listen port, restricted to a known set.

args.name: verbose
args.description: Toggle verbose output. Boolean args have no validation block.

args.name: tags
args.description: Free-form tag list. Use {{ args.tags }} in argv to expand.

examples.title: Tags expand into multiple argv elements

actions.title: Parse stdout as JSON, with action-local redaction
actions.summary: Demonstrates `parser: json` plus an extra regex redaction rule scoped to this action. The rule sits *in front of* the global rules — useful when an action emits a known secret shape that the global rules don't cover.
actions.description: Demonstrates `parser: json` plus an extra regex redaction rule scoped to this action. The rule sits *in front of* the global rules — useful when an action emits a known secret shape that the global rules don't cover.
side_effects: Calls /bin/echo with a synthetic JSON payload.

args.name: name
args.description: Name to include in the synthetic payload.

actions.title: Demonstrate opts.timeout and opts.max_stdout_bytes clamping
actions.summary: Sleeps briefly and emits a fixed payload. The interesting bit is the execution/output envelope: cloud-supplied opts.timeout is clamped to [2s, 30s] regardless of what the LLM asked for, and stdout caps are bounded between 1 KiB and 64 KiB.
actions.description: Sleeps briefly and emits a fixed payload. The interesting bit is the execution/output envelope: cloud-supplied opts.timeout is clamped to [2s, 30s] regardless of what the LLM asked for, and stdout caps are bounded between 1 KiB and 64 KiB.
side_effects: Sleeps then emits a small payload.

args.name: payload
args.description: Free-form payload string echoed at end.

actions.title: Read-only file inspection with path allowlist
actions.summary: Show a reference action for path-typed arguments. Demonstrates allowed prefixes plus an explicit deny list. The combined effect: the caller may inspect anything under /var/log or /tmp, except /var/log/secure and anything under /tmp/private.
actions.description: Show a reference action for path-typed arguments. Demonstrates allowed prefixes plus an explicit deny list. The combined effect: the caller may inspect anything under /var/log or /tmp, except /var/log/secure and anything under /tmp/private.

args.name: file
args.description: A path under /var/log or /tmp, excluding the denied set.

args.name: extras
args.description: Additional paths to inspect. Every element is validated the same way as `file`: the runner applies the path allow/deny rules and max_length to each element (not just the whole-array validators), so the same /var/log,/tmp containment holds for extras.

examples.title: Inspect a tmp file with extras

actions.title: Run a packaged shell script
actions.summary: Calls a packaged shell script. The interpreter is explicit (/bin/bash), the script path is resolved relative to the pack root, and the script's SHA-256 is journaled with every invocation so tampering is detectable after the fact.
actions.description: Calls a packaged shell script. The interpreter is explicit (/bin/bash), the script path is resolved relative to the pack root, and the script's SHA-256 is journaled with every invocation so tampering is detectable after the fact.

args.name: message
args.description: A string passed to the script as --message.

args.name: repeat
args.description: Number of times to echo the message.

packs.name: SNMP (net-snmp)
packs.description: Read a network device over SNMP with the net-snmp CLI — system group, interface table, BGP peers (BGP4-MIB) and OSPF neighbors / general group (OSPF-MIB), plus a generic single-OID get and a bounded subtree walk. The routing reads exist to give operators visibility into BGP / OSPF state on devices this control plane can't reach by a native API — e.g. a pfSense + FRR edge firewall, where the REST API exposes no routing-protocol state. All read-only.

actions.title: snmpwalk BGP4-MIB bgpPeerTable (1.3.6.1.2.1.15.3)
actions.summary: List BGP peers from BGP4-MIB bgpPeerTable — per-peer bgpPeerState (.2: 1=idle 2=connect 3=active 4=opensent 5=openconfirm 6=established), bgpPeerAdminStatus (.3: 1=stop 2=start), bgpPeerRemoteAddr (.7), bgpPeerRemoteAs (.9), in/out updates (.10/.11), and bgpPeerFsmEstablishedTime (.16). The right read for "is the BGP session up?" on a router or firewall (e.g. pfSense + FRR) that this control plane can't reach by a native API. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.15.3.
actions.description: List BGP peers from BGP4-MIB bgpPeerTable — per-peer bgpPeerState (.2: 1=idle 2=connect 3=active 4=opensent 5=openconfirm 6=established), bgpPeerAdminStatus (.3: 1=stop 2=start), bgpPeerRemoteAddr (.7), bgpPeerRemoteAs (.9), in/out updates (.10/.11), and bgpPeerFsmEstablishedTime (.16). The right read for "is the BGP session up?" on a router or firewall (e.g. pfSense + FRR) that this control plane can't reach by a native API. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.15.3.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the BGP peer table.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

examples.title: BGP peers on the edge firewall

actions.title: snmpget a single OID
actions.summary: Get a single SNMP object by OID — one snmpget. Use for a specific scalar the curated reads don't cover (e.g. a vendor MIB value). Give a numeric OID like 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0, or a symbolic one (SNMPv2-MIB::sysName.0) if the device's MIBs are installed on the runner.
actions.description: Get a single SNMP object by OID — one snmpget. Use for a specific scalar the curated reads don't cover (e.g. a vendor MIB value). Give a numeric OID like 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0, or a symbolic one (SNMPv2-MIB::sysName.0) if the device's MIBs are installed on the runner.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP GET of a single object.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

args.name: oid
args.description: The object OID — numeric (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0) or symbolic (sysName.0).

actions.title: snmpwalk IF-MIB ifTable (1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2)
actions.summary: List a device's interfaces from IF-MIB ifTable — per-interface ifDescr (.2), ifType (.3), ifSpeed (.5), ifAdminStatus (.7: 1=up 2=down), ifOperStatus (.8: 1=up 2=down), and in/out octets (.10/.16) and errors (.14/.20). Use to spot a down or errored link. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.
actions.description: List a device's interfaces from IF-MIB ifTable — per-interface ifDescr (.2), ifType (.3), ifSpeed (.5), ifAdminStatus (.7: 1=up 2=down), ifOperStatus (.8: 1=up 2=down), and in/out octets (.10/.16) and errors (.14/.20). Use to spot a down or errored link. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the interface table.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

actions.title: snmpwalk OSPF-MIB ospfGeneralGroup (1.3.6.1.2.1.14.1)
actions.summary: Show the OSPF general group from OSPF-MIB — ospfRouterId (.1), ospfAdminStat (.2: 1=enabled 2=disabled), ospfVersionNumber (.3), and the area-border / AS-border router flags (.4/.5). Use to confirm OSPF is enabled and read the router id. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.14.1.
actions.description: Show the OSPF general group from OSPF-MIB — ospfRouterId (.1), ospfAdminStat (.2: 1=enabled 2=disabled), ospfVersionNumber (.3), and the area-border / AS-border router flags (.4/.5). Use to confirm OSPF is enabled and read the router id. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.14.1.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the OSPF general group.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

actions.title: snmpwalk OSPF-MIB ospfNbrTable (1.3.6.1.2.1.14.10)
actions.summary: List OSPF neighbors from OSPF-MIB ospfNbrTable — per-neighbor ospfNbrIpAddr (.1), ospfNbrRtrId (.3), and ospfNbrState (.6: 1=down 2=attempt 3=init 4=twoWay 5=exchangeStart 6=exchange 7=loading 8=full). A healthy adjacency is 8=full (or 4=twoWay on a DR-other). Use to confirm OSPFv2 adjacencies. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.14.10. (OSPFv3 lives in OSPFV3-MIB — use snmp.walk.)
actions.description: List OSPF neighbors from OSPF-MIB ospfNbrTable — per-neighbor ospfNbrIpAddr (.1), ospfNbrRtrId (.3), and ospfNbrState (.6: 1=down 2=attempt 3=init 4=twoWay 5=exchangeStart 6=exchange 7=loading 8=full). A healthy adjacency is 8=full (or 4=twoWay on a DR-other). Use to confirm OSPFv2 adjacencies. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.14.10. (OSPFv3 lives in OSPFV3-MIB — use snmp.walk.)
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the OSPF neighbor table.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

actions.title: snmpwalk system group (1.3.6.1.2.1.1)
actions.summary: Show the SNMP system group of a device — sysDescr (model/OS), sysObjectID, sysUpTime, sysContact, sysName, sysLocation. The first stop to confirm SNMP works and identify what the device is. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.
actions.description: Show the SNMP system group of a device — sysDescr (model/OS), sysObjectID, sysUpTime, sysContact, sysName, sysLocation. The first stop to confirm SNMP works and identify what the device is. Walks 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the system group.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

actions.title: snmpwalk an OID subtree
actions.summary: Walk an SNMP subtree by root OID — one snmpbulkwalk (requires SNMP v2c or v3). Use to read a table or MIB the curated reads don't cover (e.g. OSPFV3-MIB 1.3.6.1.2.1.191, or a vendor table). Output is capped, so a very large subtree is truncated; narrow the root OID. Give a numeric OID, or a symbolic one if the device's MIBs are installed on the runner.
actions.description: Walk an SNMP subtree by root OID — one snmpbulkwalk (requires SNMP v2c or v3). Use to read a table or MIB the curated reads don't cover (e.g. OSPFV3-MIB 1.3.6.1.2.1.191, or a vendor table). Output is capped, so a very large subtree is truncated; narrow the root OID. Give a numeric OID, or a symbolic one if the device's MIBs are installed on the runner.
side_effects: One read-only SNMP walk of the given subtree.

args.name: host
args.description: Target device hostname or IP (optionally host:port).

args.name: oid
args.description: Root OID of the subtree — numeric (1.3.6.1.2.1.4) or symbolic (ipAddrTable).

packs.name: Local SSL/TLS cert inspection
packs.description: Inspect TLS certificates and keys on the local filesystem — find PEMs under a path, dump x509 details, check chain, verify private-key match, inspect PKCS#12. Read-only. Pair with `network-tls` for the remote-side view.

actions.title: openssl x509 -enddate -subject
actions.summary: Show cert expiry date + subject. Quick way to answer "is this about to expire?".
actions.description: Show cert expiry date + subject. Quick way to answer "is this about to expire?".

actions.title: openssl x509 -fingerprint -sha256
actions.summary: Show the SHA-256 fingerprint of the cert. Use for pinning checks.
actions.description: Show the SHA-256 fingerprint of the cert. Use for pinning checks.

actions.title: openssl x509 -text -noout
actions.summary: Dump a human-readable x509 certificate.
actions.description: Dump a human-readable x509 certificate.

actions.title: find *.pem *.crt *.cer under <path>
actions.summary: Lists certificate-like files under one path.
actions.description: Lists certificate-like files under one path.

actions.title: openssl rsa -modulus | sha256
actions.summary: Show the hash of the RSA private key's modulus. Compare to the modulus hash of a cert (via `openssl x509 -modulus`) to confirm key + cert match. NEVER prints the key itself.
actions.description: Show the hash of the RSA private key's modulus. Compare to the modulus hash of a cert (via `openssl x509 -modulus`) to confirm key + cert match. NEVER prints the key itself.
side_effects: Read-only — only the modulus hash is emitted; the key material is not returned.

actions.title: openssl pkcs12 -nokeys -info
actions.summary: Inspect a PKCS#12 bundle's bag contents — cert subjects, key types — without exporting the private key. Requires the bundle's password via PK12_PASSWORD env var on the runner host (or empty for password-less bundles).
